_meta.purpose: WORKING artifact — raw statewide extraction of CS-related associate degrees at all 115 CA community colleges (spec docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-17-as-degree-data-design.md). Pre-schema-finalization; transformed to the as_degree kind at import time after G2 approval.
_meta.method: in-session multi-agent: statewide survey (degree inventory) -> targeted extraction (requirements) per college, anchored to each college's own catalog. 14-college stratified pilot first.
_meta.coverage: 115 colleges; 114 with >=1 CS-related associate degree; Woodland CC (id 147) = verified none_found (no computing associate degree).
_meta.quality: mean extraction_confidence 0.92; cleanup list in docs/as-degree-template-review.md

survey.summary: Per the official Evergreen Valley College 2024-2026 catalog, the Computer Science department offers only the Computer Science - Associate in Science for Transfer (AS-T), a state-standardized transfer ADT. There is NO local (non-transfer) A.S. or A.A. titled "Computer Science." The Computer and Information Technology department offers only a "Computer Programming - Certificate of Achievement," which is a certificate (not a degree) and is therefore excluded from the degree list. No other local computing/CS/CIS/programming associate degrees were found in the catalog's degrees-and-certificates listings. Result: local_cs_as_exists=false, ast_cs_exists=true, local_computing_degrees=none.

Per LACC's official Areas of Study page (Computer Science), the CSISA department offers three associate degrees: (1) Computer Science AS-T — "Associate in Science in Computer Science for Transfer" (the transfer ADT); (2) Computer Science AA — a LOCAL Associate of Arts; and (3) Computer Information Systems (CIS) AA — a LOCAL Associate of Arts. There is NO local A.S. titled "Computer Science" — the local CS degree is an A.A., and the only "Computer Science" A.S. is the transfer AS-T. Certificates of Achievement (excluded from degree lists) include Applications Software, C++ Programming, Cloud Computing, Data Science, Full Stack Web Application Developer, and Java Programming. Data confirmed authoritatively via browser (lacc.edu was 403-blocked to WebFetch but reachable through the browser pane).

College of Marin (2024-2025 catalog) offers two LOCAL computing associate degrees, both A.S., and NO Computer Science transfer ADT. (1) LOCAL A.S. in Computer Science exists — the catalog's Computer Science program section explicitly describes "A.S. in Computer Science" (major req. 27-32 units: core programming C++/Java/Python, advanced programming, calculus, CS electives). (2) NO AS-T in Computer Science: the catalog's complete "Associate Degrees for Transfer" list (pp. 59-60) enumerates every ADT offered (Admin of Justice, Anthropology, Business Administration 2.0, Math, Physics, etc.) and includes NEITHER Computer Science NOR Computer Information Systems. This is corroborated by the master "Table of A.S./A.A. Degrees" where the Computer Science row is marked only in the A.S. Degree and Transfer columns (verified by exact character offset: X at col-53=A.S., X at col-113=Transfer, and crucially NO X at col-81=A.S.-T). The "Transfer" column merely flags the local A.S. as transfer-articulated, not an ADT. (3) Computer Information Systems also offers a LOCAL A.S. ("A.S. in Computer Information Systems," Certificate of Achievement also awarded) — again no AS-T. Certificates (CIS Certificate of Achievement, Skills Certificates such as Microsoft Office Specialist / Database Specialist) are excluded from the degree list per instructions.

College of San Mateo (2025-2026 catalog) offers three LOCAL computing-related Associate in Science degrees: (1) Computer Science Applications and Development (A.S.), (2) Computer and Information Science (A.S.), and (3) Web and Mobile Application Development (A.S.). None of these local degrees is essentially titled simply "Computer Science" (the closest, "Computer Science Applications and Development," is a distinct applied/programming degree), so local_cs_as_exists = false. The TRANSFER degree DOES exist: the college's official "Associate Degrees for Transfer" list (dated June 2026) explicitly lists "Computer Science AS-T," available at CSM beginning 2026-2027, so ast_cs_exists = true. Numerous computing certificates also exist (Certificate of Achievement in Computer Science Applications and Development and Web and Mobile Application Development, plus Certificates of Specialization in Python, C++, Java, Data Science/Big Data, Database Programming, Enterprise Cybersecurity, IoT, Machine Learning/AI, Web Programming, etc.) but certificates are excluded per the definitions as they are not degrees.

College of the Sequoias (2025-2026 official catalog, catalog.cos.edu) offers the transfer degree "Associate in Science in Computer Science for Transfer (AS-T)" (listed under Math & Engineering), so ast_cs_exists = true. There is NO local (non-transfer) A.S. titled "Computer Science" — the only degree bearing the "Computer Science" name is the AS-T, so local_cs_as_exists = false. Local (non-transfer) computing associate degrees: (1) Associate of Science in Computer Programming & Information Systems (A.S., Business division) — programming, web development, databases; (2) Associate of Science in Information Communication Technology (A.S., Industry & Technology division) — IT/networking (included as computing-related, though more IT/networking-oriented). Excluded as non-degrees: Certificates of Achievement in Computer Applications, Computer Programming & Information Systems, Computer & Network Support, and CISCO CCNA Academy. All confirmed directly against the fully-accessible official catalog.

Cerro Coso Community College does NOT offer a local "Computer Science" A.S. degree, nor a "Computer Science for Transfer" AS-T/ADT. The college's own program index lists its complete set of ~11-12 Associate in Science degrees, and none is titled Computer Science; the closest computing/CIS A.S. degrees are Information Technology, Cyber Security Technology, Computer Applications Specialist, and Web Professional (all local A.S., non-transfer). The full Associate Degrees for Transfer list (15 ADTs) contains no Computer Science for Transfer — the only STEM ADT is Mathematics AS-T. Cerro Coso also offers IT-related Certificates of Achievement (e.g., Information Technology Certificate), which are excluded here as they are not degrees. Data drawn from the college's official cerrocoso.edu program pages (AS index and ADT index); catalog PDF not separately opened but site listings are authoritative and internally consistent.

Columbia College (Sonora, CA; part of Yosemite CCD) offers NO degree titled "Computer Science" — local or transfer — and NO computing ADT. Verified against the current 2025-2026 catalog's official "Degrees & Certificates (Academic Awards)" page (eLumen), which lists these computing awards: (1) Computer Programming — A.S. (LOCAL); (2) Geographic Information Systems — A.S. (LOCAL); (3) Geographic Information Systems — Certificate of Achievement (COA, not a degree); and (4) three GIS micro-credentials (Emergency Response, Geospatial, UAV/Drone Mapping) as Skills Attainment Certificates (SAC, not degrees). All computing offerings sit in the "Computer Programming and Information Systems (COMP)" department. The catalog's complete AA-T/AS-T transfer-degree list (Anthropology, Biology, Business Admin 2.0, Communication Studies, ECE, Economics, English, Geology, History, Kinesiology, Mathematics, Music, Nutrition, Physics, Political Science, Psychology, Public Health, Social Work, Sociology, Studio Arts, etc.) contains NO Computer Science / Computer Programming / CIS entry, confirming ast_cs_exists=false. Therefore local_cs_as_exists=false (the college's local A.S. is "Computer Programming," not "Computer Science"). Note: the college marketing page (gocolumbia.edu/.../computer_programming_and_info_systems.html) inconsistently displayed an "AA-T" label, but that is a page-template artifact contradicted by the authoritative catalog award list. GIS A.S. is included in local_computing_degrees because it is a computing/information-systems degree housed in the COMP department (geospatial/information-systems, borderline but computing-adjacent). Data obtained via the eLumen catalog publish API (page rendered as an Angular SPA); no CS-titled degree exists.

Merritt College's official 2024-2025 catalog lists a LOCAL "Computer Science - A.S. Degree" (Program Control #37964, TOP 0707.00 Computer Software Development, state-approved 12/22/2021, effective 1/1/2022). It is a local CTE/transfer-hybrid A.S., NOT an AS-T/ADT. There is NO "Computer Science for Transfer" (AS-T) degree — Merritt's ADT list covers Administration of Justice, Anthropology, Business Administration 2.0, Early Childhood Education, Communication Studies 2.0, Economics, Mathematics, Nutrition and Dietetics, Psychology, and Sociology, none in computing/CS. Local computing A.S. degrees (all A.S., housed under the CS/CIS departments): Computer Science; Applications Security; Infrastructure Security; DevOps - Development and Operations Automation. Certificates (excluded from degree lists): Applications Security (Cert. of Achievement), Infrastructure Security (Cert. of Achievement), Computer Project Management (Cert. of Achievement), DevOps (Cert. of Achievement), and Computer Science and Information Systems Career Readiness (Cert. of Completion). Note: the merritt.edu department web pages did not surface the standalone Computer Science A.S. in scraping, but the authoritative catalog and the program outline report both confirm it as a current A.S. degree.

Santa Ana College (2025-2026 catalog) offers BOTH a local A.S. and the transfer AS-T in Computer Science. The credentials summary table under the "Computer Science" program of study shows AS=5, AS-T=1, CA=11, CP=4, and the Instructional Programs section confirms the individual titles. LOCAL A.S. degrees: (1) Computer Science: A.S. Degree (Program Code SAC.CMPR.AS, PCN 04197) — this is the local A.S. titled "Computer Science", distinct from the ADT; (2) Computer Information Systems: A.S. (SAC.CIS.AS); (3) Data Analytics: A.S. (SAC.CMPDA.AS); (4) Database Management Systems: A.S. (SAC.CMDMS.AS); (5) Machine Learning: A.S. (SAC.CMPML.AS). TRANSFER: Computer Science: A.S.-T Degree for Transfer (Program Code SAC.CMPR.AST, PCN 32396) — the state-standardized ADT, excluded from local_computing_degrees. Certificates (excluded as non-degrees) include Computer Science CA, CIS CA, Data Analytics CA, Backend Engineering CA, Artificial Intelligence CA, Fullstack Web Developer CA, Java/Python Programming CA, three IT operations CAs, plus Programming, Database, Enterprise Systems, and Web Site Development CPs. Authoritative source: SAC 2025-2026 catalog (pages 118-120) and the catalog's "Certificate and Associate Degrees Offered at SAC" credentials table.

Cuesta College (San Luis Obispo County) offers a LOCAL "Computer Science, Associate in Science" degree — the program map page is titled "COMPUTER SCIENCE Associate in Science Program," and the college's official "All Degrees and Certificates" page lists it as "Computer Science, Associate in Science" with no "for Transfer" designation. There is NO Computer Science AS-T/ADT (Associate in Science for Transfer): the all-degrees page (checked twice) shows no "for Transfer," "AS-T," or "AA-T" degree in Computer Science, CIS, or IT. Other local computing A.S. degrees: Computer and Networking Technology (A.S.) and Management Information Systems (A.S.). Related non-degree items (excluded from the degree list) include several Certificates of Specialization — Android Developer, iOS Developer, Internet Applications Developer, Computer Networking Support, Computer Support Specialist, Network Infrastructure Support — and a Cloud Computing Certificate of Achievement. Note: the college describes the local CS A.S. as designed to satisfy many CS transfer patterns, but it is not a state-standardized ADT.

Las Positas College offers a LOCAL Associate of Science (AS) in Computer Science, explicitly described in the catalog as a local degree (not a transfer degree) designed to prepare students to transfer into a CS baccalaureate program. There is NO Computer Science AS-T / ADT (Associate in Science for Transfer): both the catalog CS index and the Computer Studies CS department page confirm no AS-T is offered in Computer Science. The separate CIS department offers two local computing degrees: an AS in Computer Information Technologist and an AA in Computer Information Systems (an AA in Administrative Assistant was excluded as office-admin, not computing). Certificates (excluded from degree lists) include, in CS: Artificial Intelligence, Computer Programming, Computer Programming for the Web, Management Information Systems, and Red Hat Administration; and in CIS: Computer Applications Software, Cloud Computing, Data Analytics, Web Development, and others. Sources reached successfully (no browser needed); confidence limited only slightly by the possibility of an AS-T housed outside the CS pages, but multiple authoritative pages consistently confirm none exists.

Victor Valley College's current catalog (catalog.vvc.edu, Computer Information Science discipline) lists a Computer Science, AS-T transfer degree (ADT) plus one local associate degree: Computer Information Systems, A.S. There is NO local (non-transfer) associate degree titled "Computer Science" — the "Computer Science" title appears only as the AS-T transfer degree. Certificates of Achievement offered under CIS (MySQL Database Developer, Programming I, Unix Administrator, Web Authoring) are excluded as non-degrees. Therefore: ast_cs_exists=true (Computer Science AS-T), local_cs_as_exists=false, and the sole local computing degree is Computer Information Systems, A.S.

Barstow Community College offers a Computer Science AS-T (Associate of Science for Transfer / ADT), titled "Computer Science," 60 units, designed for CSU transfer — this is the transfer degree, so ast_cs_exists=true. There is NO local A.S. titled "Computer Science" (local_cs_as_exists=false). The college's one local (non-transfer) computing associate degree is the Associate of Science in Computer Business Information Systems (60 units, entry-level IT employment focus, with Cyber Security Specialist and Management Information Systems tracks). Two related credentials are certificates, not degrees, and are excluded from the degree list: Certificate of Achievement in Cyber Security Specialist (17 units) and Certificate of Achievement in Management Information Systems (16 units). Findings drawn from Barstow's own program/guided-pathways pages; not directly cross-checked against a downloaded 2024-2026 catalog PDF, hence confidence slightly below maximum.

Per LATTC's 2024-2025 General Catalog and official program pages, the computing-related associate degrees are both LOCAL A.S. degrees: (1) Computer Information Systems A.S. (prepares for programming/software/networking/web/database careers; uses CS-prefix courses like Intro to Computer Science, Programming in C++/Python/Java/JavaScript, but the degree is titled 'Computer Information Systems', not 'Computer Science'), and (2) Information Technology A.S. (formerly Microcomputer Technician). No LOCAL A.S. is titled 'Computer Science' (local_cs_as_exists=false). No Computer Science AS-T/ADT exists — LATTC's complete CSU ADT list covers only Admin Justice, Business Admin, Child & Adolescent Dev, Economics, English, Kinesiology, Math, Nutrition & Dietetics, Physics, Political Science, Psychology, and Sociology, with no CS/CIS transfer degree (ast_cs_exists=false); the CIS catalog sheet explicitly directs transfer-bound students to consult a counselor for a transfer curriculum. Certificates excluded per instructions: Computer Information Systems Certificate of Achievement and Information Technology Certificate of Achievement. Computer Applications & Office Technologies (CAOT) also exists but is office/clerical administration (Word/Excel/assistant tracks), not CS/CIS/programming, so it is excluded from the computing degrees list.

Contra Costa College offers a LOCAL Associate in Science in Computer Science (a ~20-unit major; the 2023-24 catalog program page explicitly describes it as a local two-year pathway and directs transfer-bound students to see a counselor for additional prerequisites — i.e. it is NOT an AS-T). There is NO Computer Science AS-T / ADT for transfer: Computer Science does not appear in the catalog's AS-T transfer-degree list (which contains only Admin of Justice, Biology, Business Administration, Early Childhood Education, Mathematics, Physics, and Public Health Science), and the current 2025 Computer Science department page lists only "Computer Science – AS" with no transfer variant. Other LOCAL computing associate degree: Information Technology (A.S.), in the Computer Information Systems dept. Note on change over time: the 2023-24 catalog also listed CIS "Computer Operations" (A.S.) and "Computer Programming" (A.S.) as local degrees, but the current (2025) CIS department page shows these as Certificates of Achievement (CA) rather than degrees, so they are excluded from the degree list. Certificates offered (not degrees): CIS Computer Programming, CIS Computer Operations, Computer User Support Specialist, Network and Security Specialist, and Liberal Arts STEM. Limitation: the current eLumen catalog and Program Mapper are JS-rendered/403-blocked; findings rest on the directly-read 2023-24 catalog PDF plus current official department web pages, which are consistent.

College of the Desert's official catalog (catalog.collegeofthedesert.edu, 2024-2026) shows the following computing associate degrees. TRANSFER: "Associate in Science in Computer Science for Transfer" (Computer Science AS-T / ADT) exists under the Computer Science program — so ast_cs_exists=true. There is NO local (non-transfer) A.S. titled "Computer Science"; the CS program lists only the AS-T, so local_cs_as_exists=false. LOCAL (non-transfer) A.S. computing degrees: (1) Computer Information Systems (Transfer Preparation) — A.S.; (2) Computer Information Systems (Employment Preparation) — A.S.; both are local A.S. degrees under the CIS program, not AS-T/ADTs. I also included "Applications and Information Systems" A.S. as a computing/information-systems local degree, though it is office-applications/Business Information Worker oriented (borderline; it anchors a set of BIW Certificates of Achievement). Certificates (BIW I-IV, Real Estate Specialist, etc.) are excluded per instructions as they are not degrees. Note: the CIS program description elsewhere mentions cybersecurity/software-development/data-analytics coursework, but the catalog presents only the two CIS A.S. degrees plus the AIS A.S. as degree awards. Confidence high — all data drawn directly from the college's own catalog program pages.

Per the LAHC 2025-2026 General Catalog (degree index p.52-54 and program pages), the "Computer Science and Information Technology" area offers: (1) Computer Information Systems — Associate in Science (AS) Degree, LOCAL (Academic Plan H010686C); (2) Computer Science and Engineering — Associate in Science (AS) Degree, LOCAL (Academic Plan H030026C); (3) Computer Science — Associate in Science for Transfer (AS-T) Degree, the state ADT/Transfer Model Curriculum (Academic Plan H043922H, confirmed via the college's Computer Science (AS-T) program sheet); and (4) a Computer Science Skill Certificate (SC, excluded as a certificate). ast_cs_exists = true (the Computer Science AS-T is offered). local_cs_as_exists = false: the exact title "Computer Science" only appears on the transfer AS-T and the Skill Certificate — there is no LOCAL A.S. degree simply titled "Computer Science." The local A.S. degrees are "Computer Information Systems" and the combined-title "Computer Science and Engineering," neither of which is essentially titled "Computer Science." Note: "Computer Technology," an A.S. in older catalogs, no longer appears as a degree in the current 2025-2026 index. Sources reached via curl with a browser user-agent; the lahc.edu site returns 403 to plain fetches but the catalog PDF and AS-T program sheet were retrieved and read directly.

Mission College (Santa Clara, CA) offers the transfer Computer Science Associate in Science for Transfer (AS-T/ADT) — so ast_cs_exists = true. It does NOT offer a LOCAL A.S. titled "Computer Science"; "Computer Science" appears only as the AS-T transfer degree, so local_cs_as_exists = false. The college's own computing-related LOCAL associate degrees are: (1) Computer Information Systems, A.S. (listed under the CIS department alongside the CS AS-T), and (2) Computer Network Technology, A.S. (listed under Computer Information Technology). Related items that are CERTIFICATES (not degrees, excluded from the list): Artificial Intelligence, Data Science, Computer Information Systems, Data Analyst Associate, and IT Helpdesk Technician Certificates of Achievement, plus a Cisco CCNA Cybersecurity and Cloud Computing Certificate. Sources are Mission College's own academic department pages (missioncollege.edu). The full eLumen catalog is JavaScript-rendered and could not be fully parsed, but the department pages are authoritative and internally consistent.

City College of San Francisco (CCSF) offers BOTH a local Computer Science A.S. and a Computer Science AS-T (for Transfer). Verified against CCSF's own CurriQunet curriculum system and the CCSF catalog: (1) LOCAL "Computer Science, AS" — CurriQunet Program Outline Report 1472 header reads verbatim "Computer Science, AS: Associate of Science (AS)"; active since 2006-07 (local, non-transfer). (2) "Computer Science, AS-T" (Associate in Science for Transfer) — listed in the CCSF majors-by-major record as first available 2025-26 and shown alongside the AS on the CS catalog page. (3) LOCAL "Computer Networking and Information Technology, AS" — CurriQunet Report 1317 header "Computer Networking and Information Technology, AS: Associate of Science (AS)". Computer Programming is offered only as Certificates of Achievement (e.g., Computer Programming: C++, Android App Programming, Build Automation for DevOps & QA) — no associate degree, so excluded. A former "Computer and Information Science, AS" (2006-07 to 2012-13) is discontinued and excluded. Note: ccsf.edu blocked direct WebFetch (403) and the Chrome extension was not connected, so CCSF catalog pages/PDFs were read via a public reader proxy; the two local AS degrees were also independently confirmed by directly fetching ccsf.curricunet.com reports.

local_computing_degrees.name: Information Systems: Web Development and Design Option

survey.summary: Per Reedley College's official 2025-2026 catalog (verified in the Computer Science Programs section, the master Degree & Certificate List, and the index), Reedley offers a LOCAL "Computer Science" Associate in Science Degree (program ID R.6921.AS, dept: Engineering/Computer Science/Physical Science, p.96). Its code is .AS (not .AS-T) and it does not appear in the college's AS-T list, so it is a local A.S., not a state transfer ADT — despite a transfer-prep purpose statement. Therefore local_cs_as_exists=true. There is NO Computer Science AS-T/ADT: the CS program lists only the local A.S. plus a "Computer Science - Counseling" certificate (R.4100.CN, excluded); no .AS-T code maps to Computer Science, so ast_cs_exists=false. Additional LOCAL computing A.S. degrees are in the Business dept under Information Systems: Information Technology Support (R.6952.AS, p.132), Networking (R.6975.AS, p.133), and Web Development and Design Option (R.6981.AS, p.135). Excluded certificates: Computer Science-Counseling (CN); IS IT Support Technician (CA/CC); IS Networking/Cisco CCNA (CA/CC); IS Web Development & Design (CA/CC); Cybersecurity Fundamentals (CA/CC); Cybersecurity Network Defense Technician (CA). Also excluded: Business Administration: Information Systems Management (R.2063.AS), a business-administration degree rather than a computing/CIS degree.

local_computing_degrees.name: General Studies: Office and Computer Technologies

survey.summary: Shasta College offers the Associate in Science in Computer Science FOR TRANSFER (AS-T in Computer Science, program AS-T.2005) — a state-standardized transfer ADT (ast_cs_exists=true). There is NO local (non-transfer) A.S. titled Computer Science; the college's own CS offering is exclusively the AS-T, so local_cs_as_exists=false. The college's LOCAL computing associate degrees fall under the Computer Information Systems / Business & Computer Technology area: (1) CIS - Systems Management (A.S.), which combines Microsoft Server, CISCO Networking, and Computer Maintenance emphases for entry-level IT work, and (2) General Studies: Office and Computer Technologies (A.S.). Computing certificates (NOT degrees, excluded from the list) include CIS - CISCO Networking and CIS - Computer Maintenance. All findings confirmed from Shasta College's official website program/degree pages (2024-2026 offerings).

Lake Tahoe Community College's Computer and Information Sciences (CIS) department offers ONE local associate degree in computing: an Associate in Science in "Information Technology (IT) Technician - Cybersecurity." There is NO local A.S. titled "Computer Science" and NO Computer Science AS-T/ADT. The CIS department page lists only the IT Technician-Cybersecurity A.S. plus certificates (Certificate of Achievement in IT Technician-Cybersecurity, Computer Programming Foundational Skills Certificate, and GIS Employable Skills / Non-Credit certificates) — all certificates are excluded from the degree list per instructions. The college's Associate Degrees for Transfer page confirms its AS-T offerings are Administration of Justice, Business Administration, Early Childhood Education, Geology, Hospitality Management, and Mathematics — no Computer Science AS-T among them. Sources are the college's own current program pages (ltcc.edu).

Glendale Community College (California, glendale.edu — not to be confused with the Arizona campus gccaz.edu) offers a LOCAL Associate of Science in Computer Science. Its dedicated program page describes "The Associate of Science degree" with no "for transfer"/AS-T/ADT wording, and the college's full Associate Degrees for Transfer list (verified twice) does NOT include any Computer Science AS-T. Therefore local_cs_as_exists=true and ast_cs_exists=false. A second local computing degree exists: Computer Information Systems, offered as an Associate of Science plus a Certificate of Achievement. The complete ADT roster (10 AS-T: Administration of Justice, Biology, Business Administration 2.0, Early Childhood Education, Film/TV/Electronic Media, Geology, Mathematics, Nutrition and Dietetics, Physics, Public Health; plus 20 AA-T) contains no computing transfer degree. Excluded certificates (not degrees): Computer Science Certificate, Computer Information Systems Certificate of Achievement, Computer Networking, Computer Programmer, Computer Software Technician, Cybersecurity Specialist, Information Technology Technician, Cloud Computing, Data Analytics, and Web Development.

Los Angeles Valley College (LACCD; CSIS department, STEM pathway) offers BOTH a transfer and a local Computer Science associate degree. Per LAVC's own official ProgramMap (STEM interest cluster), the Degrees list shows "Computer Science — Associate of Science for Transfer" (the AS-T/ADT) AND, separately in the non-transfer "Associate in Science" group, "Computer Science — Associate in Science" (the college's local A.S.). So local_cs_as_exists=true and ast_cs_exists=true. The only computing-related LOCAL associate degree is the Computer Science A.S. — no local CIS/CAOT associate degree was found; the CIS area-of-study page lists only certificates (Cloud Computing, Data Science and Analytics). Computing CERTIFICATES (excluded from the degree list) include Computer Science Programming (Certificate of Achievement), Cloud Computing (CoA), Data Science and Analytics (CoA), plus skills certificates in .NET, C/C++, Microcomputer Systems. IMPORTANT SOURCE NOTE: catalog.valleycollege.edu is San Bernardino Valley College (SBVC), NOT LAVC — that source was discarded; findings rely on LAVC's own programmap.lavc.edu and lavc.edu pages. LAVC's main www.lavc.edu pages return HTTP 403 to WebFetch, but the programmap.lavc.edu ProgramMap was reachable via the browser pane and is authoritative.

San Diego Miramar College (Computer and Information Sciences department) offers, per the official 2024-2025 SDCCD catalog (p.193-195): (1) a LOCAL "Associate of Science Degree: Computer and Information Sciences" (32-unit major + electives to reach 60 units); and (2) the state TRANSFER "Associate in Science in Computer Science for Transfer" (AS-T, 33-unit pattern). The "Computer Science" title exists ONLY as the AS-T transfer degree — there is NO local A.S. or A.A. titled "Computer Science" (so local_cs_as_exists=false). The AS-T in Computer Science exists (ast_cs_exists=true). The only LOCAL computing associate degree is the A.S. in Computer and Information Sciences. Two certificates exist but are excluded per instructions: Certificate of Achievement in Computer and Information Sciences, and Certificate of Performance in Computer Programming. Confirmed against both the college program page (sdmiramar.edu/programs/computer-and-information-sciences) and the catalog PDF.

Los Angeles Mission College (lamission.edu, LACCD) offers the state ADT "Computer Science" Associate in Science for Transfer (AS-T) — listed authoritatively as "ADT Computer Science 2025-2026" on the college's Associate Degrees for Transfer page. It does NOT offer a LOCAL A.S. titled "Computer Science." The Computer Science department (which is named "Computer Science") offers three LOCAL Associate in Science degrees: Computer Programmer (A.S.), Cyber Security (A.S.), and FullStack Developer (A.S.). It also offers non-degree Certificates of Achievement including Cloud Computing, Data Science/Data Scientist, Data Analyst, Computer Programming, and Full Stack (Web Application) Development (certificates excluded from the degree lists). lamission.edu blocks WebFetch (403), so data was obtained via the browser pane directly from two official pages; the transfer degree and the local degree list are each authoritatively confirmed. Note: majors.missioncollege.edu / missioncollege.edu results refer to Santa Clara's Mission College (a different institution) and were disregarded.

Per Ohlone College's official current 2025-2026 Curriculum Guide index, the CS/CNET departments offer one Computer Science TRANSFER degree — "Computer Science For Transfer" (ADT/AS-T) — so ast_cs_exists=true. There is NO local associate degree titled simply "Computer Science": the only "Computer Science"-titled award besides the ADT is "Computer Science Foundations" (Certificate of Achievement, not a degree). Therefore local_cs_as_exists=false. The college's five LOCAL (non-transfer) computing associate degrees are all A.S. and IT/networking-oriented: Database Administration, Information Technology, IT Service and Support, Network Administrator, and System Administration (each also offered as certificates). Additional computing CERTIFICATES only (excluded from the degree list): Cybersecurity (COA), Cisco Certified Network Associate (COAcc), Cloud Computing Administration (COAcc), IT Program Management (COAcc), Oracle Database Operations (COAcc), and Foundations for Learning Computer Science (noncredit). No local A.A. computing degrees exist. Note: ohlone.edu 403-blocks automated WebFetch; data was confirmed via a browser session reading the authoritative curriculum-guide index page.

local_computing_degrees.name: Computer Information Systems – Computer Retail Sales & Support
Computer Information Systems – Full Stack Web Development
Computer Information Systems – Help Desk/User Support
Computer Information Systems – System and Network Administration

survey.summary: Pasadena City College (id 49) offers the transfer degree "Associate in Science in Computer Science for Transfer (AS-T)" — confirmed on the official academic catalog as a CSU transfer ADT — so ast_cs_exists=true. There is NO local (non-transfer) A.S. or A.A. simply titled "Computer Science"; the only CS-titled degree is the AS-T, so local_cs_as_exists=false. Local (non-transfer) computing associate degrees, all Associate in Science: Data Science (under the Computer Science dept, each also has an accompanying Certificate of Achievement), and six Computer Information Systems (CIS) A.S. degrees — Computer Retail Sales & Support, Cybersecurity, Full Stack Web Development, Help Desk/User Support, Software Development, and System and Network Administration (each CIS AS also has a paired Certificate of Achievement). CIS also offers two Occupational Skills Certificates (CCNP Routing & Switching Preparation; CISCO Enterprise Network Associate) — certificates, excluded from the degree list. No CIS/Data Science degrees are ADTs. Sources: official curriculum.pasadena.edu catalog (Computer Science program page, CS AS-T page, and Computer Information Systems program page).

Foothill College offers BOTH a local A.S. in Computer Science and the transfer AS-T in Computer Science. The official 2024-2026 catalog program sheet (computer-science.pdf) for the "Computer Science" program lists "Award Type(s): AS = Associate in Science Degree" with a 56-unit major (90 units total, using the Foothill College General Education pattern), and states separately "This program also offers an Associate Degree for Transfer" — confirming the local A.S. is distinct from the AS-T. The transfer degree is confirmed as "Computer Science, AS-T" on its own catalog page/PDF. Under the college's Associate in Science (AS) degrees list, the only computing-related LOCAL degrees are Computer Science (A.S.) and Enterprise Networking (A.S.). Artificial Intelligence is offered by the CS department but appears only as certificate-level programming, not a listed associate degree. The CS program also offers numerous Certificates of Achievement (Software Development in C++/Java/Python, Advanced Software Development, Web Applications Development, Advanced Web Applications Development, Cloud Computing, Cybersecurity, IT Support, Network Computing) — these are certificates, not degrees, and are excluded from the degree lists.

Modesto Junior College (MJC) offers BOTH a local Computer Science A.S. degree and the transfer ADT. Confirmed via MJC's own award pages: (1) "Computer Science A.S. Degree" (cmps_aa.html), Degree/Certificate Type "A.S. Degree", explicitly "designed to meet the needs of non-transfer students" — this is the LOCAL A.S. in Computer Science, so local_cs_as_exists=true. (2) "Computer Science for Transfer Degree" (cmps_ast.html), type "A.S.-T Degree", guarantees CSU admission — this is the AS-T/ADT, so ast_cs_exists=true. (3) "Computer Information Systems A.S. Degree" (cmps_as.html), type "A.S. Degree" — a second local computing A.S. Excluded from the degree lists: four Certificates of Achievement (Computer Programming Specialist, Computer Applications Specialist, Computer Network Administration, Computer Network Technician) are certificates, not degrees. Also excluded is a separate "Computer Electronics A.S. Degree" (Technical Education division), which is an electronics/hardware technology degree rather than a computing/CS/CIS/programming degree. Sources are authoritative MJC catalog award pages reached directly (the elumenapp online catalog is JS-rendered and returned only a header, but the mjc.edu/awards pages rendered fully).

Per the MSJC 2026-2027 official catalog (catalog.msjc.edu), the Computer/Information Systems department offers three associate degrees: (1) Computer Science A.S.-T — an Associate in Science FOR TRANSFER (ADT, program code AST.CIS.CS.CG), the only degree titled "Computer Science"; (2) Computer Information Systems, A.S. — a LOCAL A.S. with emphases in Artificial Intelligence, Internet Authoring, Networking, and Programming; (3) Cybersecurity, A.S. — a LOCAL A.S. There is NO local (non-transfer) A.S. titled "Computer Science" — the only Computer Science degree is the transfer AS-T, so local_cs_as_exists=false and ast_cs_exists=true. Excluded certificates (not degrees): Internet Authoring, Networking, and Programming Certificates of Achievement.

San Diego City College's computing programs live in the Computer Information Systems (CIS) department (School of Business, Information Technology & Cosmetology). Per the official 2024-2025 catalog "Degree and Certificate List" table (verified via pdftotext), the CIS department offers three LOCAL Associate of Science degrees: Computer Programming (A.S.), Cybersecurity (A.S.), and Information Technology Management (A.S.). It also offers a Bachelor of Science in Cyber Defense and Analysis (baccalaureate, excluded from the associate list) and many certificates (C++, Computer Programming CoA, Cybersecurity CoA, Game Programming, Web Application Development, Desktop Support, etc. — all excluded). The separate "Computer Business Technology" program area offers only Certificates of Performance (Microsoft Office/Excel Essentials) — no associate degree in the current catalog. There is NO local A.S. titled "Computer Science" (local_cs_as_exists=false) and NO "Computer Science for Transfer" AS-T/ADT anywhere in the catalog (ast_cs_exists=false). The CIS department's own transfer section explicitly advises CS-bound students to consult a counselor or earn an AA in Liberal Arts and Sciences rather than pointing to a CS ADT, and a catalog-wide search for "Computer Science" degree titles returned only course/major-preparation references, never a degree. Note: within SDCCD, the Computer Science ADT is offered at Mesa and Miramar Colleges, not at City.

local_computing_degrees.name: Computer Science, Associate in Science Degree
Software Development, Associate in Arts Degree

survey.summary: Golden West College (Coast Community College District, catalog.cccd.edu) currently offers three computing-related associate degrees. LOCAL degrees: (1) "Computer Science, Associate in Science Degree" — a local A.S. distinct from the transfer version, confirmed as a stand-alone local program; and (2) "Software Development, Associate in Arts Degree" (local A.A.). TRANSFER degree: "Computer Science, Associate in Science Degree for Transfer" (AS-T / ADT, banner code 2_AST_CSCI, launched Fall 2023, guarantees CSU admission). So both local_cs_as_exists and ast_cs_exists are TRUE — the college has BOTH a local CS A.S. and the CS AS-T. A "Software Development, Certificate of Achievement" also exists but is excluded as it is a certificate, not a degree. Note: an older web summary mentioned a "Video Game Development, Associate in Arts" degree, but the authoritative 2026-2027 catalog programs page does NOT list it among current degrees, so it is not included (likely discontinued or renamed); no CIS-titled associate degree was found in the current catalog.

local_computing_degrees.name: Computer Science with Emphasis in Video Gaming

survey.summary: Palomar College's CSIT department offers a LOCAL "Computer Science" Associate in Science (A.S.) degree (listed as "Computer Science (AS, CA)", where CA is a Certificate of Achievement, excluded here). So local_cs_as_exists = true. There is NO Computer Science AS-T/ADT: Palomar's official "A Degree with a Guarantee" articulation page lists 31 Associate Degrees for Transfer, and Computer Science is not among them (it lists Mathematics AS-T but no CS). None of the CSIT degrees carry a "for Transfer" title, so ast_cs_exists = false. Local (non-transfer) computing A.S. degrees include Computer Science, Computer Science w/ Emphasis in Video Gaming, Information Technology, Data Science, CIS: Data Analytics, Management Information Systems, Cybersecurity, Cloud Computing and Virtualization, and Web Design and Development. Numerous Certificates of Achievement/Performance also exist but are excluded as non-degrees. Confidence is moderate: department pages and the authoritative ADT list are consistent, but the 2026-27 CurriQunet interactive catalog is JS-rendered and could not be fully loaded to independently reconfirm every award type.

Santa Rosa Junior College offers a LOCAL Computer Science degree awarded A.S. — a 34-unit major in the Computer Studies department (course/program prefix "CS" = Computer Studies) described as preparing students for transfer but NOT structured as a state ADT. It is NOT an Associate in Science for Transfer. SRJC does NOT offer a Computer Science AS-T/ADT: the authoritative program list (curriculum.santarosa.edu/majors, which flags all ADTs with AS-T/AA-T badges) shows none of the computing majors carry an AS-T/AA-T marker. Other LOCAL computing associate degrees in the same department: Cybersecurity (A.S.), Game Development (A.S.), Web Full-Stack Developer (A.S.), and Web and Multimedia (A.A.). Two additional Computer Studies programs — Graphic Design (A.A.) and Virtual Production/RVPA (A.A.) — are design/media rather than computing/programming and were excluded from the computing list. No certificates are counted as degrees. Confidence is high: the Computer Studies department page, the CS program description, and the curriculum majors list (fetched twice) agree. Catalog list-of-majors pages truncated under WebFetch but the curriculum majors page is authoritative and consistent.

Berkeley City College offers computing associate degrees only under Computer Information Systems (CIS), not a "Computer Science" title. The 2024-2025 official catalog lists two LOCAL A.S. degrees: "Applied Computer Information Systems - A.S. Degree" and "Advanced Computer Programming - A.S. Degree" (each also available as a Certificate of Achievement, excluded here). There is NO local A.S. titled "Computer Science" and NO Computer Science AS-T/ADT: the catalog's complete Associate Degrees for Transfer list includes Biology, Business Administration 2.0, Mathematics, and Physics AS-Ts, but no Computer Science for Transfer. "CIS 005 Introduction to Computer Science" is a course, not a degree. Verified against both the BCC CIS department page and the 2024-2025 catalog PDF (pdftotext extraction).

Los Medanos College (Contra Costa CCD) offers Computer Science ONLY as a transfer degree: the "Computer Science" Associate in Science for Transfer (AS-T / ADT). There is NO local (non-transfer) A.S. titled "Computer Science" — so local_cs_as_exists = false, ast_cs_exists = true.

The department states it offers "three AS degrees," which are: (1) Computer Science AS-T (transfer), (2) Information Technology A.S. (local), and (3) Computer Applications Support Specialist A.S. (local). The two local A.S. degrees are the computing-related non-transfer associate degrees.

Numerous certificates exist and were excluded from the degree list per definitions: Computer Support Specialist (Basic/Advanced), Information Technology (Basic/Advanced), plus College Skills Certificates (Foundation, Game Design, Web Design). Note the college's "Advanced/Basic" IT and Computer Support items are Certificates of Achievement, not degrees.

Sources: the college's official 2025-2026 catalog Degrees & Certificates listing (degree4cat25-25.aspx) shows verbatim: "Computer Science" = AS-T; "Information Technology" = AS; "Computer Application Support Specialist" = AS; the remaining computer entries are certificates. The department pages confirm Computer Applications Support Specialist is a local A.S. degree (IGETC or LMC associate-degree requirements, not transfer-specific) and Information Technology is an "Associate of Science in Information Technology." The Computer Science AS-T is corroborated by the ADT program sheet (COMSCADTRevised8-27-19.pdf) titled "Associate of Science in Computer Science for Transfer." Confidence high.

local_computing_degrees.name: Cloud and Network Cyber Security Administration
Liberal Arts and Sciences: Information Technology Emphasis

survey.summary: Mt. SAC does NOT offer a degree titled "Computer Science" of any kind. The Computer Science (CSCI) discipline page (mtsac.edu/computer) explicitly states "AS Degree Programs: None" and "Certificates: None" — CSCI offers only stand-alone courses (fundamentals, C++, Java, assembly, data structures, discrete math) that transfer, but no CSCI-titled degree. So local_cs_as_exists=false. There is also NO Computer Science AS-T/ADT: the Math & Computer Science division offers only a Mathematics AS-T (and a Mathematics-emphasis AA), with no Computer Science for Transfer degree, so ast_cs_exists=false. The college's actual computing associate degrees are LOCAL A.S. degrees, mostly under the Computer Information Systems (CIS) program: Computer Programming; Computer Database Management Systems; Cloud and Network Cyber Security Administration; Artificial Intelligence for Business; Big Data Analytics (these five are among the six CIS A.S. degrees — the sixth, Administrative Assistant A.S., is office-admin, not computing, so excluded), plus Computer and Networking Technology A.S. There is also a local A.A. in Liberal Arts and Sciences with an Information Technology emphasis. CIS additionally offers ~22 professional certificates (excluded as non-degrees). None of the computing degrees are "for transfer"/ADT. Note: the CIS degree lineup has evolved from older catalog names, so some catalog PDFs may show slightly different titles.

Palo Verde College offers one LOCAL computing associate degree: an A.S. in Computer Information Systems (27 units; 18 required + 6 restricted electives + 3 restricted). There is NO local degree titled "Computer Science" (the CIS A.S. mentions "further study in computer science / computational science" in its description, but the degree title is Computer Information Systems). There is NO Computer Science AS-T/ADT — the college's transfer-degree roster is limited to Administration of Justice, Anthropology, Business Administration (+ Business Administration 2.0), Early Childhood Education, and Child & Adolescent Development, none in computing. Computing-related CERTIFICATES exist (Computer Applications, 3D Computer Animation, 3D Printing & Rapid Prototyping, Graphic Design & Web Content, IT Technician Prep, Information Technology Literacy, Management Information Systems) but are excluded as they are not degrees. A broad A.A. "Area of Emphasis: Business & Technology" also exists but is a general business area-of-emphasis, not a computing/CS/CIS degree, so it is excluded. Findings corroborated across the college's own programs page, its ADT listing, and a third-party program directory; the acalog online catalog pages are JS-rendered and returned no content via fetch.

local_computing_degrees.name: Computer Information Technology/Information Systems and Technology

survey.summary: Rio Hondo College's Computer Science Department offers ONLY the transfer degree: Associate in Science in Computer Science for Transfer (AS-T). There is NO local (non-transfer) A.S. titled "Computer Science" — so local_cs_as_exists = false and ast_cs_exists = true. Local computing associate degrees are all A.S. degrees housed in the Computer Information Technology (CIT) department: Computer Systems, Information Systems and Technology, Cybersecurity, Network Administrator, and Systems Administrator (all A.S.). The CIT department also offers an "Office Technologies" A.S., which is office-productivity/clerical focused rather than core computing/programming, so it is excluded from the computing list above but noted here. Numerous Certificates of Achievement exist (Computer Systems, Cybersecurity, Cybersecurity Technician, Ethical Hacker, Network Administrator, Systems Administrator, Office Technologies, plus low-unit Career Certificates and Desktop Technician / Cloud Computing Practitioner CCs) — these are certificates, not degrees, and are excluded. Confirmed via the college's own department "degrees & certificates" page and the Guided Pathways program pages (2024-2025 catalog).

Saddleback College offers BOTH a local Associate in Science (A.S.) in Computer Science AND the Associate in Science for Transfer (AS-T/ADT) in Computer Science. Three authoritative Saddleback pages (the all-programs Computer Science directory entry, the STEM programs-and-degrees page, and the dedicated Computer Science program page) consistently list Computer Science as "AS, ADT," and the program page describes both a local AS ("provide the transfer student the opportunity to achieve an Associate degree") and a distinct "Associate in Science for Transfer" that grants CSU priority admission. So local_cs_as_exists=true and ast_cs_exists=true. No Computer Science certificate is listed. Other LOCAL computing-related associate degrees in the STEM school include Cyber Defense (A.S.) and Computer Maintenance Technology (A.S.) — the latter is more hardware/IT-oriented and is included here as computing-adjacent. Cyber Operations and Cybersecurity/Information Security Analyst exist only as certificates (excluded from the degree list). Confidence is high; no browser was needed as pages fetched cleanly.

Santiago Canyon College (SCC, part of Rancho Santiago CCD) offers Computer Science as BOTH a local A.S. and a transfer AS-T, plus a local Computer Information Systems A.S.

Confirmed via SCC's official program-finder (pathways) pages:
- Computer Science, A.S. — LOCAL degree. Page titled "Computer Science, AS" (sccollege.edu/pathways/stem/computer-science2); described as preparing students for careers as engineering aides, scientific computing technicians, and junior programmers. This is the college's own A.S., distinct from the ADT.
- Computer Science, AS-T (Associate in Science in Computer Science for Transfer) — TRANSFER ADT (sccollege.edu/pathways/stem/computer-science3), the state-standardized degree guaranteeing CSU admission.
- Computer Information Systems, A.S. — LOCAL degree (sccollege.edu/pathways/business/computer-information-systems); explicitly a local A.S., not an ADT; covers programming (Visual BASIC, C++, Java), systems analysis.

So local_cs_as_exists = true (a local Computer Science A.S. exists, separate from the AS-T) and ast_cs_exists = true.

Certificates (excluded from the degree list per instructions): a Computer Information Systems Certificate of Achievement appears in SCC's eLumen catalog. Not counted as a degree.

Caveats: The 2024-2025 catalog PDF and the eLumen catalog (sccollege.elumenapp.com) are JS-rendered / returned 404 or session-expired via WebFetch, and the Chrome browser extension was not connected, so I could not read the raw catalog program tables directly. Findings rest on the college's authoritative pathways pages, which are current program-finder pages. Confidence is high for the CS AS-T and the local CIS A.S.; the local Computer Science A.S. is confirmed by its dedicated official pathway page but was not additionally cross-checked against the raw catalog PDF.

local_computing_degrees.name: Computer Information Systems (CIS) - Microcomputer Application Specialist

survey.summary: Coalinga College (formerly West Hills College Coalinga) offers one computing associate degree: a LOCAL A.S. in Computer Information Systems (CIS) - Microcomputer Application Specialist. There is NO standalone 'Computer Science' A.S. degree and NO Computer Science AS-T/ADT transfer degree (the college's AS-T transfer degrees are in Biology, Chemistry, Geology, Mathematics, and Physics only). A CIS - Microcomputer Application Specialist Certificate of Achievement also exists but is excluded as it is not a degree. Confirmed consistently across the dedicated CIS program page, the 2025-2026 catalog programs-of-study page, and the Degrees & Certificates listing.

Cañada College (Redwood City, CA) offers the Computer Science Associate in Science for Transfer (AS-T / ADT) — a state-standardized transfer degree (60 units), completable in C++ or Java. This is the ONLY associate degree in computing the college offers. There is NO local (non-transfer) A.S. or A.A. in Computer Science or CIS. The remaining computing credentials are Certificates of Achievement, not degrees: Computer Science - C++ (16 units), Computer Science - Java (16 units), and Computer Science - Swift (16 units). Findings are consistent across three authoritative catalog pages (department degrees page, AS-T program page, and the full program listing). Therefore local_cs_as_exists=false, ast_cs_exists=true, and no local computing associate degrees.

Verified against Chaffey College's official 2025-2026 catalog (extracted from the catalog PDF). Chaffey offers exactly ONE computing transfer degree: "Computer Science for Transfer - AS-T Degree" (p.139) — this is the state ADT, so ast_cs_exists=true. There is NO local A.S. titled "Computer Science" (a full-catalog grep for a local "Computer Science - A.S." returned nothing), so local_cs_as_exists=false. Local (non-transfer) computing A.S. degrees in the current catalog, all housed under the Computer Information Systems (CIS) department: Computer Information Systems - A.S. (p.135), Cloud Security - A.S. (p.126), Cloud Solutions Architecture - A.S. (p.127), and Cybersecurity Professional - A.S. (p.149). Certificates of Achievement (excluded from degree list) include Computer Game Development, Computer Information Systems, Cybersecurity Analyst, Cybersecurity Defender, Programming Foundations, and Cloud Analyst. Note: A March 2026 college news release announced four NEW programs (Artificial Intelligence, Data Science, DevOps, Game Development) with associate degrees + certificates launching Fall 2026; these are not yet in the current 2025-2026 catalog (Game Development is currently only a certificate), so they are not included in the confirmed local list. Transfer status of the forthcoming programs was not specified.

Per Crafton Hills College's official 2025-2026 SmartCatalog, the college offers BOTH a local A.S. in Computer Science and the transfer AS-T in Computer Science, listed as separate programs under Computer Science (CSCI). local_cs_as_exists=true (Computer Science - Associate of Science, a local non-transfer degree distinct from the AS-T). ast_cs_exists=true (Associate in Science in Computer Science for Transfer, AS-T). Other local computing degree: Computer Information Systems: Network Administration - A.S. A former "Computer Information Systems: Programming - A.S." existed in the 2023-2024 catalog but its 2025-2026 page now returns HTTP 404 and it appears only as a "Programming Certificate," so it is excluded. Excluded certificates (not degrees): Cisco Certified Network Associate, Cloud Technology Specialist, Computer Information Systems, Cybersecurity Specialist, Computer Hardware Technician, IT Technician, Programming. Confirmed via authoritative smartcatalogiq.com catalog pages plus the college's official major sheets.

Cypress College's Computer Science department currently offers only the Computer Science Associate in Science Degree for Transfer (AS-T) — the state ADT/transfer degree. There is NO local (non-transfer) A.S. titled "Computer Science" (so local_cs_as_exists=false, ast_cs_exists=true). Local computing degrees live in the Computer Information Systems department: Computer Information Systems (A.S.) and Cyber Defense (A.S.) — both confirmed current in the 2025-2026 NOCCCD catalog (Cal-GETC GE language), neither an AS-T. CIS also offers a Business Administration AS-T (Transfer Program) and ~11 certificates including a Computer Programming Certificate (certificates excluded per definitions). A "Computer Programming Associate in Science Degree" still appears in stale search-engine results, but its dedicated catalog page now returns HTTP 404, it is absent from the current CIS degrees-and-certificates landing page (which lists Computer Programming only as a certificate), and its cached text uses obsolete CSU GE Breadth/IGETC language — indicating it was discontinued; it is therefore excluded from the current-offerings list.

Gavilan College offers the transfer AS-T in Computer Science (Associate in Science in Computer Science for Transfer), confirmed via search results and the college's standalone program page (business/computer_science/as_t_degree). There is NO local A.S. titled "Computer Science" — the "Computer Science" title at Gavilan belongs solely to the AS-T transfer degree. The CSIS department offers six LOCAL A.S. degrees (verified across two fetches of the department page): Business Computer Applications, Computer Networking, Computer Programming, Programming for the Internet, Scientific Programming, and UNIX Operating System — all A.S., none titled "Computer Science." Certificates (excluded from the degree list per definitions): Certificates of Achievement in Business Computer Applications, Computer Networking, Computer Programming, and UNIX Operating System; plus Certificates of Proficiency in Computational Preparation for Bioinformatics, Computer Hardware, Computer Networking, and Web Page Production Specialist. Current catalog year is 2026-2027. So local_cs_as_exists=false, ast_cs_exists=true.

Napa Valley College's current catalog (2025-2026 / 2026-2027) lists exactly one computing associate degree: "Computer Science" awarded A.S.-T. (Associate in Science in Computer Science for Transfer / ADT). This is verified against the college's complete official Degrees and Certificates listing, which contains no other computer-related degree. There is NO local (non-transfer) A.S. or A.A. in Computer Science, and no Computer Information Systems or Computer Studies degree in the current catalog. Note: the college's separate career-education marketing page mentions an "A.S. Degree in Computer Studies" plus Microsoft Office certificates, but that Computer Studies A.S. does not appear anywhere in the current official catalog degree list (appears discontinued/stale). Certificates only (not degrees, excluded per definitions): Geographic Information Systems (Certificate of Achievement) and Microsoft Office skills certificates. So the only computing degree is the transfer AS-T; no local computing associate degrees are currently offered.

Per Orange Coast College's current CCCD catalog (catalog.cccd.edu), OCC offers a LOCAL Computer Science, Associate in Science Degree — the degree page explicitly describes it as designed for entry-level programmers and notes "Courses in this program may or may not qualify for transfer credit," confirming it is a local A.S., not an AS-T. There is NO Computer Science Associate in Science for Transfer (AS-T): CS does not appear in the catalog's alphabetical list of "for Transfer" degrees, nor in the master programs list, though course descriptions reference students separately pursuing "the Computer Science AS-T transfer degree" (i.e., transferring, not an OCC-conferred AS-T). Other local (non-transfer) A.S. computing degrees: Computer Information Systems (A.S.) and Embedded Systems (A.S.). Certificates (Computer Science Certificate of Achievement; C++, Java, and Python Certificates of Specialization; CIS Certificate of Achievement; Embedded Systems Technician Certificate of Achievement) are excluded as they are not degrees. Note: Embedded Systems is computing/programming-related but is listed somewhat apart from the core CS/CIS pathway; included here as a computing A.S.

Per Laney College's official 2025-2026 catalog (Computer Information Systems / CIS Department section), Laney offers three LOCAL A.S. degrees in computing: (1) CIS--Computer Programming - A.S., (2) Computer Information Science - A.S., and (3) Data Analytics - A.S. NO local degree is titled "Computer Science" (A.S.) — the closest, "Computer Information Science," is a distinct CIS-titled degree, not "Computer Science," so local_cs_as_exists=false. There is NO Computer Science AS-T/ADT: the catalog's complete "Available Associate Degrees for Transfer (ADT)" list contains no computing/CIS/CS transfer degree (only Anthropology, Art, Business Administration 2.0 AS-T, Communication Studies, Economics, English, Geography, Global Studies, History, Hospitality Management, Journalism, Kinesiology, Mathematics AS-T, Music, Philosophy, Physics AS-T, Political Science, Psychology, Social Justice tracks, Sociology, Spanish, Theatre Arts), so ast_cs_exists=false. Excluded certificates (not degrees): Computer Programming with C++, Computer Programming with Java, Computer Systems Analysis, Database Management with SQL, Data Science Fundamentals (Certificates of Achievement). Note: CIS 005 "Introduction to Computer Science" is a course, not a degree.

Riverside City College offers the transfer AS-T in Computer Science (Associate in Science in Computer Science for Transfer, code AS650), confirmed on the official RCC Computer Science program page and via 2022-2025 AS-T major sheets. There is NO local A.S. degree titled "Computer Science" — the only "Computer Science"-titled degree is the AS-T (transfer), so local_cs_as_exists=false. Local (non-transfer) computing A.S. degrees are housed under Computer Information Systems (CIS): Computer Programming (A.S., code AS728), Cyber Defense (A.S.), Cyberpreneurship (A.S.), and Data Science (A.S.). Numerous certificates of achievement exist (C++, Java, Cisco Networking, AWS Cloud Computing, Web Master/Web Developer/Web Designer, Information Security & Cyber Defense, Information Technology, IT Technician, Data Analytics) but these are certificates, not degrees, and are excluded. Data verified from official rcc.edu program pages; catalog curricular-pattern pages were JS-rendered and did not return degree detail, but the program pages were authoritative.

West Valley College (Saratoga, CA; id 80) offers Computer Science ONLY as a state-standardized Associate in Science Degree for Transfer (AS-T in Computer Science, 60 units). No LOCAL (non-transfer) A.S. or A.A. in Computer Science exists in the current 2025-2026 catalog, so local_cs_as_exists = false. No local CIS/computing associate degree appears either — the catalog's computing department is consolidated under "Computer Science," a catalog CIS department page 404s, and the full "Degrees and Certificates" programs list shows no local computing degree. Therefore local_computing_degrees is empty. Non-degree credentials (excluded per definitions) include the Computer Science Certificate of Achievement (24 units), the Cisco Certified Network Associate Certificate of Achievement (12 units), and several Google/Microsoft continuing-education Certificates of Completion. CAUTION: search results also surfaced catalog.valleycollege.edu (San Bernardino Valley College), a DIFFERENT college that DOES have a local "Computer Science Associate of Science Degree"; that was excluded and not attributed to West Valley College. Findings are consistent across the CS department page, the AS-T program page, and the full programs list, all on the authoritative westvalley.edu catalog.

Lassen Community College offers one LOCAL computing associate degree: an Associate in Science in Computer Information Systems (A.S., 60 units), delivered fully online, per the college's official CIS program page. There is NO local degree titled "Computer Science" and NO Computer Science A.S. — the only computing associate is the CIS A.S. There is NO Computer Science for Transfer (AS-T/ADT); the college's official Transfer Degrees page lists all ~21 ADTs it offers (Admin of Justice, Agriculture, Business Admin, ECE, Nutrition/Dietetics, and various University/General Studies transfer degrees), and no computing ADT appears among them. Certificates (NOT degrees, excluded from the degree list) under CIS: Computer Support Specialist (Certificate of Achievement, 16 units) and IT Support Professional (Certificate of Completion, 150 hours). Sources are authoritative lassencollege.edu pages; the large 2024-2025 catalog PDF could not be fetched (exceeded size limit) but the program and transfer-degree pages corroborate the findings.

Per the official VCCCD 2025-26/2026-27 Oxnard College catalog (catalog.vcccd.edu), Oxnard offers exactly one Computer Science associate degree, and it is the transfer degree: "Computer Science, Associate in Science for Transfer" (AS-T). There is NO local (non-transfer) A.S. or A.A. titled "Computer Science" — the AS-T is the only CS credential listed under the Computer Science program. The one local (non-transfer) computing associate degree is "Computer Networking Information Technology, Associate in Science" (A.S.). Confirmed against three authoritative catalog pages: the Computer Science program page, the Computer Networking/IT program page, and the comprehensive "Associate Degrees in Specific Majors" listing, all of which agree. Related computing offerings that are NOT degrees (excluded from the list): Computer Networking Information Technology, Certificate of Achievement; Cybersecurity, Certificate of Achievement; and three Proficiency Awards (Administering Microsoft Windows, Cisco Networking, Wireless Networking). The Computer Information Systems / Office Technology area appears as a department but has no distinct CIS associate degree listed in the catalog majors index.

Yuba College's 2025-2026 catalog lists, under COMPUTER SCIENCE, two degrees plus one certificate. (1) A LOCAL Associate in Science in Computer Science ("A.S.-Computer Science") — the catalog explicitly calls it "the local AS degree," a flexible 19-21 unit major intended for students transferring to CSU/UC/private institutions where requirements don't align with the AS-T. (2) A TRANSFER Associate in Science for Transfer in Computer Science ("A.S.-T-Computer Science" / Computer Science AS-T, 29-30 unit major), the state-standardized ADT. (3) A Certificate of Achievement in Computer Science (not a degree, excluded from the degree list). Therefore local_cs_as_exists=true and ast_cs_exists=true. No other local computing associate degrees (no separate Information Technology/CIS/networking A.S. or A.A. in the current catalog; GIS is offered under Geography, not as a computing degree). Confirmed against the authoritative catalog PDF's "Yuba College Degrees and Certificates" index and the Computer Science program pages (catalog pp. 85-87).

Per the authoritative WLAC 2025-2026 Academic Catalog (Computer Science & Application Division, pages 89-95). TRANSFER: "Computer Science (AS-T)" exists (p.89, "Associate in Science in Computer Science for Transfer", Major Code 0706.00) — so ast_cs_exists=true. LOCAL degrees in computing: (1) "Computer Science Information Technology" (A.A., p.90, Major Code 0702.00 — catalog states "The Associate of Arts degree in Computer Science Information Technology"); (2) "Computer Web Support & Database Administration" (A.S., p.92 — "The Associate of Science degree in Computer Web Support..."). There is NO local A.S. titled essentially "Computer Science" — the only "Computer Science" degree is the AS-T transfer version — so local_cs_as_exists=false. Also present but excluded from the computing list: "Liberal Arts & Sciences: Math, Science & Computer Science (A.A.)" (p.130), a general-studies breadth emphasis rather than a computing major; and "Engineering: Computer & Software Track (A.S.)" which is an engineering, not CS/CIS, degree. Many Certificates of Achievement exist (Cyber Security Essentials, Computer Network & Security Management, Cloud Computing–AWS, Data Science, Data Analytics, Essentials of Video Game Programming, Mobile Application Development, Business Applications & Database Mgmt, etc.) but certificates are excluded per definition.

local_computing_degrees.name: Computer Information Systems: Emphasis in System Administration

survey.summary: Per the official SBCC 2025-2026 CourseLeaf catalog, both key credentials exist. The Computer Science department offers a LOCAL "Computer Science, Associate of Science (AS)" and the state transfer "Computer Science, Associate in Science for Transfer (AS-T)". So local_cs_as_exists=true and ast_cs_exists=true. Additional local computing associate degrees: Data Science A.S. (also from the CS dept), and from the Computer Information Systems dept, CIS A.S. and CIS A.S. with an Emphasis in System Administration. All local computing degrees are awarded A.S.; no A.A. degrees found. Excluded (not degrees): Computer Science and Data Science Certificates of Achievement; CIS certificates (Cloud Computing Architect, Database Programming & Applications Development, IT Support Technician, PC Support and Network Management, Technical Writing); and Skills Competency Awards (Mobile Application Developer, Software Engineering, Data Analytics, and several system-administration SCAs). Confirmed via WebFetch on individual CourseLeaf degree pages, so no browser fallback was needed.

Sierra College's current catalog (verified via the official degrees-certificates PDF extracted with pdftotext, and the CS department catalog page) shows both a transfer and a local Computer Science associate degree.

TRANSFER (ADT): "Computer Science for Transfer: AS-T Degree" — the state-standardized AS-T. So ast_cs_exists = true.

LOCAL CS A.S.: "Computer Science—Computer Science: AA/AS Degree" — a local degree offered as BOTH Associate in Arts (A.A.) and Associate in Science (A.S.). Because an A.S. option essentially titled Computer Science exists locally (not the ADT, not a certificate), local_cs_as_exists = true.

Other LOCAL computing degrees, all under the Information Technology department and all awarded A.S.: Cloud Computing, Cybersecurity, Data Analytics, IT Technician, and Network Technician. Each also has a corresponding Certificate of Achievement, which are excluded from the degree lists per the definitions. The Mathematics department separately offers a local "Data Science: AS Degree" (data/programming-adjacent, listed under Math, not counted among the CS/IT computing degrees above but noted for completeness).

No standalone "Management Information Systems" degree appears in the current catalog under Computer Science; it showed up only in older/marketing text and is not a current award. Confidence is high — data came directly from Sierra College's own current catalog rather than third-party summaries.

Per Solano Community College's authoritative 2026-2027 catalog (pp. 206-217): The Computer Science Department offers exactly one degree, the "Associate in Science in Computer Science for Transfer (ADT: A.S.-T)" — a state-standardized TRANSFER degree (ast_cs_exists=true). There is NO local (non-transfer) A.S. titled "Computer Science" (local_cs_as_exists=false). The local computing degrees are all housed in the Computer Information Science Department: Computer Programming (A.S.), Microcomputer Applications (A.S.), and Web Design and Development (A.S.) — each 30-33 unit majors that are also offered as Certificates of Achievement (certificates excluded from the degree list). Caveat: Solano's marketing page (solano.edu/academic-programs/computer-science.php) claims a local "Associate in Science (AS) in Computer Science" for workforce entry, but the official catalog does not list any such local A.S. — the only Computer-Science-titled award is the AS-T; the marketing copy appears inaccurate/auto-generated.

Per the authoritative VCCCD catalog (catalog.vcccd.edu, current 2025-2026), Ventura College's Computer Science department offers exactly ONE associate degree: "Computer Science, Associate in Science for Transfer" (AS-T/ADT) — a state-standardized transfer degree, so ast_cs_exists=true. There is NO local (non-transfer) A.S. or A.A. in Computer Science, so local_cs_as_exists=false. No local A.S./A.A. degrees exist in any computing area (Computer Information Systems, Computer Programming, Computer Networking) at Ventura — those local degrees exist at sister colleges Moorpark and Oxnard, but not at Ventura. Therefore local_computing_degrees is empty. The department's remaining computing credentials are Certificates of Achievement only (Cisco CCNA, CyberSecurity, and Data Science and Artificial Intelligence), which are excluded per the definitions since certificates are not degrees. Three sources cross-checked: the Computer Science catalog page, the Computer Science program PDF, and the programs-courses index — all agree.

Chabot College offers a LOCAL Associate in Science (A.S.) in Computer Science. The CurriQunet program map (Fall 2024, 60-63 units) and the department page describe it as a local A.S. degree that prepares students to transfer to a 4-year CS program, but it is NOT a state ADT. No "Computer Science for Transfer" (AS-T) exists in Chabot's official Degree & Certificate Programs list — consistent with the fact that California has no statewide TMC/ADT template for Computer Science. Other local computing A.S. degrees live in the Computer Application Systems (CAS) department: Information Technology (A.S., with Cloud Computing/Cybersecurity/Networking options) and Software Specialist (A.S.). Certificates of Achievement also exist for Information Technology and Software Specialist but are excluded as they are not degrees. Sources cross-confirmed: chabotcollege.edu programs.php degree list, the CS department page, the CAS department page, and CurriQunet program reports.

Per the official Citrus College 2024-2026 catalog, the Computer Science discipline offers exactly one associate degree: the ADT in Computer Science, which the catalog explicitly labels an "A.S.-Transfer Degree" (Associate in Science for Transfer / AS-T). So ast_cs_exists = true. There is NO local (non-transfer) A.S. or A.A. in Computer Science — the only CS associate credential is the transfer ADT, so local_cs_as_exists = false. The CS discipline also lists a Certificate in Gaming and Applications Development, but that is a Certificate of Achievement, not a degree, and is excluded. No separate CIS/Computer Information Systems local associate degree exists at Citrus. The comprehensive associate-degrees list turned up no local computing/CS/CIS/programming A.S./A.A. degrees; the only other computing-adjacent A.S. is "Digital and Web Design," which sits under the Visual Art & Photography discipline (a design degree, not CS/CIS/programming) and is therefore excluded from local_computing_degrees. Net: local_computing_degrees is empty.

local_computing_degrees.name: Networking, Security and System Administration - Enterprise Networking
Networking, Security and System Administration - Enterprise System Administration

survey.summary: Per the Grossmont-Cuyamaca CCD 2024-2026 academic catalog, Cuyamaca College offers the Computer Science for Transfer (AS-T/ADT) under its Computer Science program (ast_cs_exists=true). There is NO local A.S./A.A. titled "Computer Science" — the only degree bearing that title is the transfer AS-T, so local_cs_as_exists=false. Under Computer and Information Science (CIS), three LOCAL Associate in Science degrees are offered: Enterprise Networking (A.S.), Enterprise System Administration (A.S.) — both under "Networking, Security and System Administration" — and Web Development (A.S.); each is paired with a Certificate of Achievement. Certificate-only programs (excluded from the degree list) include Cisco Certified Network Associate, Computer Programming, Computer Support Technician, Cyber Security Specialist, Web Design, and Web Programming (Certificates of Specialization). Confidence is high from the authoritative district catalog.

Mendocino College offers Computer Science ONLY as the state transfer degree: "Associate in Science in Computer Science for Transfer" (AS-T), confirmed on its dedicated official page. There is NO local (non-transfer) A.S. titled "Computer Science" — so local_cs_as_exists is false and ast_cs_exists is true. The college's LOCAL (non-transfer) computing associate degrees, all A.S., are: Information Technology, Cybersecurity, and Computer Applications and Office Administration. Related certificates (excluded as they are not degrees) include IT Specialist, Cybersecurity Defense, Digital Forensics, Penetration Testing, and Computer Applications and Office Administration. Sources are the official Mendocino College degrees listing and program pages; findings are consistent and high-confidence, no browser needed.

College of the Siskiyous (id 102) offers BOTH a local Computer Science A.S. and a Computer Science AS-T for Transfer. The college's official Counseling "Degrees, Majors & Certificates" page lists "Computer Science: AS" (local associate) and "Computer Science: AS-T" (Associate in Science for Transfer). It also offers two certificates in this area — "Programming" and "PC Network/Hardware/Software" — which are certificates, not degrees, and are therefore excluded from the local_computing_degrees list. So local_cs_as_exists=true (the local "Computer Science" A.S.) and ast_cs_exists=true (the CS AS-T). Confirmed via the siskiyous.edu counseling majors page and corroborated by the CTE Computer Science department page and multiple third-party listings; the full elumen 26-27 catalog degree-detail pages are JS-rendered and could not be parsed directly, but the college's own majors page is authoritative.

El Camino College (id 103) does NOT offer a local A.S. degree titled "Computer Science" — its Computer Science offering is only a Computer Science Certificate of Achievement (a certificate, not a degree), per the college's Computer Science area-of-study page. It also does NOT offer a Computer Science AS-T/ADT: the official 2025-2026 Transfer Guide (p.22) lists all 24 current Associate Degrees for Transfer, and Computer Science is absent (the STEM ADTs present are Mathematics AS-T, Physics AS-T, and Geology AS-T). The only LOCAL computing associate degree is Computer Information Systems, A.S. (confirmed as the sole associate degree on the CIS area-of-study page and in catalog search results). Related certificates (excluded from the degree list) include the Computer Science Certificate of Achievement and several CIS certificates (Business Programming, Cisco Networking Administration, Computer User Support Specialist, Database Management, Technical Support and Networking Specialist, Business Information Worker). Caveat: the primary catalog host catalog.elcamino.edu has an expired TLS certificate and ProgramMapper returned 403, so both were unreachable; findings rest on El Camino's own www.elcamino.edu area-of-study pages and the official Transfer Guide PDF, which are authoritative.

local_computing_degrees.name: Computer and Information Sciences: Computer Science
Computer and Information Sciences: IT Support Specialist
Computer and Information Sciences: Network/Systems Administration

survey.summary: Per the authoritative Cerritos College CourseLeaf catalog (2024-2026), the college offers BOTH a local Computer Science A.S. and the Computer Science AS-T transfer degree. LOCAL A.S.: "Computer and Information Sciences: Computer Science (A.S.)" — a local (non-transfer) Associate of Science essentially titled Computer Science, so local_cs_as_exists = true. (Note: its catalog URL slug reads "computer-science-aa," but both the page H1 heading and the master program-list page show the award as "(A.S.)" / ASSOCIATE OF SCIENCE, corroborated across two independent catalog pages.) TRANSFER: "Computer Science (AS-T)" = "Associate in Science in Computer Science Transfer," an ADT for seamless CSU transfer, so ast_cs_exists = true. Five local A.S. computing degrees total under the CIS department: Computer Science, Cybersecurity, IT Support Specialist, Network/Systems Administration, and Programmer.
