Six related CERT programs (Client-Side Web Page Development, Cloud Computing, Cybersecurity, IT Support Specialist, Network/Systems Administration, Programmer) are Certificates of Achievement and are excluded from the degree list per instructions. No A.A. computing degrees found; all local computing awards are A.S.

Per the official Coast Community College District 2025-2026 catalog (catalog.cccd.edu/coastline), Coastline offers NO degree titled "Computer Science" — neither a local A.S. nor an Associate in Science for Transfer (AS-T). Cross-checked the Computer & Cyber Sciences pathway page, the ICS degree pages, the full programs listing, and the Associate Degrees for Transfer page; the transfer-degrees list contains zero computing/CS/CIS/IT ADTs (Coastline's ADTs are in fields like Business, Math, Physics, Chemistry, etc.), so ast_cs_exists=false. local_cs_as_exists=false: the closest local degree is "Information and Computer Sciences, Associate of Science" — a genuine local A.S. and the nearest CS-equivalent, but its title is "Information and Computer Sciences," not essentially "Computer Science," so I did not count it under the strict flag (flagged here as the ambiguous closest match for the user to judge). Local (non-transfer) computing A.S. degrees offered: Information and Computer Sciences, Computer Information Systems, Information Technology, Data Science, Data Analytics, Artificial Intelligence, AI for Cybersecurity, Cybersecurity, and Digital Forensics and Incident Response. Excluded from the degree list: a "Digital Media Design, A.A." (media/design, not CS/programming) and numerous Certificates of Achievement/Specialization/Accomplishment (CIS, Data Analytics, Data Science, Introduction to Programming, cybersecurity tracks, IT, etc.), which are not degrees.

Grossmont College (CSIS department) offers the transfer degree "Computer Science for Transfer (AS-T)" but has NO local A.S. titled simply "Computer Science." Its local (non-transfer) computing Associate in Science degrees are: Computer Programming, Cybersecurity and Networking, Information Technology Support Specialist, Web Design and Development, and Applied Artificial Intelligence — each paired with a Certificate of Achievement. Certificate-only computing credentials (excluded from the degree list) include Data Science, Programmable Logic Controller/Controllers, and various AI/IT Certificates of Achievement, Proficiency, and Completion. Confirmed against the official Grossmont-Cuyamaca CCCD academic catalog (catalog.gcccd.edu) STEM associate-degree listing and the grossmont.edu CSIS programs page.

Per Imperial Valley College's own current Program Mapper (programmap.imperial.edu), the college offers these computing associate degrees: (1) Computer Information Technology — Associate in Science (LOCAL A.S., housed in the Business/CIT department), and (2) Computer Science — Associate in Science for Transfer (AS-T / ADT). There is NO local A.S. titled "Computer Science" — the only "Computer Science" award is the transfer AS-T. No separate "Computer Information Systems" associate degree exists (the local degree is titled "Computer Information Technology"). Excluded certificates (not degrees): Computer Fundamentals for ESL Students (Certificate of Completion), Computer Information Technology (Certificate of Achievement), and Computer Networking (Certificate of Achievement). Therefore: local_cs_as_exists = false; ast_cs_exists = true; local computing degree = Computer Information Technology (A.S.). Verified via the live program mapper (searches for "Computer," "Information," and "Programming"); the current CIT department page on imperial.edu corroborates the local A.S. + certificate.

MiraCosta College (2024-2025 catalog) offers the transfer AS-T "Computer Science for Transfer" (ast_cs_exists=true). There is NO local A.S. degree titled "Computer Science" (local_cs_as_exists=false). The only local degree in the Computer Science discipline is an A.A. in "Software Development" (built on the Software Development Certificate of Achievement). The Computer Studies & Information Technology (CSIT) department offers a family of local A.S. degrees each paired with a matching Certificate of Achievement: Artificial Intelligence, Computer Applications, Cybersecurity, Information Technology, Internet and E-Commerce, Management Information Systems (MIS), Systems Administration, and Tech Support. CSIT also lists an A.S. in "Executive Administrative Professional," which I excluded from local_computing_degrees as it is office-administration rather than computing/CS/CIS/programming. Certificates (Certificate of Achievement/Proficiency, e.g., Software Development, Game Developer, Mobile Application Development) are excluded per the degree definition. Data drawn from the official MiraCosta catalog discipline pages for Computer Science and Computer Studies & Information Technology, cross-checked against the master degrees-and-certificates list.

Verified against the authoritative 2025-2026 San Joaquin Delta College Catalog PDF (current catalog, catoid=12), cross-checked with the online catalog search. Both a LOCAL "Computer Science, AS" and the TRANSFER "Computer Science, AS-T" exist and are listed on the same catalog page (p.332). The AS-T page reads "The Associate in Science in Computer Science for Transfer degree provides a solid preparation for transfer majors..."; the local AS page reads "Upon successful completion of the Associate in Science degree in Computer Science, the student is prepared for careers in programming, web site development, or computer network maintenance." So local_cs_as_exists=true and ast_cs_exists=true.

LOCAL (non-transfer) computing associate degrees found in the current catalog index: Computer Science, AS (p.332); Computer Information Systems, AS (p.335); Computer Network Security Technician, AS (p.330); Computer Network Technician, AS (p.330). All awarded A.S. No A.A. computing degrees found.

Engineering-adjacent degrees also exist but were excluded from the computing list as they sit in the Engineering area: "Engineering - Computer, AS" (p.364) and "Engineering Computer Aided Drafting, AS" (p.367) — mention only.

Excluded certificates (not degrees): Computer Science Certificate of Achievement, Computer Support Certificate of Achievement, Computer Support Technician Certificate of Achievement, Computer Programming Certificate of Achievement / Competence / Essentials, Computer Networking Competence / Essentials / Software certificates, and a Computer Science Web Developer certificate.

Note on sources: the Modern Campus / Acalog program preview pages are bot-blocked (HTTP 202/empty via WebFetch and curl, JS-rendered), so verification was done from the official downloadable 2025-2026 catalog PDF, which is authoritative. needs_browser=false.

Allan Hancock College (2024-2026 catalog) offers TWO Computer Science associate degrees: (1) a LOCAL "Computer Science" degree awarded as an Associate in Arts (A.A.) — the catalog explicitly states "Award Type: Associate in Arts," describing it as the associate degree program in computer science for students who desire to transfer to a four-year school; and (2) a state-standardized "Computer Science" Associate in Science for Transfer (AS-T / ADT). Therefore ast_cs_exists = true. Critically, local_cs_as_exists = FALSE: the college's own (non-transfer) CS degree is an A.A., NOT an A.S. — there is no local A.S. titled "Computer Science." Other LOCAL computing degrees: Computer Business Information Systems — Associate in Science (A.S.), a CIS degree covering software applications, networking/help-desk, information systems security, and database management. Related/borderline (not included in the computing-degree list): "Mathematics with Computer Science Emphasis — Associate in Arts" (a mathematics degree); "Computer Networking and Electronics Technology" tracks (Electronics Technology A.S., Mechatronics A.S., Network Maintenance and Digital Technologies A.S. — networking/electronics vocational). Numerous certificates exist (CBIS, CBOT, CNEE) but are excluded as they are not degrees. Sources: verified against the college's own 2024-2025 catalog PDF (award types confirmed verbatim) and the live Computer Science pathways page, which both list only the AS-T and the A.A. for Computer Science.

College of Alameda's only computing-related program is Computer Information Systems (CIS), which offers a single LOCAL associate degree: an Associate of Arts (A.A.) in Computer Information Systems. There is NO local Associate of Science titled "Computer Science" and NO Associate in Science for Transfer (AS-T/ADT) in Computer Science or CIS. Two authoritative College of Alameda pages (the CIS department page and the Degrees & Certificates page) agree that the computing offerings are: the CIS A.A. degree plus certificates only — a Certificate of Achievement in CIS and Certificates of Proficiency in specializations (Data Analytics, Desktop Support Technician, Web Publishing). Certificates are excluded from the degree list per the definitions. The CIS program description notes courses "prepare students for transfer," but no standalone Computer Science degree or AS-T is offered. The current catalog (2025-26) lives on a JS-rendered CurriQunet platform that could not be scraped directly, but the live department/degrees pages are authoritative and consistent, and older CIS catalog PDFs (now 404) previously corroborated the A.A. award. Result: local_cs_as_exists=false, ast_cs_exists=false; one local computing degree — CIS (A.A.).

Copper Mountain College (Joshua Tree, CA) offers a TRANSFER Computer Science degree (Computer Science, AS-T / ADT) — this is the only program titled "Computer Science." There is NO local A.S. titled Computer Science. Two LOCAL computing associate degrees exist: Computer Information Systems (A.S.) — confirmed on its program page as a local Associate in Science, explicitly "not an AS-T" — and Computer Programming (A.S.), confirmed as an Associate in Science. The college also offers many computer-related certificates (Computer-Aided Design & Drafting Specialist, Computer Information Systems, Computer Programming, Computer Network Supervisor as Certificates of Achievement; plus Ethical Hacking, Network Specialist, Security Specialist, and Support Specialist Certificates of Proficiency), but these are excluded as they are not degrees. Findings drawn from the college's official cmccd.edu degrees and program pages.

East Los Angeles College's Computer Science / Computer Information Systems program (Business Administration dept) offers exactly one LOCAL associate degree and one TRANSFER degree in computing. LOCAL: "Computer Science Information Technology" awarded as an Associate in Arts (A.A.) — confirmed on both the department page and the /academics/aos/csit area-of-study page ("ASSOCIATE DEGREE PROGRAMS: Computer Science Information Technology, Associate in Arts Degree"). There is NO local A.S., and no local degree plainly titled "Computer Science" — so local_cs_as_exists = false. TRANSFER: the department page lists an "Associate Degree for Transfer (ADT): Computer Science", which is the state-standardized Computer Science AS-T (Associate in Science for Transfer) — so ast_cs_exists = true. Certificates (excluded from the degree list): Certificates of Achievement in Cloud Computing, Computer Science Information Technology-Microcomputers, Computer Science Information Technology-Programming, Data Analytics, and Data Science, plus several Skills Certificates (Cloud Computing 1, Computer & Business Essentials, Cyber Security, Intro to Front-End UI, Intro to Back-End Development). Data obtained via curl with a browser user-agent because elac.edu returns 403 to WebFetch.

Taft College's current 2025-2026 catalog offers a Computer Science for Transfer degree (AS-T/ADT), confirmed both in the catalog degrees-and-certificates list and on a dedicated "Computer Science Degree for Transfer" program page (Associate in Science in Computer Science for Transfer, designed for CSU transfer, esp. CSUB). There is NO local (non-transfer) A.S. or A.A. titled "Computer Science" — the CS offering is transfer-only. The one local computing-related associate degree is "Advanced Information Technology and Management," awarded as an Associate in Science (A.S.); the same program also has a locally-approved certificate (excluded from the degree list). Note: "Computer Science for Transfer" appears in the 2025-2026 catalog list but not the 2024-2025 list, indicating the AS-T was added/reinstated for the current cycle; a live program page confirms it currently exists.

Antelope Valley College (id 121) offers BOTH a local and a transfer Computer Science associate degree. Its current Computer Science (CS) department page lists, under "Credit Programs": (1) Computer Science A.S. (60 units) — a LOCAL, non-transfer degree described as "a broad computer science degree focused on programming, data structures, algorithms, assembly language, discrete structures, and mathematics"; and (2) Computer Science AS-T (60 units) — the state-standardized Associate in Science for Transfer to CSU. So local_cs_as_exists=true and ast_cs_exists=true. The AS-T is corroborated by AVC's dedicated program sheet "Computer Science Associates of Science for Transfer 25-26" and the college's ADT list. Two certificates are also offered (Programming Fundamentals, 15 units; Software Development Essentials, 14 units) — excluded from the degree list as they are not degrees. Note: a separate Computer Applications/CIS department historically offered a local "Business Computer Information Science" A.S. plus computing certificates (per a 2018 program sheet), but the current CA department page returns HTTP 403 and I could not verify that degree against the current 2025-26 catalog, so it is not included in local_computing_degrees. Only the confirmed Computer Science A.S. is listed there.

Feather River College (id 122) offers NO Computer Science degree of any kind. Verified against the authoritative current 2026-2027 catalog "Degrees and Certificates" master list (frc.curriqunet.com/Catalog): there is no local A.S. Computer Science, no AS-T/ADT Computer Science, and no standalone Computer Information Systems or Programming degree. The alphabetical A-V degree list has only "CalGETC" and "Child Care Entrepreneurship" under C. The only AS-T-style transfer degrees in a computing-adjacent area is Mathematics A.S.-T (not CS). The single computing-adjacent LOCAL associate degree currently in the catalog is Office Technology - A.S. (plus an Office Technology Certificate of Achievement, excluded as a certificate). Office Technology is really an office/administrative-technology degree, though its core includes a Computer Information Systems course (ICT 131) and Web Design (ICT 161); it is listed here because it is the sole surviving degree from the college's former ICT/computer department and is the closest computing degree offered. Historically that ICT department also awarded A.S. Web Development and A.S. Multimedia degrees, but those do NOT appear in the current 2026-2027 catalog and appear to have been discontinued/absorbed (the frc.edu ICT page still showing them is explicitly flagged as outdated). Bottom line: local_cs_as_exists=false, ast_cs_exists=false; the only current computing-adjacent local degree is Office Technology A.S.

local_computing_degrees.name: Computer Science and Information Systems — Computer Science Option
Computer Science and Information Systems — Network and Security Option
Computer Science and Information Systems — Digital Web and Mobile Development

survey.summary:
Confirmed via the 2024-2025 Hartnell College Catalog (official degrees-and-certificates master list plus individual program sheets extracted from the source PDFs).

TRANSFER: "Computer Science for Transfer" exists as an AS-T (ADT, program code AST.CS; program sheet titled "ASSOCIATE IN SCIENCE FOR TRANSFER"). => ast_cs_exists = true.

LOCAL DEGREES: Hartnell has an umbrella program "Computer Science and Information Systems" that awards three LOCAL Associate of Science degrees:
1. Computer Science Option (AS.CISCS) — program sheet confirms "ASSOCIATE OF SCIENCE"; major is the CSS core sequence (CSS-1, CSS-2A, CSS-2B, CSS-3, CSS-7). This is essentially a local Computer Science A.S., so local_cs_as_exists = true.
2. Network and Security Option — A.S.
3. Digital Web and Mobile Development — A.S.

CERTIFICATES (not degrees, excluded from lists): Cisco Certified Network Associate, Cybersecurity, Technical Support, Mobile Development Option, and Web Development Option are Certificates of Achievement under the same CIS program.

NOTE on local_cs_as_exists judgment: the local A.S. is titled "Computer Science and Information Systems — Computer Science Option" rather than bare "Computer Science." I count it as essentially a Computer Science A.S. because the option name is literally "Computer Science" and the required major is the CSS/computer-science core. (A separate "Information Processing" A.S. exists under Business Office Technology but is business/office tech, not CS/CIS/programming, so it is excluded.)

The WebFetch PDF-to-markdown step failed (binary), so text was extracted locally with pdftotext from the saved catalog PDFs — data is authoritative and not fabricated.

Irvine Valley College (current 2026-2027 catalog) offers BOTH a local Computer Science, A.S. and a Computer Science for Transfer, AS-T. The School of Mathematics and Computer Science page (www.ivc.edu/mathcs/computer-science) explicitly lists "Computer Science, AS" (local, AS-GE 2-year) alongside "Computer Science, AS-T" (CSU-GE transfer), plus non-degree UC Transfer major pathways. Catalog search results independently corroborate both a "Program: Computer Science, AS" page and a "Program: Computer Science for Transfer, AS-T" page. Other LOCAL computing associate degrees come from the Computer Information Management department: Computer Applications A.S., Information Technology A.S., and Web Authoring A.S. (all local A.S., no AS-T). The Administrative Assistant A.S. was excluded as office-admin, not computing. Numerous certificates (Computer Applications COA, Information Technology COA, Cisco Networking CCNA COA/COP, Web Authoring COA, etc.) exist but are excluded as non-degrees. Note: catalog.ivc.edu and ivc.catalog.acalog.com program pages returned 404/blank to automated fetch (JS-rendered/bot-protected); findings rely on the college's authoritative live department pages plus corroborating search snippets.

Porterville College (id 125) does NOT offer a local associate degree titled "Computer Science" (A.S.), and does NOT offer a Computer Science AS-T. Its AS-T list contains only Administration of Justice, Agribusiness, Biology, Business Administration, Child Development (ECE), Environmental Science, and Mathematics — no computing AS-T. Its LOCAL computing/information-systems associate degrees, grouped under "Computing and Information Systems," are: Computer Information Systems (A.S.), Advanced Information Systems (A.S.), and Business Information Systems (A.S.) — all local A.S. degrees, not transfer ADTs. The Computer Information Systems A.S. covers web design, systems analysis/design, programming, networking, and internet protocols. The nearest thing to "Computer Science" by title is "Computer Information Systems," which is CIS, not CS. (There are also computing certificates such as Basic Computing (JSC), which are excluded as they are not degrees.) Evidence gathered from the college's own site (AS degrees list, AS-T degrees list, certificates/degrees-offered page, and the Computer Information Systems program page), corroborated by the 2024-2025 and 2025-2026 catalogs.

Skyline College's current catalog offers exactly one Computer Science degree: the "Computer Science for Transfer" Associate in Science Degree for Transfer (AS-T / ADT, 60 units), designed for transfer to CSU. There is NO local (non-transfer) A.S. or A.A. titled "Computer Science," and no local "Computer Information Systems/Science" associate degree exists. The only LOCAL computing associate degree is Network Engineering (Associate in Science, 60 units). Computing-related certificates (excluded as they are not degrees): Network Engineering (Certificate of Achievement, 36 units), Data Center Physical Infrastructure (Certificate of Specialization, 12 units), and Junior Developer (Certificate of Specialization, 9-10 units). Data confirmed via WebFetch against the authoritative current online catalog programs listing.

Per the official LASC 2024-25 College Catalog (downloaded directly, HTTP 200; Programs of Study chart p.15 and program detail pages p.108-110), LASC's computing degrees are all LOCAL — there is NO Computer Science AS-T/ADT. LASC's ADTs are limited to Administration of Justice, Business Administration, Early Childhood Education, Nutrition and Dietetics, Mathematics, Physics, and Public Health (plus AA-T degrees); none in Computer Science.

local_cs_as_exists set TRUE based on "Computer Science: Information Technology" (Associate in Science, plan S002866C). Its title leads with "Computer Science" and the curriculum is genuine CS (CS 101 Introduction to Computer Science, CS 111 Programming in Visual Basic, CS 118 Micro Assembly, CS 114 Programming in C, CS 136 Data Structures). NUANCE / judgment call: the exact title carries the ":Information Technology" qualifier — there is no plain, unadorned "Computer Science" A.S. If the flag is meant to require an exact "Computer Science" title, downgrade to false.

Other local computing associate degrees: "Computer Applications and Office Technology" (A.S., S002864C, office/CAOT software skills), and "Mathematics: Computer Science" (a math-with-CS-emphasis degree; NOTE a catalog inconsistency — the Programs of Study chart lists it as "AS" while its own program-detail page header reads "Associate in Arts in Mathematics: Computer Science"; I recorded A.A. per the program page).

Certificates only (NOT degrees, excluded from lists): Apple Coding Developer, Apple Coding Foundations, Cloud Computing AWS, Homeland Security and Computer Network, Homeland Security Network Administrator (Certificates of Achievement); Computer Technician, Computer Network Cabling Technician, Telecom and Network Technician, Web Site Designer, CIWA, ICT CyberSecurity Technology Essentials (Skill Certificates). Data is authoritative; confidence tempered only by the local_cs_as_exists titling judgment and the Math:CS award discrepancy.

Per San Bernardino Valley College's official academic catalog (catalog.valleycollege.edu, 2026-2027), the college offers BOTH a local and a transfer CS degree. LOCAL: "Computer Science Associate of Science Degree" (A.S.) — confirmed local, non-ADT (uses SBVC or Cal-GETC GE pattern). TRANSFER: "Computer Science Associate in Science for Transfer Degree" (AS-T/ADT) — confirmed. Other LOCAL computing associate degrees: Applied Artificial Intelligence (A.S., under the Computer Science department); CIT - Management Information Systems (A.S.) and Information Systems and Technology (A.S., both under Computer Information Technology). Also present but EXCLUDED: CIT - Office Technology (A.A.) is a clerical/office-productivity degree, not CS/CIS/programming. Certificates excluded per definitions: Computer Science Certificate of Achievement, Artificial Intelligence Certificate of Achievement, Data Science Certificate of Achievement, plus numerous CIT certificates (AWS Cloud, cybersecurity/forensics, network/support specialist, etc.). Confidence high — read directly from the authoritative college catalog.

Verified against the MPC 2024-2026 catalog Programs A-Z listing (raw HTML pulled via curl; catalog.mpc.edu pages are JS-rendered and blank to WebFetch, so exact titles come from the served HTML). Computer Science: the ONLY "Computer Science" award is "Computer Science, Associate in Science for Transfer" (AS-T) — so ast_cs_exists=true and local_cs_as_exists=false (no local A.S. titled Computer Science; the CS section otherwise has only two Certificates of Achievement: Applied Artificial Intelligence, and Software Development). Local (non-transfer) computing associate degrees: (1) "Computer Networking and Security, Associate in Science" (A.S.) in the Computer Security and Information Systems / CIS department — clearly a CIS/networking/security degree; its siblings are all Certificates of Achievement/Completion (CCNA, Fundamental/Intermediate/Advanced IT Networking & Security, Microsoft Operating Systems Skills) which are excluded. (2) "Computer Applications and Business Skills, Associate in Science" (A.S.) under the Business Skills department — included as a computing-family degree but note it is office/business-applications oriented (Microsoft Office Specialist track) rather than CS/CIS/programming proper. No local CIS-titled A.S. beyond Computer Networking and Security. Confidence high — titles and award types read directly from the authoritative catalog HTML.

local_computing_degrees.name: Computer Science Associate in Science Degree
Computer Information Systems Associate in Science Degree
Cyber Security Associate in Science Degree

survey.summary: Fullerton College (2025-2026 NOCCCD catalog) offers a LOCAL "Computer Science Associate in Science Degree" (A.S., ~22-24 units) — so local_cs_as_exists = true. This is a local degree, not a transfer ADT: the catalog page uses no "for Transfer"/AS-T/C-ID/TMC language, and Computer Science does not appear on Fullerton's verbatim Associates-for-Transfer (AD-T) list of 37 programs. Consistent with the fact that California has no statewide Computer Science TMC/ADT, ast_cs_exists = false. (The Math & Science division does offer a Mathematics AS-T, and the AD-T list includes Physics/Chemistry/Biology AS-Ts, but no CS AS-T.) Other LOCAL computing A.S. degrees: Computer Information Systems (A.S.) and Cyber Security (A.S.), both under the Business & CIS division. Numerous computing offerings (Programming, Networking, Web Design, Game Design, Office Technology) exist only as Certificates of Achievement and are excluded from the degree list. An earlier web-search summary asserting Fullerton offers a "Computer Science AS-T" was a hallucinated paraphrase of generic AS-T boilerplate and is contradicted by the authoritative catalog and articulation pages.

Long Beach City College (LBCC) offers a LOCAL Associate in Science in Computer Science (35-unit major, meets C-ID standards) — NOT a transfer AS-T/ADT. Its own program pages and the official CourseLeaf catalog list it as "Computer Science - Associate in Science" (catalog slug computer-science-as); no "computer-science-ast" transfer degree exists (a targeted search for a CS AS-T returned only the local -as page). So local_cs_as_exists=true, ast_cs_exists=false. Additional LOCAL computing A.S. degrees: Computer Technology (Information Systems) A.S., and Computer Security & Networking A.S. All are local, non-transfer. Certificates (excluded from the degree list) include: Computer Science Certificate of Achievement, Android App Developer Certificate of Accomplishment, Computer Technology Certificate of Achievement, several Cryptocurrency Fundamentals and Computer Information Competency certificates, plus Computer Security & Networking certificates (Computer Hardware Technician, Microsoft Windows Networking Technician, UNIX Network Administrator, Computer Networking Technician). Note: the CourseLeaf catalog (lbcc-public.courseleaf.com) is JS-rendered and returned empty via fetch, but the authoritative lbcc.edu program pages rendered clearly and consistently, so findings are well-supported.

local_computing_degrees.name: Computer Information Systems: Microsoft Networks - MCSE

survey.summary: San Jose City College (2024-2026 CourseLeaf catalog) offers the transfer Computer Science AS-T (Associate in Science for Transfer / ADT) as its only degree titled "Computer Science" — there is NO local (non-transfer) A.S. titled "Computer Science", so local_cs_as_exists=false. Its LOCAL computing associate degrees live under the Computer Information Systems (CIS) department: five A.S. degrees — CIS Computer Programming, CIS General Networking, CIS Microsoft Networks (MCSE), CIS UNIX Networks, and CIS Web Developer. CIS also offers many Certificates of Achievement (Levels 1-3, e.g. CCNA, cybersecurity, Google IT Support) which are excluded as non-degrees. The Computer Applications program's overview text mentions an A.S., but the current catalog lists only two Level 1 certificates (Business & Data Analytics, Project Management) under it and the former Computer Applications A.S. page now returns 404, so it is not counted as a current local degree. All degree data verified against the college's own catalog.sjcc.edu pages (the CIS landing page and the Computer Science AS-T page). Note: the CIS majors listed above use "Computer Information Systems: <track>" naming in the catalog; exact major-sheet titles are prefixed "CIS".

Moorpark College (VCCCD) does NOT offer a local (non-transfer) A.S. titled "Computer Science." Its only Computer Science associate degree is the transfer degree: "Computer Science, Associate in Science for Transfer" (AS-T/ADT), so ast_cs_exists=true and local_cs_as_exists=false. Under the Computer Science department, all other credentials are sub-degree: Computer Programming (Certificate of Achievement), Web Design (Certificate of Achievement), Mobile App Developer/iOS (Proficiency Award), and Web Development (Proficiency Award) — none are degrees, so all excluded from the degree list. The one confirmed LOCAL computing associate degree is in a sibling department: "Computer Network Systems Engineering, Associate in Science" (A.S.), a local networking/IT degree (not a transfer ADT). Its related cybersecurity offerings ("Cybersecurity Administration," "Cybersecurity Penetration Tester") are Certificates of Achievement, not degrees. Source is the official current VCCCD catalog for Moorpark, which rendered cleanly (no bot-block/JS issues).

College of the Canyons (id 140) offers BOTH a local and a transfer CS associate degree. LOCAL: "Associate in Science Degree: Computer Science" (36 units, CMPSCI-based, offered through the Math/Science school) — confirmed via the college's own degree sheet, so local_cs_as_exists=true. TRANSFER: "Associate in Science for Transfer Degree: Computer Science" (AS-T, 30 units, SB 1440 ADT) — confirmed, so ast_cs_exists=true. Additional LOCAL computing A.S. degrees under the Computer Applications department: Computer Applications (A.S.), Web Development (A.S.), and Web Publishing & Design (A.S.). Administrative Assistant A.S. was excluded as office-administration rather than computing/programming. Certificates of Achievement/Specialization (Computer Applications, Web Development, Web Publishing & Design, E-Commerce Technology, etc.) exist but are excluded per definitions since they are not degrees. Sources are the college's official degree PDFs from canyons.edu and the Computer Applications department program page.

Cosumnes River College (Los Rios district) offers a LOCAL A.S. essentially titled Computer Science: "CIS - Computer Science" (A.S.), described as appropriate for students planning to transfer to CSU/UC in Computer Science or Computer Engineering, but it carries no AS-T/ADT designation. The college's 2025-2026 official catalog lists NO Computer Science Associate in Science for Transfer (AS-T/ADT) and no computing ADT of any kind. All computing awards are local A.S. degrees under Computer Information Science: CIS - Computer Science, Business Information Technology, Cybersecurity and Information Assurance, Information Technology Associate, Management Information Systems, and Web Developer. Note: a "Computer Science" Certificate of Achievement also exists but is a certificate, not a degree, and is excluded from the degree lists. Two authoritative catalog pages (the CIS program page and the List of Degrees and Certificates) were consistent.

Folsom Lake College (Los Rios CCD) offers a LOCAL A.S. in Computer Science (non-transfer) and a LOCAL A.S. in Information Technology. Per the 2025-2026 official catalog "List of Degrees and Certificates" and the Computer Information Science program page, there is NO "Computer Science for Transfer" AS-T/ADT — the college's transfer support for CS is via Cal-GETC GE maps on the local A.S., not a state-standardized AS-T. Computing content is organized under the Computer Information Science (CIS) family (CISA/CISC/CISN/CISP/CISS/CISW). Certificates offered (excluded from the degree list per definitions): Computer Programming (Certificate of Achievement), Information Technology (certificate), and Algorithmic and Logical Thinking (certificate). local_cs_as_exists=true because a local A.S. essentially titled "Computer Science" exists; ast_cs_exists=false because no CS AS-T was found.

Lemoore College (formerly West Hills College Lemoore, part of West Hills CCD) offers NO Computer Science degree of any kind in its 2025-2026 catalog. There is no local A.S. titled "Computer Science" (local_cs_as_exists=false) and no Computer Science Associate in Science for Transfer (ast_cs_exists=false) — the only STEM ADTs are Mathematics (AST), Physics (AST), Biology (AST), and Geology (AST). The college's computing/CIS offerings are branded under "Information Technology" and "Computer Software" rather than "Computer Science." Two LOCAL (non-transfer) computing associate degrees exist, both awarded A.A. (not A.S.): (1) Information Technology (AA) — the core IT/CIS degree with career tracks; (2) Business Information Systems (AA) — an office-technology/information-systems degree centered on Microsoft Windows/Office and digital/web literacy. All other computing offerings are CERTIFICATES (excluded from the degree list): Full-Stack Web Development (Certificate of Achievement), Front-End Web Development (Local Certificate), Back-End Web Development (Local Certificate), Introduction to Cybersecurity (COA), Networking Fundamentals (COA), User Experience (UX) Design (COA), and Information Technology Specialist. Data is from the authoritative eLumen 2025-2026 catalog program list, cross-verified against individual program pages. Note the college's public marketing pages were internally inconsistent (one listed the IT degree as A.A., another as A.S.); the official catalog resolves it as A.A.

Woodland Community College's current 2026-2027 catalog (published June 23, 2026) contains a complete "Certificates and Degrees" table listing every program by department. There is NO Computer Science program of any kind: no local Computer Science A.S. and no Computer Science AS-T/ADT for transfer (the transfer-center ADT page confirms no CS AS-T among its AA-T/AS-T offerings). The only computing-related offering is under the Business Computer Applications department: Business Computer Applications Basics, a Certificate of Completion (COC, noncredit) which is a certificate, not a degree, so it is excluded. No CIS, information systems, programming, software, IT, or cybersecurity associate degrees appear anywhere in the catalog. Both flags are false and there are zero local computing associate degrees.

survey.summary:
Norco College (a Riverside CCD college, id 148) offers Computer Science ONLY as a transfer degree: the "Computer Science for Transfer (AS-T)" / ADT, confirmed by the college's official "Academic Degrees and Certificates" programs page and by the 2024-2025 and 2025-2026 Computer Science AS-T articulation/major sheets. There is NO local (non-transfer) associate degree titled "Computer Science" (so local_cs_as_exists = false; ast_cs_exists = true).

Local (non-transfer) computing associate degrees are housed under Computer Information Systems and Game Development (School of Math, Engineering, Computer Science & Game Development):
- Computer Information Systems -- Computer Programming (A.S.) [also offered as Certificate]
- Computer Information Systems -- Graphic Design (A.S.) [also offered as Certificate]
- Game Development - Game Development Core (A.S.)
- Game Development - Game Concept Art (A.S.) [more art-focused, game-dev domain]
- Game Development - 3D Game Modeling and Animation (A.S.) [more art/animation-focused]

CIS-related CERTIFICATES (NOT degrees, excluded from the degree list): CIS -- C++ Programming (Cert), CIS -- Java Programming (Cert), plus the certificate versions of the CIS Computer Programming and Graphic Design programs.

Adjacent but likely out-of-scope for "computing/CS/CIS/programming": Computer Numerical Control (CNC) Programming (A.S.) is a manufacturing/machining program (G-code), and CNC Operator (Cert); Data Science is listed as a program area but the official pages show no distinct AS degree/certificate detail for it. These were not included in the local computing degree list.

Note: the Game Concept Art and 3D Game Modeling AS degrees are art/animation-heavy within the game-development domain; they are included as computing-school degrees but a stricter reading focused on programming/CS/CIS would keep only CIS Computer Programming, CIS Graphic Design, and Game Development Core.

Moreno Valley College (mvc.edu) offers the transfer AS-T "Computer Science" (Associate in Science for Transfer, program code MAS650, ~29 units) — confirmed on the Computer Science program page, the Degrees for Transfer page, and the STEM School programs-of-study page. There is NO separate LOCAL associate degree titled "Computer Science" (the only Computer Science degree is the AS-T), so local_cs_as_exists=false. Local (non-transfer) computing A.S. degrees offered by the STEM school: Computer Programming (A.S.), Data Science (A.S.), Cybersecurity (A.S.), and Simulation and Gaming: Game Art (A.S.). Information Technology is offered only as a Certificate, not a degree, and is therefore excluded. Numerous certificates exist as well (Computer Programming, Python Programming, Data Analytics, multiple Cybersecurity and IT tracks, Web Master Designer/Developer, Game Art) and are excluded per the definitions. Note: some third-party/aggregator search results referenced catalog.valleycollege.edu, which is San Bernardino Valley College (a different college); those were disregarded in favor of authoritative mvc.edu pages.

Per the official 2025-2026 Clovis Community College catalog (California, Fresno-area; not to be confused with the same-named New Mexico college at clovis.edu, which uses "Associate of Applied Science" terminology). LOCAL CS A.S.: YES — "Computer Science" (Major #C.6920.AS) is explicitly titled "ASSOCIATE IN SCIENCE DEGREE," a local A.S. Although its catalog description states a "Transfer Purpose," it is NOT an Associate in Science for Transfer / ADT. CS AS-T: NO — the college's complete list of Associate Degrees for Transfer (AS-T/AA-T) includes Chemistry, Geology, Physics, Mathematics, Biology, Environmental Science, etc., but contains no Computer Science AS-T. Other LOCAL (non-transfer) computing A.S. degrees: Data Science (C.6921.AS); Information Systems – Networking (C.693B.AS); Information Systems – Programming the Web (C.693C.AS); Information Systems – Web Design (C.693D.AS). Adjacent (excluded from the computing list as an engineering major): "Computer or Software Engineering" A.S. (C.3013.AS). Certificates (NOT degrees, excluded): Cyber Security (CA), Information Systems (CA), Information Technician Support (CA), and Certificate of Achievement versions of the three Information Systems concentrations. Source is the primary authoritative catalog PDF.

Compton College offers no standalone "Computer Science" program of any kind — there is no local A.S. in Computer Science and no Computer Science Associate in Science for Transfer (AS-T/ADT). The only local (non-transfer) computing associate degree is Computer Information Systems (A.S.). Related computing offerings are certificates, not degrees: "Computer Systems Applications" (Certificate of Achievement) and "Apple Apps Development: Swift" (Certificate of Completion) — both excluded from the degree list per rules. Compton's AS-T/AA-T transfer degrees include Mathematics, Physics, Economics, etc., but none in Computer Science or Computer Information Systems. The current college majors page and the 2022-2023 catalog's Degrees and Certificates table agree on these findings.

survey.summary: Verified against Madera Community College's official 2024-2025 catalog (PDF downloaded and text-extracted). Madera offers a LOCAL "Computer Science" ASSOCIATE IN SCIENCE DEGREE (MAJOR #M.6920.AS, catalog p.99) — so local_cs_as_exists = true. Its major code is .AS (not .AS-T) and it is NOT titled "for Transfer", though its stated purpose is to prepare students to transfer. There is NO Computer Science AS-T / ADT: the catalog's full list of Associate Degrees for Transfer (Admin of Justice, Ag Business, Ag Plant Science, Art History, Biology, Business Admin, Communication Studies, Early Childhood Ed, Economics, English, History, Kinesiology, Mathematics, Philosophy, Physics, Political Science, Psychology, Sociology, Spanish, Studio Arts, and others) contains no Computer Science or Computer Information Systems ADT — so ast_cs_exists = false. Other LOCAL computing A.S. degrees: "Information Systems, Information Technology Support Option" (M.6950.AS) and "Information Systems, Networking and Security" (M.6972.AS). Excluded (certificates, not degrees): Information Systems Certificate of Achievement (M.6930), Information Systems Networking and Security Certificate (M.6972.CA), and Information Technology Support Technician Certificate (M.6931.CA). No AA computing degrees found.

extraction.notes:
Source: EVC official catalog (catalog.evc.edu), 2025-2026, degree page https://catalog.evc.edu/degrees-certificates/computer-science/computer-science-associate-science-transfer/ (reached from the provided source URL). This is the correct CS-for-Transfer AS-T; it contains programming/CS courses (COMSC 041/042/075/076/077/080). Fetched cleanly via WebFetch, no browser needed.

Degree structure as printed (semester units):
- REQUIRED CORE (all required): COMSC 077 Introduction to Computer Systems (3), COMSC 080 Discrete Structures (3), PHYS 007A Calculus-Based General Physics I (4), PHYS 007B Calculus-Based General Physics II (4). OR-option: BIOL 004A (General Principles and Cell Biology) may substitute for PHYS 007B; BIOL 004A units were NOT printed on the page so I did not fabricate them (I list PHYS 007B, 4 units, as the printed default and note the BIOL 004A substitution here).
- COMPUTER SCIENCE SEQUENCE (choose ONE full 2-course sequence, 6 units): Option 1 = COMSC 041 Programming Concepts and Methodology I (3) + COMSC 042 Programming Concepts and Methodology II (3); OR Option 2 = COMSC 075 Computer Science I: Introduction to Program Structures (3) + COMSC 076 Computer Science II: Introduction to Data Structures (3). Modeled as choose_units min 6; the two valid sequences are the only allowed combinations (do not mix across options).
- CALCULUS SEQUENCE (choose ONE full sequence): Option 1 = MATH 066 Calculus I Late Transcendentals for STEM (4) + MATH 067 Calculus II Late Transcendentals for STEM (4) = 8 units; OR Option 2 = MATH 071 Calculus I With Analytic Geometry (5) + MATH 072 Calculus II With Analytic Geometry (5) = 10 units. Modeled as choose_units min 8.

Totals table as printed: Major Requirements = 23 units; General Education (Cal-GETC) = 34 units; Transferable Electives = as needed to complete 60 units; Total = 60 units. Note the printed 23-unit major figure is lower than the raw sum of listed major courses (~28-30) because major courses that also satisfy Cal-GETC areas are double-counted per standard ADT accounting. GE follows the Cal-GETC pattern (34 units); GE courses not enumerated per instructions. Minimum 2.0 GPA (some CSU majors may require higher).

extraction.notes:
Anchored strictly to LACC's own official catalog (lacc.edu domain, Los Angeles Community College District). WebFetch was 403-blocked on lacc.edu and the programmap.lacitycollege.edu program-mapper (bot-blocked), so I downloaded the official catalog PDFs via curl and parsed them with pdftotext. Used the CURRENT 2025-2026 complete catalog (https://www.lacc.edu/sites/lacc.edu/files/2025-08/Complete%20LACC%202025-26%20College%20Catalog_0.pdf) for BOTH degrees. NOTE: the older 2024-2025 catalog did NOT yet list the Computer Science AS-T (only the local A.A.); the AS-T (State Code 44987, Academic Plan C044987H) is new in 2025-2026, which is why the survey flagged transfer=true. Both CS degrees are in the CSISA (Computer Science / Computer Information Systems) department, pages 136-137 of the 2025-2026 catalog.

DEGREE 1 - Computer Science AS-T (degree_type ast), 32 major units, minimum 60 total, semester units. Header prints Academic Plan as 'C44987C' but the Programs Offered table prints 'C044987H' (44987 is the state code). Structure of the 32-unit major core (the two-column PDF placed the 'COMPLETE ONE OF THE FOLLOWING PROGRAMMING COURSES' sub-heading directly above CS 130/131/136, which is a layout artifact — those three are required core, and the programming choice is CS 113/116/119; the units only reconcile to 32 this way): (a) required CS core CS 130 + CS 131 + CS 136 = 9; (b) choose ONE programming course from CS 113 / CS 116 / CS 119 = 3; (c) 'COMPLETE TWO CALCULUS COURSES' = Calculus I (MATH 261 OR MATH 261S) AND MATH 262 = 10; (d) PHYSICS 101 required = 5; (e) 'COMPLETE ONE OF THE FOLLOWING BIOLOGY, CHEMISTRY OR PHYSICS COURSES' from PHYSICS 102 / BIOLOGY 006 / BIOLOGY 007 / CHEM 101 = 5. Total = 9+3+10+5+5 = 32. GE: catalog requires Cal-GETC (CalGETC), 34 semester units, C or better each; minimum 12 units in residence at LACCD.

DEGREE 2 - Computer Science A.A. (degree_type local_computing), 35-37 major units, minimum 60 total, semester units, Academic Plan C010774C, State Code 10774, TOP 070600. This is the closest LOCAL computing degree (there is no local CS A.S.; it is awarded as an A.A.). Major core (35-37): choose ONE programming (CS 116 C++ OR CS 113 Java) = 3; CS 130 = 3; CS 136 = 3; choose ONE discrete (CS 131 [3] OR MATH 272 [5]) = 3-5 (this is what creates the 35-37 range); choose ONE OOP (CS 213 Advanced Java OR CS 216 OOP in C++) = 3; MATH 261 = 5; MATH 262 = 5; PHYSICS 101 = 5; PHYSICS 102 = 5. GE: satisfied via LACC local GE pattern OR IGETC OR CSU GE-Breadth (student's choice); English and Math must be C or better; minimum 60 degree-applicable units, 2.0 GPA, 12 units at LACCD.

No course codes or units were fabricated; all transcribed verbatim from the PDF. Course titles for the AS-T's alternate calculus (MATH 261S 'Calculus I with Support') and science options are exactly as printed.

extraction.notes:
Source: official College of Marin 2024-2025 catalog PDF (https://www1.marin.edu/sites/default/files/Catalog-2024-2025-F.pdf), catalog pages 115-117 (PDF pages 118-119), section 'A.S. in Computer Science' under discipline 'Computer Science - COMP'. Extracted via pdftotext; the two-column layout interleaved text, so I re-extracted in raw reading order to confirm the full list (the 'PHYS207B - Electricity and Magnetism 5' line printed at the top of the page is the final item of the elective list, continuing from PHYS207A).

College of Marin uses the SEMESTER system. This is the ONLY Computer Science associate degree at the college (award: A.S. in Computer Science). The survey flags 'local CS A.S. = true' and 'closest local computing degree = Computer Science' both resolve to this single degree, so I emitted two records (local_cs_as and local_computing) with identical requirements by design; there is no separate second computing degree named Computer Science.

TOTAL UNITS: The catalog prints only 'Major Requirements (Total 27 - 32 Units)'; it does not print an explicit overall degree total. Per California minimums an associate degree is at least 60 semester units, so total_units is set to 60 (major requirements + general education + electives/graduation requirements). The 27-32 major-requirement range is captured in the group labels.

OR / choose options: (1) Core Programming - select ONE of COMP130 C++ / COMP135 Java / COMP138 Python (4 units). (2) Advanced Programming - select TWO of COMP232 / COMP235 / COMP220 (8 units). (3) Mathematics - MATH123 required (5 units). (4) Computer Science Electives - select THREE 'of the following or anything not used above' (10-15 units). Within the electives, COMP150 and ENGG150 are the SAME cross-listed MATLAB course (credit for only one); COMP117 and MATH117 are the SAME cross-listed Discrete Mathematics course (credit for only one) - both cross-listings are included in the group but are single course options, not duplicates.

GENERAL EDUCATION: Not enumerated. Courses carry local 'AA/AS Area E' etc. designations; the degree follows College of Marin's local associate-degree GE pattern (with CSU GE Breadth / IGETC (Cal-GETC) available for transfer students). Recorded as a single high-level ge_area group.

extraction.notes:
Authoritative source domain: catalog.collegeofsanmateo.edu (this exact college). Current published catalog is 2025-2026 at /current/; the 2026-2027 catalog is live at /2627/. Both were used.

LOCAL DEGREE (Computer Science Applications and Development, A.S.): Fully extracted VERBATIM from the 2026-2027 catalog page https://catalog.collegeofsanmateo.edu/2627/programs/computer-science-applications-and-development-as.php (identical program also present in /current/ 2025-2026). 60 semester units total; ~30-34 major units. Structure: (a) Required Core Courses = 18 units: CIS 110 (3), CIS 151 (3), CIS 254 (4), plus a CS1 choice [CIS 255 Java OR CIS 278 C++, 4 units] and a CS2 choice [CIS 256 Java OR CIS 279 C++, 4 units]; (b) Required Selective Courses Group 1 = Select 6-8 units from CIS 255/256/278/279/121/132/262/264/363/364; (c) Required Selective Courses Group 2 = Select 6-8 units from CIS 114/117/127/128/129/135/137/138/380. Note: an earlier read of the 2025-2026 page also showed CIS 133 (NoSQL Databases) in Group 1 and CIS 113 in Group 2; the explicit 2026-2027 verbatim transcription omitted those two, so exact Group-1/Group-2 membership has minor uncertainty (I used the 2026-2027 list). GE: catalog states "required General Education coursework and electives as needed to meet the minimum 60 units for the Associate degree per AA/AS Degree Requirements" — follows CSM local associate GE (with CSU GE / Cal-GETC options); individual GE courses intentionally not enumerated. This is a genuine CS program (all CIS programming courses), satisfying the CS-content check.

TRANSFER AS-T (Computer Science): CONFIRMED to exist but requirements NOT yet published anywhere fetchable. CSM's official "Associate Degrees for Transfer Offered at College of San Mateo" document (dated June 2026), https://collegeofsanmateo.edu/transfer/docs/AssociateDegreesForTransfer.pdf, lists "Computer Science AS-T" as "Available at CSM beginning 2026-2027." However: (1) the 2025-2026 catalog has no computer-science-as-t.php (HTTP 404); (2) a complete verbatim link inventory of the 2026-2027 /2627/programs/ index contains NO computer-science-as-t.php — the brand-new 2026-2027 AS-T degrees (Computer Science, Chemistry, Mathematics 2.0, Physics 2.0, Biology 2.0) have not had their catalog pages published yet, while older AS-T pages (e.g., administration-of-justice-as-t.php, biology-as-t.php) do exist; (3) assist.org returned only a JS shell ("Welcome to ASSIST"); (4) CSM's own ProgramMapper (san-mateo.programmapper.com) is JavaScript-only and its program list shows only the local CS programs, no CS AS-T. No CS AS-T major-course worksheet PDF was found. Therefore the AS-T's required courses could not be transcribed and were NOT fabricated. total_units=60 recorded for the AS-T is the statutory CA AS-T maximum (not a transcribed value). needs_browser=true: once CSM publishes the 2026-2027 CS AS-T catalog page / degree worksheet (or assist.org 2026-2027 articulation loads via a real browser), the major requirement groups (expected to follow the statewide Computer Science TMC: programming/data-structures + calculus sequence + physics/discrete math) should be re-extracted. No course codes were invented.

extraction.notes:
Both degrees extracted from the current 2026-2027 College of the Sequoias catalog (catalog.cos.edu), verified against the official CourseLeaf PDF program sheets (pdftotext -layout). Unit system is semester; both degrees total 60 units minimum, consistent with CA associate-degree rules.

CS AS-T (degree_type ast): "Associate in Science in Computer Science for Transfer (AS-T)", Math & Engineering division. Required Major Courses = 30 units, all required (CSCI 001, CSCI 002, CSCI 005, CSCI 006, MATH 065, MATH 066, PHYS 055, PHYS 056). Confirmed it contains real CS/programming courses (CSCI 001/002 = Programming Concepts/Method 1 & 2, CSCI 005 = Computer Architecture, CSCI 006 = Discrete Structures) — not the wrong math-only program. GE follows Cal-GETC (34 units). Asterisk note printed: "Course(s) meets General Education requirement and 7 units may be double counted above in Cal-GETC" — this applies to MATH 065 (Calculus 1) and PHYS 055 (Physics 1), which carry asterisks in the printed sheet, so the raw component total (30 major + 34 Cal-GETC + 3 electives = 67) nets to the stated 60 after ~7 double-counted units. Electives group: "Select additional transferable courses numbered 1-199 to achieve 60 units" (3 units).

CPIS (degree_type local_computing): "Associate of Science in Computer Programming & Information Systems (AS)", Business division, explicitly "not designed for transfer." GE = College of the Sequoias Associate Degree General Education (COS-GE), 25 units. Required Major Courses = 26 units, all required. OR option: the first requirement is COMP 005 Computer Concepts (4 units) OR COMP 130 Introduction to Personal Computers (COMP 130 carries the asterisk = meets GE requirement, 3 units may double count in COS-GE); I listed COMP 005 (the primary, with printed units) in the required group and note COMP 130 as its alternative here. Restricted Electives = "Select three units from" (printed range 3-4 units because some options are 4-unit courses): BUS 271 (3), COMP 233 (3), ICT 201 (4), ICT 210 (4). Electives = "Select additional degree applicable courses numbered 1-299 to achieve 60 units" (printed 8-9 units; recorded units_min 8). Required Major and Restricted Elective courses must be completed with a "C" or better.

No same-named out-of-district college was used; all data is from the College of the Sequoias (Visalia, CA) official catalog domain catalog.cos.edu. No course codes or units were fabricated; all values transcribed directly from the printed PDF program sheets.

extraction.notes:
Source: Butte College official 2026-2027 program pathway pages under butte.edu/academic-programs/pathways/2026-2027/ (the old programs.butte.edu ProgramInfo URLs now 301-redirect to these). Catalog year 2026-2027 (current, within allowed range). Butte is on the semester system; both degrees total 60 semester units minimum.

LOCAL_COMPUTING NOTE: Butte's closest local computing associate degree named "Computer Science" IS the local Computer Science A.S. itself (program 11138.00AS, "Computer Science AS Degree"). There is no separate local computing program distinct from the CS A.S., so degree_type local_computing = the same program already extracted here as local_cs_as (do not double-count; dedupe by catalog_url). Butte also offers a separate "Computer Programming AS" (31307-01as) but that is a different, distinct program not named Computer Science and was not requested.

Computer Science AS-T (ast, 31930.30AS-T): Standard CS Transfer Model Curriculum. Required major core (all): CSCI 20, CSCI 21, CSCI 22, CSCI 10, MATH C2220, PHYS 41, PHYS 42; plus Calculus I as a select-one between MATH C2210 (5) and its embedded-support version MATH C2210E (5) — same course, C2210E adds in-class embedded support, choose one. Major subtotal ~29 units. GE follows Cal-GETC (~30 units). One free elective may be needed to reach 60. Passes the CS-content check (CSCI 20/21/22/10 are programming/CS courses).

Computer Science A.S. local (local_cs_as, 11138.00AS): Required major core (all): CSCI 20, CSCI 21, CSCI 22, MATH C2220. Plus three select-one/select-two groups: (a) Calculus I select-one MATH C2210 (5) OR MATH C2210E embedded support (5); (b) Computer architecture select-one CSCI 10 (3) OR ENGR 11 Embedded Systems Development (3); (c) two lab-science courses selected from CHEM 1 (5), CHEM 2 (5), CHEM 51 (5), CHEM 52 (4), PHYS 41 (4), PHYS 42 (4) — the term-by-term pathway shows a science slot in Term 2 and another in Term 3, i.e. two science courses required (each 4-5 units). GE follows Butte's local associate-degree GE pattern; exact GE unit count not itemized on the pathway page (electives fill remaining units to reach 60). Passes CS-content check.

Confidence: unit values and course codes transcribed verbatim from the pathway tables and cross-checked across two fetches. The "select two sciences" count for the local A.S. is inferred from two distinct science slots in the pathway (Terms 2 and 3); exact GE-area unit totals were not enumerated (GE recorded high-level per instructions). Data was fully fetchable via WebFetch, so needs_browser=false.

MAJOR CORE: 7 required courses, all required (rule=all). The seven itemized units are 3+4+3+3+3+3+3 = 22 units. Includes IT C251 Introduction to Programming Concepts and Methodologies, a programming course, so the computing-content sanity check passes.

GENERAL EDUCATION: Not enumerated per instructions. The page states students "Complete one of the following general education patterns: Option A" (local/CSU-style GE) OR "Option B — Cal-GETC (California General Education Transfer Curriculum)." Recorded as a single ge_area group; this is the only OR option in the requirements — there are no OR course options within the major core itself.

Total degree units: 60.0 (semester units; CA associate minimum). Core (22) + GE + free electives reach the 60-unit minimum; the program page does not enumerate a separate restricted-elective list for the major, and a second targeted fetch explicitly found none beyond the 7 core courses. Confidence held at ~0.8 because the major total could not be independently cross-verified in the full catalog PDF (size-limited).

extraction.notes:
Source: live eLumen catalog, 2026-2027 Columbia College Catalog, exact program page https://gocolumbia.elumenapp.com/catalog/2026-2027-Catalog/program/computer-programming-as-cc-fall-2026 (reached from the official degrees-certificates listing). The eLumen catalog is fully JavaScript-rendered, so WebFetch returned only the word "Catalog"; I rendered it in a browser to transcribe the exact printed course codes, titles, and units. Cross-confirmed by the official gocolumbia.edu program advising guide (Google Doc linked from https://gocolumbia.edu/academics/programsofstudy/area_stem/computer_programming_and_info_systems.html), which matched the same courses and 21-22 major / 60 total unit structure.

Degree: "Computer Programming AS" (A.S. DEGREE). Catalog states 21.0-22.0 Major Units | 60 Total Units Required. This is a local (non-transfer) computing degree; it correctly contains programming/CS (COMP) courses.

Structure as printed:
- Required Courses (8 units): Programming I (4 units) = choose one of COMP11J (Java) OR COMP11P (Python); Programming II (4 units) = choose one of COMP12J (Java) OR COMP12P (Python).
- Electives (13-14 units) split into two subgroups:
  * Math Electives (4-5 units): choose from MATH16 Precalculus (5), MATHC2210 Calculus I (4), MATHC2220 Calculus II (4), MATH18C Calculus III (5), STATC1000 Intro to Statistics (4).
  * Computer/Other Electives (9 units): choose from COMP1 (4), COMP10 (3), COMP40 (2), COMP41 (2), COMP42 (2), COMP50 (3), COMP61 (3), PHYCS5A (4). The catalog also allows "Any courses not already used from the Math Electives Group (0-9 units)" to count toward this 9-unit elective block.

General Education: to earn the degree students complete the major requirements (C/P or better in each course) PLUS one of two full GE patterns: (1) Columbia College General Education (CC-GE) Pattern, or (2) California General Education Transfer Curriculum (Cal-GETC) Pattern. Per instructions I did not enumerate GE courses; recorded as a single ge_area group.

Course code formatting: catalog concatenates prefix+number without a space (e.g., "COMP11J", "MATHC2210", "STATC1000", "PHYCS5A"); I split them into prefix/number. Note MATH and STAT "C####" and the "C" in some codes are as printed (Common Course Numbering).

Survey flags confirmed consistent: no standalone local CS A.S. and no CS AS-T at this college. The program's STEM page does list a separate "Computer Programming & Information Systems" A.A.-T transfer degree, but that was outside the requested scope (only the local computing degree was requested), so it was not extracted.

Source: Merritt College 2024-2025 Catalog with Supplement (official, HubSpot-hosted PDF). Computer Science program appears on catalog pp. 215-217. Text extracted cleanly with pdftotext -layout; high confidence.

DEGREE COINCIDENCE: Merritt offers exactly ONE computing associate degree — the "Computer Science - A.S. Degree" (Peralta CCD, semester units). There is no AS-T in CS (confirmed: only Economics, Mathematics, Nutrition/Dietetics AS-T listed; the "Computer Science and Information Systems" offering is a Career Readiness Certificate, not an associate degree). Therefore the LOCAL Computer Science A.S. (local_cs_as) and the closest LOCAL computing degree named Computer Science (local_computing) are the SAME degree. It is reported once, tagged local_cs_as, to avoid fabricating a distinct row; the local_computing category resolves to this identical degree.

DEGREE STRUCTURE (as printed):
- Required Courses: 31.0-32.0 units = "Core Courses" (27 units, all required) + "Choose One" (1 of CIS 006 5u OR CIS 007 4u = 4-5 units).
- OR option: "Choose One" is CIS 006 Introduction to Computer Programming (5) OR CIS 007 Control Structures and Objects (4).
- OPTIONAL Major Electives for 12-17 units: "Select ONE group of OPTIONAL Major Elective courses" from Groups A-F. These are optional and fill the degree's elective bucket (they are not additional required units beyond the 31-32 major requirement units). Captured as electives-rule groups A-F for completeness. CIS 178 appears in both Group B and Group D as printed (not a dedupe error within a single group).
- General Education: follows Local AA/AS GE pattern; printed as "General Education (Local AA/AS) Units (includes 2-4 double-counted units to achieve required minimum of 18) = 15-17". Individual GE courses not enumerated per instructions.
- Elective Units (degree-applicable): 12-17.
- Total Degree Units: printed as 60-68 (semester). total_units recorded as 60 (CA associate minimum / floor of printed range 60-68).

catalog_url is the exact authoritative PDF used for extraction.

extraction.notes:
Source: Santa Ana College 2025-2026 Catalog PDF (https://sac.edu/admissions/catalogarchive/SAC_Catalog_2025-2026.pdf), catalog pages 119-121 (Computer Science section). The main catalog PDF is compressed and unreadable via WebFetch; extracted successfully with pdftotext -layout. Unit system = semester. The catalog uses a two-column layout.

DEGREE IDENTITIES: SAC offers a degree literally named "Computer Science" both as a local A.S. and as an AS-T. Therefore local_cs_as and local_computing map to the SAME degree (Computer Science: A.S. Degree, Program Code SAC.CMPR.AS, PCN 04197) — I entered it under both degree_type values as instructed ("extract each that exists"); they are identical, not two separate programs. The AS-T is Computer Science: A.S.-T Degree for Transfer (SAC.CMPR.AST, PCN 32396).

TOTAL UNITS: total_units is reported as 60 (the CA associate-degree / ADT minimum degree total incl. GE + electives). The catalog's printed "Total" figures are MAJOR-only: AS-T major = 29.0 units; local A.S. major = 25.0-27.0 units. AS-T explicitly requires 60 CSU-transferable semester units.

AS-T (SAC.CMPR.AST) — major is all 8 courses required (no choose-one options): CMPR 121 (3.0), CMPR 131 (3.0), CMPR 129 (4.0), CMPR 140 (3.0), MATH 180 (4.0), MATH 185 (4.0), PHYS 217 (4.0), PHYS 227 (4.0) = 29.0. GE = Cal-GETC (CSU GE or IGETC may be used in some cases); min 2.0 GPA; grade C/P in each major course; min 18 units in major.

LOCAL A.S. (SAC.CMPR.AS) — 3 major groups: (1) "Take ALL" core 16.0 units: CMPR 100, 120, 121, 129, 131; (2) "Select ONE" 3.0 units: CMPR 112 / CMPR 205 / CMPR 213; (3) "Select an additional SIX units" 6.0-8.0: choose from a 17-course CMPR/MATH list. Note CMPR 112, 205, 213 appear in BOTH group 2 and group 3 in the catalog (a course used to satisfy the choose-one cannot double-count). GE follows SAC local associate-degree GE pattern (Plan A/B/C) plus competency/electives to reach the 60-unit minimum.

Both degrees contain genuine CMPR programming courses (Programming Concepts, Data Structures, etc.), confirming these are the correct Computer Science programs and not a math/physics-only mis-grab. Not to be confused with the separate Computer Information Systems: A.S. (SAC.CIS.AS) or the Computer Science: Certificate of Achievement (SAC.CMPR.CA), which are different programs on adjacent pages.

extraction.notes:
Cuesta College is a SEMESTER-system CCC (San Luis Obispo County). Associate degrees require a minimum of 60 degree-applicable semester units (min 2.00 GPA, min 12 units at Cuesta) per the catalog degree-requirements page.

DEGREE COUNT: There is exactly ONE Computer Science associate degree at Cuesta — "Computer Science, Associate in Science" (A.S.). The earlier survey's "closest local computing degree named Computer Science" is this SAME degree; no separate computing program named Computer Science exists. It is therefore reported once with degree_type=local_cs_as. If a local_computing record is needed downstream, it is identical to this local_cs_as entry (same courses, same units, same catalog page). AS-T in CS = false (confirmed: no Computer Science Transfer degree appears in the all-degrees list; Cuesta offers Computer Science as a local A.S. only).

SOURCE: Extracted from the fetchable official Cuesta CurricUNET program report (reportId=440, entity 2114), catalog year 2025-2026, which lists the exact major courses and units. The programmap.cuesta.edu program page returned HTTP 403 (bot-blocked) but was not needed. Anti-error checks passed: this is the Cuesta (id 16) OWN catalog domain (cuesta.edu / cuesta.curriqunet.com); the extracted core contains genuine CS/programming courses (CS 201/231/232/233/240/241), not just math+physics.

MAJOR STRUCTURE (37 units total): (a) CS core — all 6 courses required; (b) Science requirement — choose ONE two-course lab-science sequence: EITHER CHEM 201A + CHEM 201B (5+5=10) OR PHYS 208A + PHYS 208B (5+5=10); (c) Mathematics — both MATH 265A + MATH 265B (Calculus I & II) required (10 units). I modeled the science choice as a choose_units group (units_min 10) listing all four course options because it is a pick-one-sequence, not a pick-one-course; see the OR pairing above.

GENERAL EDUCATION: The CurricUNET report only enumerates the 37 major units and does not itemize GE. Per Cuesta's catalog, a local A.S. is completed by adding a GE pattern (Cuesta local GE / CSU GE / Cal-GETC) plus electives to reach the 60-unit minimum. I recorded GE as a high-level ge_area group (not enumerated) rather than listing individual GE courses, per instructions.

total_units set to 60 (degree minimum; the specific catalog page does not print an exact program total, and the 37 major units plus GE/electives round to the 60-unit associate-degree minimum). No course codes or units were fabricated; all major courses/units are exactly as printed in the CurricUNET report.

Source: https://laspositascollege.edu/catalog/2025-2026/cs/computerscience-as.php (Las Positas College's own official catalog domain, 2025-2026 current catalog). Data verified against the raw page HTML, not just an LLM summary.

Only ONE Computer Science associate degree exists at Las Positas College: "Computer Science Associate of Science (AS)". The survey's two roles both point to it — the local CS A.S. (local_cs_as) IS the closest local computing degree named "Computer Science." There is no separate/second computing associate degree, so it is returned once (as local_cs_as) to avoid duplication rather than as two identical records. The department also offers Certificates of Achievement (AI, Computer Programming, Computer Programming for the Web, Management Information Systems, Red Hat Administration) but those are certificates, not associate degrees, so they are excluded.

Contra Costa College (CCC), San Pablo, CA — Contra Costa Community College District. Confirmed anchored to THIS college's own official 2025-2026 catalog (ccc.elumenapp.com) and official program mapper (contra-costa.programmapper.com); not confused with any same-named college elsewhere.

Only ONE Computer Science associate degree exists at CCC: "Computer Science Associate in Science Degree (Credit)". The earlier survey's two slots — local CS A.S. and "closest local computing degree = Computer Science" — refer to the SAME single degree. I therefore returned the same degree twice (degree_type local_cs_as and local_computing) with identical requirements; downstream should treat them as one program, not two. There is no separate/second computing associate named Computer Science.

MAJOR REQUIREMENTS (authoritative, from the eLumen catalog program page, header states "20 Credits"): heading printed as "Computer Science - A.S. Degree / Required courses, with suggested sequence:" — all 5 courses required, each 4 units: COMP 112 Introduction to Programming; COMP 165 Advanced Programming With C and C++; COMP 200 Object Oriented Programming C++; COMP 210 Program Design and Data Structures; COMP 265 Assembly Language Programming/Computer Organization. These are genuine programming/CS courses (rule 4 satisfied). No "choose one"/elective options within the major — it is a fixed all-required 20-unit block. Cross-checked and identical to the 2025-2026 program-mapper "MAJ" courses (COMP-112/165/200/210/265).

TOTAL UNITS: The catalog program page prints only the 20-unit MAJOR block; it does not print a single degree grand-total. Per CCC graduation rules the associate degree is a minimum of 60 semester units (major 20 + General Education + electives). I recorded total_units = 60 (the degree minimum). The program mapper's sample-plan range was 55.5–66.0 units depending on GE course selections.

GENERAL EDUCATION: Not enumerated per instructions. Degree page says "complete all general education requirements as listed in the catalog." CCC uses its local Associate in Science GE pattern (see https://www.contracosta.edu/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/Local_GE_2025-2026.pdf) / Cal-GETC; program mapper shows CCC GE Areas A1, A2, B, C, D, F plus CSU/UC graduation requirements. Recorded as a high-level ge_area group.

SOURCING/BROWSER: WebFetch could NOT reach the sources — the eLumen catalog is a JavaScript SPA (returns only "Catalog") and programmapper returned HTTP 403 to plain fetch. I used the headless browser pane to render both and fully transcribed the degree, so needs_browser=false (data is complete). A future plain-HTTP re-fetch of these exact sources WOULD require a browser. Nothing fabricated; all course codes, titles, and units transcribed exactly as printed. Grade rule: each major course must be "C" or higher.

Core (30) + Cal-GETC (34) exceeds 60 because major courses double-count toward Cal-GETC with counselor approval (catalog notes major courses "can be double counted for Cal-GETC"). Core contains genuine CS/programming courses (CS 007A Computer Science I, CS 007B Computer Science II, CS 008 Computer Architecture and Organization) plus the Calculus/Discrete Math/Engineering Physics sequence — confirmed correct CS program, not a mis-grabbed math/physics-only program. Completion standards: min 2.0 GPA, grade of C in each major course, all units CSU-transferable.

CIS (Transfer Preparation) local_computing: Exact printed title "Computer Information Systems AS Degree (transfer preparation)". Structure: Required Courses (subtotal 25 units) + Cal-GETC (34) + Transferable Electives as needed = DEGREE TOTAL 60. Prepares transfer to CSU San Bernardino's Information Systems & Technology (IST) BS. OR-option: the introductory programming requirement is CIS 087 (Introduction to Programming Using Python, 3 units) OR CIS 088 (Introduction to Programming Using Java, 3 units) — modeled as a separate choose_courses group (choose 1). CIS 088's 3-unit value confirmed from the CIS course catalog page (it was printed on the degree sheet as an alternative without its own unit column). BUMA 003 (Business Statistics) and BUMA 020A (Business Law I) are the non-CIS required courses.

General Education for both degrees follows the Cal-GETC pattern (34 units) — recorded as a high-level ge_area group rather than enumerating individual GE courses, per instructions.

extraction.notes:
Anchored to Mission College (Santa Clara, CA) only, via its official site missioncollege.edu and its eLumen catalog (mission.elumenapp.com), catalog year 2024-2025 (current). Both target degrees exist and were extracted.

SOURCE/METHOD: The eLumen catalog is a JavaScript-only Angular SPA — direct WebFetch of every program URL returned only the word "Catalog," and the legacy static site (majors.missioncollege.edu) now 302-redirects into the same SPA. I rendered the two program pages through a JS-executing reader proxy (r.jina.ai) to transcribe them. The CS AS-T page was rendered twice with consistent results. Because I successfully transcribed both degrees, needs_browser is false, but a rendered-catalog re-verification of GE and the exact degree total would be a reasonable double-check.

UNITS / TOTALS: The eLumen program pages list MAJOR requirement units only — CS AS-T "Total 35-36" and CIS AS "Total 20-22." California associate degrees require a minimum of 60 semester units, with the remainder made up of General Education plus free electives; I set total_units = 60 for both to represent the degree total (not fabricated — it is the Title 5 minimum and the CS AS-T source explicitly notes "60+ total CSU-transferable units required"). GE is not enumerated on the program pages.

CS AS-T (degree_type ast): Core contains programming/CS courses (CIS039 Computer Systems & Assembly, CIS043 Software Development with Java, CIS044 Data Structures) plus MAT020, MAT003B, PHY004A — passes the "must contain CS/programming" check. Two OR-option groups: (1) Calculus I = MAT003A OR MAT003AH; (2) science-with-lab = one of BIO001A / BIO001AH / CHM001A / CHM001AH / PHY004B (units 4-5, which is why the major total is a 35-36 range). GE follows Cal-GETC or IGETC.

CIS AS (degree_type local_computing): This is the closest local computing associate degree (Mission does not offer a local CS A.S.). Core = all of CIS007 Python, CIS043 Java, CIS044 Data Structures (12 units). Electives = choose 8-10 units from the nine listed courses. WRK300GW General Work Experience is a variable 1-2 units (recorded as units_seen 2). Major total 20-22 units; GE (Mission local/CSU GE) fills the balance to 60.

CAVEATS: Course titles recorded as rendered; CIS044's title was truncated to "Intro to Data Structures Using" on the CIS AS render but is fully "Introduction to Data Structures Using Java" per the CS AS-T page (used the full title). No course codes or units were fabricated; where a range exists it is noted here.

Authoritative sources: CCSF's official CurricuNet program-outline reports, which are exactly what the CURRENT 2025-26 catalog department page (https://www.ccsf.edu/academics/ccsf-catalog/courses-by-department/computer-science) links to for each degree. The public catalog page and the direct www.ccsf.edu URL both 403 via WebFetch; I retrieved the CurricuNet reports (report 1472 = Computer Science AS, report 1849 = Computer Science AS-T) and parsed the full course tables. All course codes/titles/units are transcribed exactly as printed; nothing fabricated. unit_system = semester; CCSF associate degrees require a minimum of 60 degree-applicable semester units (GE + major), so total_units is reported as 60 for both (major-only unit counts: local AS = 25.0, AS-T = 33.0).

Degree identity: The survey's "closest local computing degree named Computer Science" is the SAME program as the local Computer Science A.S. (CurricuNet program 1472). There is no separate local_computing degree, so it is NOT duplicated — only two distinct degrees exist: the local Computer Science A.S. and the Computer Science AS-T.

Local Computer Science A.S. (program 1472, "Computer Science, AS: Associate of Science (AS)", approved Oct 2021 / effective Fall 2022, active in the current 2025-26 catalog): Major = 25.0 units. (a) Programming-fundamentals requirement — choose ONE: CS 110B Programming Fundamentals: C++ (4.0) OR CS 111B Programming Fundamentals: Java (4.0). (b) Data-structures requirement — choose ONE: CS 110C Data Structures and Algorithms: C++ (4.0) OR CS 111C Data Structures and Algorithms: Java (4.0). (c) CS 270 Computer Architecture with Assembly Language (4.0). (d) Required math (all): MATH 110A Calculus I (5.0), MATH 110B Calculus II (5.0), MATH 115 Discrete Mathematics (3.0). IMPORTANT: the report explicitly lists PHYC 4A/4AL/4B/4BL and MATH 110C as "NOT required for the AS degree" (transfer-prep examples only) — I excluded them from the major. Students are advised to pick a matched programming series (110B+110C or 111B+111C). Recommended prep: MATH 60/Algebra, ENGL 88/ESL 188/college-English readiness, CS 10/CS 110A.

Computer Science AS-T (program 1849, "Computer Science, AST: AS-T", approved March 2024 / effective Fall 2025): Major = 33.0 units. (a) Choose a C++ focus OR a Java focus (8.0 units): C++ focus = CS 110B (4.0) AND CS 110C (4.0); Java focus = CS 111B (4.0) AND CS 111C (4.0). The two courses must be a matched pair (both C++ or both Java) — modeled as choose_courses choose_n=2 over the four listed courses. (b) All required: CS 270 Computer Architecture with Assembly Language (4.0), MATH 110A Calculus I (5.0), MATH 110B Calculus II (5.0), MATH 115 Discrete Mathematics (3.0), PHYC 4A Classical Mechanics for Scientists and Engineers (3.0), PHYC 4AL Mechanics Laboratory (1.0), PHYC 4B Electromagnetism for Scientists and Engineers (3.0), PHYC 4BL Electromagnetism Laboratory (1.0). Physics IS required for the AS-T (unlike the local AS). GE follows the Cal-GETC pattern; courses used for the major may also count toward Cal-GETC. Students with no programming background are advised to take CS 110A before CS 110B/111B.

Some course status tags appear as "(Historical)" in the reports (e.g., MATH 110A/110B/115, and PHYC 4A/4AL for the AS-T) reflecting course-version records in CurricuNet, but they remain the courses named in each degree's requirement list; titles/numbers/units recorded as printed. General education for both degrees is not enumerated per catalog instruction (recorded as a ge_area group). Local AS follows CCSF GE (or Cal-GETC); AS-T follows Cal-GETC.

IMPORTANT — both degree_type records refer to the SAME single degree. Fresno City College has exactly ONE Computer Science associate degree: "Computer Science, Associate of Science - Major #2790" (F.2790.AS).
