Catalog-printed grand total for the MAJOR = 29-30 units. total_units is reported as 60 because that is the FCC associate-degree minimum (major requirements + general education + electives to reach 60 degree-applicable semester units); the program page itself lists only the 29-30 major units.

General Education: not enumerated on the program page. FCC associate degrees require completing one of three GE patterns — FCC local GE, CSU GE Breadth, or Cal-GETC (formerly IGETC) — captured as a high-level ge_area group. A grade of "C" or better is required in each major course.

Course numbers/units are identical in both.

This degree is genuinely a computing degree: the six required courses are IT/networking/OS/security (CompTIA A+, Network+, Security+, Microsoft client/server administration) plus a project-management course — no math/physics-only mistake. The identically named Certificate of Achievement (same six required courses; "six classes") also exists but was not requested. college_name normalized to "Lake Tahoe Community College" (Program Pathways Mapper renders it "Lake Tahoe College").

Source is the official Cabrillo College eLumen catalog, 2025-2026 (cabrillo.elumenapp.com). The catalog is a JavaScript-only SPA — plain WebFetch returned only a "Catalog" shell, so I rendered it in the browser and transcribed the program page plus each individual course page for exact units. needs_browser=false because full extraction succeeded, but note the source cannot be scraped by a non-JS fetch pipeline.

ONE degree only: Cabrillo has exactly one associate degree literally named "Computer Science" — "Computer Science - Associate in Science (A.S.)" in the Computer Science (CS) department. The 30-unit major is presented as TWO alternative pathways within the same degree:
(1) Default CS pathway = four subgroups totaling 30 units: [Core 14 units, all required: CS21 + MATH5A + MATH5B] + [4 units, choose ONE programming language: CS19 C++ OR CS20J Java OR CS20P Python] + [4 units, choose ONE discrete math: CS23 OR MATH23 — these are cross-listed equivalents] + [8 units of Recommended Major Electives chosen from CHEM1A/1B, CS24, MATH5C/6/7, PHYS4A/4B/4C, BIO9A/9B].

I captured this as a choose_units(30) group labeled "Computer Engineering Pathway".

OR options: CS19/CS20J/CS20P (pick one language); CS23/MATH23 (pick one, equivalent cross-listed Discrete Mathematics). Every per-course unit value was verified directly from that course's own 2025-2026 catalog page. Note MATH6 and MATH7 are 3.0 units (not 4). Anchored strictly to Los Angeles Valley College (lavc.edu); I deliberately did NOT use catalog.valleycollege.edu results that appeared in search — that domain is San Bernardino Valley College, a different, same-named college.

IMPORTANT — only TWO distinct CS associate degrees exist at LAVC: (1) Computer Science Associate of Science (AS), local, State Code 02952, Program Code 070600, Major Units 33; (2) Computer Science Associate in Science for Transfer (AS-T), State Code 44542, Program Code 070600, Major Units 32. The survey's "closest local computing degree named Computer Science" is IDENTICAL to the local CS A.S. — same physical program (State Code 02952). I therefore report the local Computer Science AS twice: once as degree_type local_cs_as and once as degree_type local_computing, with identical requirements. Do not count them as two separate programs.

Ground truth for major-unit totals is the PROGRAMS OFFERED table on catalog p.135; both degrees' course lists arithmetic-check against it (AS-T: 27 core + 5 choose-1 = 32; local AS: 18 required + 3 choose-1 + 12 choose-units = 33).

Then "COMPLETE 12 UNITS FROM THE FOLLOWING" = CIS 112, CIS 210, CS 111, CS 115, CS 119, CS 130, CS 213, CS 220 (choose 12 units). Note CS 111 and CS 115 appear in BOTH the choose-1 group and the choose-12-units group (they are distinct groups in the catalog; not a dedup error). - AS-T: "REQUIRED CORE: COMPLETE 7 COURSES" (all) = CS 113, CS 130, CS 131, CS 136, MATH 261, MATH 262, PHYSICS 037. Then "COMPLETE 1 COURSE FROM THE FOLLOWING" = BIOLOGY 006 OR BIOLOGY 007 OR CHEM 101 OR PHYSICS 038 (5 units). GE follows Cal-GETC (34 semester units). Minimum 18 major units required by Title 5; LAVC's listed major = 32 units. Total minimum 60 semester units. A "C" or higher required in each course.

Units shown in parentheses in the catalog (e.g. (3), (5)) denote courses inside choose-from groups; I recorded the numeric value. Unit system = semester. No course codes or units were fabricated; all transcribed verbatim from the catalog PDF.

Source: official San Diego Community College District 2024-2025 San Diego Miramar College Catalog PDF (https://www.sdccd.edu/docs/StudentServices/catalogs/2024-2025/Miramar_2024-2025_Catalog.pdf). WebFetch could not parse the compressed PDF, so I downloaded it and ran pdftotext; both degrees appear on printed/physical page 195 in a two-column layout. I verified the course lists with a clean single-column re-extraction of page 195, so the OR/choose options are reliable. Unit system = semester (catalog states "60 CSU-transferable semester units"). SDCCD is a semester-unit district.

DEGREE 1 — Computer Science for Transfer (AS-T, degree_type=ast): Printed heading "Associate in Science in Computer Science for Transfer Degree." Major = "Courses Required for the Major" (Total Units = 33), and ALL eight courses are required with no in-major choices: CISC 190 (4), CISC 191 (4), CISC 211 (4), CISC 246 (3), BIOL 210B (4), MATH 150 (5), MATH 151 (4), PHYS 195 (5). GE: student completes CSU GE-Breadth OR IGETC (catalog says "Certified completion of the California State University General Education-Breadth pattern (CSU GE)... OR the Intersegmental General Education Transfer Curriculum pattern (IGETC)"). Degree total = 60 CSU-transferable units (major 33 + GE + electives to 60). "This degree is accepted by some but not all CSU campuses."

DEGREE 2 — Computer and Information Sciences (local A.S., degree_type=local_computing): Printed "Associate of Science Degree: Computer and Information Sciences" (Total Units = 32 for the major). This is a business/information-systems flavored computing degree but it does contain programming/CS content (CISC 181 + a required 8 units of programming), so it is the correct local computing degree, not a mis-grab. Structure: (a) required core = CISC 181 (4), ACCT 116A (4), ACCT 116B (4), BUSE 119 (3), BUSE 140 (3), ECON 120 (3); (b) statistics requirement CHOOSE ONE: BUSE 115 Statistics for Business (3) OR MATH 119 Elementary Statistics (3); (c) "Select 8 units from the following": CISC 179 (4), CISC 186 (4), CISC 190 (4), CISC 192 (4). Then local graduation/GE requirements ("see the Requirements for the Associate Degree on page 94") plus "Electives as needed to meet minimum of 60 units required for the degree." Degree total = 60 semester units minimum. total_units recorded as 60 (full degree) for both degrees per the CA 60-unit minimum; the printed major subtotals are 33 (AS-T) and 32 (local).

Note: This department also offers a "Computer Programming" Certificate of Performance (CISC 186/190/192, 12 units) and a "Computer and Information Sciences" Certificate of Achievement (32 units) — not degrees, so not extracted. No fabricated codes/units; everything transcribed verbatim from the 2024-2025 catalog.

Source: official Los Angeles Mission College Catalog 2025-2026 (current; no 2026-2027 catalog published yet — probed URLs return 404). Computer Programmer AS extracted from catalog p.72 (https://www.lamission.edu/sites/lamc.edu/files/2025-05/LAMC-Catalog-2025-2026.pdf), downloaded via curl and converted with pdftotext -layout. Also corroborated by the LAMC department page https://www.lamission.edu/academics/aos/computer-programmer (same course ladder).

COMPUTER PROGRAMMER AS (M036322C): Catalog heading is a single "REQUIRED CORE COURSES ... Total 30" block. I split it into three groups for accurate unit accounting: 8 fixed all-required courses (24u) plus two embedded either/or slots — CS 113 Programming in Java OR CS 116 Programming in C++, and CS 213 Advanced Programming in Java OR CS 216 Object-Oriented Programming in C++ (3u each). Major core total as printed = 30 units. Degree NOTE: "A minimum of 60 units and a cumulative GPA of 2.0 ... Major courses must each be completed with a grade of C or better." So total_units = 60 (degree minimum; 30 major core + LAMC GE/Cal-GETC + electives to 60). CS 101 title is printed in the catalog as "Introduction to Computers Science" (catalog typo; course is Introduction to Computer Science). Courses marked with a dagger in the catalog (CS 130, CS 136, CS 213, CS 216) have a prerequisite/corequisite. This is a stacked award: a Skills Certificate in Programming and a Certificate of Achievement in Computer Programmer feed into this AS.

COMPUTER SCIENCE AS-T (ast) — DOES NOT EXIST at this college. The earlier survey's flag "transfer AS-T in CS = true" is NOT supported by the current catalog and appears incorrect. Evidence: (1) the catalog's complete Associate Degree for Transfer list contains no Computer Science entry — the AS-T/AA-T majors are Admin of Justice, Business Administration 2.0, Film/TV/Electronic Media, Art History, Studio Arts, Theatre Arts, Communication Studies 2.0, English, Philosophy, Political Science, Psychology, Social Justice Studies, Sociology, Spanish, Early Childhood Education, Elementary Teacher Education, Biology, Kinesiology, Mathematics, Nutrition & Dietetics, Public Health; (2) the two UC Transfer Pathway (UCTP) associate degrees are Chemistry and Physics only; (3) the Program Completion award matrix on catalog p.215 shows the row 'Computer Programmer' with dots only in the AS and Certificate columns (no AS-T), and there is no 'Computer Science' program row at all. This is consistent with California having no approved statewide Transfer Model Curriculum / ADT for Computer Science. No AST degree was fabricated; only the local_computing degree that actually exists is returned.

Both degrees transcribed verbatim from Ohlone College's own official curriculum guides in the /current/ folder (catalog year printed on both PDFs is 2026-2027). WebFetch was 403-blocked by the site WAF; downloaded via curl with a browser User-Agent, then extracted with pdftotext -layout.

CS FOR TRANSFER (AS-T / ADT): Degree title as printed "Associate in Science in Computer Science for Transfer (ADT)". GE follows Cal-GETC; full degree requires 60 CSU-transferable semester units at 2.0 GPA, min 12 units at Ohlone. total_units=60 is the degree total (33 of which is the enumerated major). DATABASE ADMINISTRATION (local A.S., degree_type=local_computing): Degree title as printed "Associate in Science in Database Administration". Structured as MAJOR FIELD (19.0 units, all required) + SUPPORTING COURSES (complete a minimum of 5 units from a 5-course list) + ELECTIVES (complete a minimum of 4 units from a 5-course list). The guide's stated "Total Required Units" for these three sections is 28.0–34.0; the full degree additionally requires Ohlone College OR Cal-GETC GE and at least 60 degree-applicable units total with 2.0 GPA and min 12 units at Ohlone, so total_units=60 reflects the degree total. Supporting/electives are choose-min-units lists (modeled as choose_units with units_min), not fixed course sets.

Note: a separate computer_science_as.pdf also exists in the /current/ folder (a local CS pathway) despite the survey flag local_cs_as=false; it was not requested and not extracted. All course prefixes, numbers, titles, and units are transcribed exactly as printed; nothing fabricated.

DEGREE MAPPING: The survey's three requested degree_types collapse to TWO actual degrees at MJC:
- local_cs_as = ast? No. local_cs_as = "Computer Science A.S. Degree" (local, non-transfer, slug computer-science-mjc-fall-2026).
- ast = "Computer Science for Transfer Degree / AS-T" (slug computer-science-for-transfer-degree-mjc-fall-2025).

(MJC also has a distinct "Computer Information Systems A.S." and a "Computer Electronics A.S.", but neither is named "Computer Science.")

LOCAL CS A.S. structure: Major = 19-21 units. Catalog heading is "REQUIRED OPTION - COMPLETE ONE OPTION FROM THE LIST BELOW FOR 19-21 UNITS" — the student picks exactly ONE of three options: (1) INFORMATION SYSTEMS OPTION (21 u), (2) NETWORKING OPTION (19 u), or (3) PROGRAMMING OPTION (21 u). Each option = a Required Core (all courses required) + 9 units of Electives chosen with a faculty advisor. I encoded all three options as six groups, prefixing each label_seen with its option name. The degree page prints only major units (19-21); total_units=60 is the MJC Associate Degree Requirement minimum (60 semester units incl. GE), not separately printed on the program page. GE follows MJC's Associate Degree Requirements 2026-2027 (MJC GE Pattern or Cal-GETC). NOTE: the catalog's PROGRAMMING OPTION core heading reads "REQUIRED CORE - COMPLETE 21 UNITS" but lists 4 courses = 12 units (21 is the option total incl. 9 elective units); a minor catalog typo. Elective headings contain a typo ("DISCUSSS"). All courses verified to contain programming/CS content (CSCI prefix).

AS-T structure: Major = 28-29 units, 60 total. REQUIRED CORE (24 u, all required): CSCI204/271/272/273 + MATH171/172 + PHYS101. Second REQUIRED CORE block (4-5 u): choose ONE lab science from BIO101 (4), CHEM101 (5), or PHYS103 (4) — encoded as choose_units, units_min 4 (range 4-5). GE = Cal-GETC (34 units). Follows the statewide AS-T in Computer Science TMC.

Source: MSJC official catalog (catalog.msjc.edu), 2026-2027 edition. Both degrees under the Computer Information Systems program. Unit system is semester; both degrees total 60 semester units.

CS A.S.-T (ast): The catalog "Required Core" is 28 major units and DOES contain CS/programming courses (CSIS-113A/113B, CSIS-118B, CSIS-211, CSIS-213), satisfying the anti-error CS-content check. Resolved an ambiguity via a targeted re-fetch: PHY-201 Mechanics and Wave Motion (4) is REQUIRED on its own, and there is a SEPARATE "select one" science group (PHY-202 or BIOL-150 or BIOL-150H, 4 units). Programming is "select one of CSIS-113A / CSIS-113B" (3). The two Calculus courses each have an Honors alternative printed as "MATH-C2210 or MATH-C2210H" and "MATH-C2220 or MATH-C2220H" — I listed the base course; the Honors section is an equivalent substitute, not an additional requirement. GE follows the Cal-GETC pattern (34 units) with 6-9 units possible double-counting; minimum 2.0 GPA and C-or-better in all major courses required. Remaining units are transferable electives to reach 60.

CIS, A.S. (local_computing): This local degree = 27 units MSJC Local General Education + ONE of four 18-unit emphasis areas + electives to reach 60. IMPORTANT: the student selects only ONE emphasis; the four emphasis groups below (Artificial Intelligence, Internet Authoring, Networking, Programming) are MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE alternatives, not all-required. Internet Authoring has two internal "select one" choices (intro course CSIS-103 or CSIS-111B; and a programming language CSIS-113B / CSIS-113C / CSIS-116E) plus four required courses. Programming Emphasis requires CSIS-118B + CSIS-201, PLUS one complete 2-course/6-unit programming-language sequence — matched Level 1 + Level 2 pair: (CSIS-113A+CSIS-123A) OR (CSIS-113B+CSIS-123B) OR (CSIS-116E+CSIS-126E) — represented as a choose_units(6) group where the pair must match (schema can't express the pairing constraint), PLUS six additional units chosen from the listed CSIS electives. Course renumbering noted in catalog: CSIS-514A formerly CSIS-114A; CSIS-525 formerly CSIS-125A. All CSIS/STAT courses are 3 units each. There is also a Cybersecurity, A.S. in this same program, but it was not requested.

No course codes or units were fabricated. All data transcribed from the two official degree pages plus one verification fetch.

Golden West offers exactly TWO Computer Science associate degrees: (1) Computer Science, Associate in Science Degree (local A.S., Banner Code 2_AS_CS) and (2) Computer Science, Associate in Science Degree for Transfer (A.S.-T / ADT, Banner Code 2_AST_CSCI). Both are semester-unit, 60-unit minimum.

There is no separate local computing degree. I returned three degree_type entries as requested, but the local_computing entry is byte-identical to the local_cs_as entry (same catalog page, same requirements). Treat them as one real program. (A separate "Software Development, Associate in Arts Degree" also exists in this department but is not a Computer Science degree and was not requested.)

Modeling of OR options: The catalog prints a single "Required Courses" table. I split it into groups to preserve OR semantics. Programming Sequence 1 and Programming Sequence 2 are each a choose-one row. The remaining core courses are all-required.

LOCAL A.S. (local_cs_as): Required Courses (Major Total = 28): pick one of CS G153 Java Programming 1 / CS G175 C++ Programming 1 (3); pick one of CS G154 Java Programming 2 / CS G189 C++ Programming 2 (3); then all of CS G242 (3), CS G262 (3), MATH G180 (4), MATH G185 (4), PHYS G185 (4), PHYS G280 (4). Note: in the LOCAL A.S., BOTH physics courses (PHYS G185 and PHYS G280) are required with no substitution. GE = "GE Pattern (Local or Cal-GETC)" 24-34 units (I recorded units_min 24 as the ge_area group). Electives added as needed to reach 60.

TRANSFER A.S.-T (ast): Required Courses (Major Total = 28-29): pick one of CS G153/CS G175/CS G131 (3); pick one of CS G154/CS G189/CS G231 (3); all of CS G242 (3), CS G262 (3), MATH G180 Calculus 1 (4), MATH G185 Calculus 2 (4), PHYS G185 Calculus Based Physics: Mechanics (4); then a science choose-one slot printed as "4-5" units: pick one of PHYS G280 Calculus Based Physics: Electricity/Magnetism / BIOL G180 Cell and Molecular Biology / BIOL G186 Diversity of Organisms / CHEM G180 General Chemistry A. The degree sheet left units blank on the BIOL/CHEM "or" rows; I verified from the course-description pages that BIOL G180 = 5, BIOL G186 = 5, CHEM G180 = 5 units (PHYS G280 = 4), which is why the slot shows 4-5. So the transfer degree differs from the local A.S. by (a) adding Python as a programming option and (b) making the second science course a choose-one among physics/biology/chemistry rather than requiring PHYS G280. GE = Cal-GETC (34 units); ADT graduation requires 60 semester units, 2.0 GPA, grade C or P in each major course, and completion of Cal-GETC. Transferable electives added as needed to reach 60.

No fabrication: every course prefix/number/title/units is exactly as printed on the official catalog pages; the only unit lookups (BIOL/CHEM) were verified from the same catalog's course-description pages and are flagged above.

Authoritative source: SRJC official curriculum portal program-of-study page for "CS Computer Science (AS)", MajorCertID=3003 (https://portal.santarosa.edu/srweb/SR_ProgramOfStudy.aspx?MajorCertID=3003), reached from the survey URL https://curriculum.santarosa.edu/majors. Effective/catalog term shown on the page is "Summer 2025" (2025-2026 catalog cycle) — current per the anti-stale rule. SRJC is a semester-unit college.

DEGREE IDENTITY: The task lists two extraction targets — (a) the local Computer Science A.S. and (b) the closest local computing associate degree named "Computer Science." At SRJC these are the SAME degree: there is one "Computer Science (AS)" and no separate computing degree also named Computer Science. I therefore report it once, tagged local_cs_as. It simultaneously satisfies the local_computing target; treat this single entry as filling both. (SRJC also offers separate CS-department A.S. degrees — Cybersecurity, Game Development, Web Full-Stack Developer — and A.A. degrees in Graphic Design and Web & Multimedia; none of these is the plain "Computer Science" degree, so they were excluded.)

TITLE NOTE: "CS" in the printed title "CS Computer Science (AS)" is SRJC's department/cluster prefix (it prefixes all CS-department majors), not part of the degree name. The degree is Computer Science, Associate in Science.

MAJOR CORE (all required, transcribed verbatim and cross-checked across two fetches): CS 10A Introduction to Programming Concepts and Methodologies (4.00), CS 10B Programming Concepts and Methodologies 1 (4.00), CS 10C Programming Concepts and Methodologies 2 (4.00), CS 12 Assembly Language Programming/Computer Architecture (4.00), MATH 1A Calculus, First Course (5.00), MATH 1B Calculus, Second Course (5.00), MATH 4 Discrete Mathematics (4.00). Subtotal = 30.00 units. Contains real CS/programming courses (anti-error rule 4 satisfied).

ELECTIVE GROUP: The page states "Complete any combination totaling at least 4.00 units from the following": PHYS 40 Classical Mechanics for Scientists and Engineers (5.00), PHYS 41 Waves, Optics, and Thermodynamics for Scientists & Engineers (4.00), PHYS 42 Electricity and Magnetism for Scientists and Engineers (4.00). Modeled as choose_units, units_min = 4. No OR-substitutions exist inside the required-core group; every core course is mandatory.

UNITS: The program-of-study page prints "Total Units: 34.00" for the major (30 core + 4 elective minimum). That 34 is the MAJOR-requirement subtotal only. I set the degree total_units field to 60, the documented California/SRJC minimum for any associate degree; the balance beyond the 34 major units is general education plus free electives. GE is not enumerated on this page — recorded as a high-level ge_area group. SRJC A.S. students may complete SRJC local GE, CSU GE Breadth, or Cal-GETC; the program page does not fix which pattern, so no GE courses were transcribed (per the GE instruction).

CONFIDENCE: Major-core and elective groups are high-confidence verbatim transcriptions. The 60-unit degree total and the GE group are inferred from standard SRJC/California associate-degree rules (not printed on this specific page), which is the only reason confidence is not higher.

DEGREE 1 — Computer Science AS-T (degree_type ast). Catalog heading "ASSOCIATE IN SCIENCE / COMPUTER SCIENCE FOR TRANSFER DEGREE"; narrative title "Associate in Science in Computer Science for Transfer Degree." Awarded on completion of 60 semester units, min 2.0 GPA, grade C/P in each major course, and the full Cal-GETC general education pattern. The catalog prints one heading "REQUIRED CORE" (30 units total) which I split into: (a) seven all-required courses = 25 units, and (b) a science choose-one = 5 units. The science OR option: BIOSC-020 Principles of Biology: Cell and Molecular (5) OR BIOSC-021 Principles of Biology: Organismal (5) OR CHEM-025 General College Chemistry (5) — pick exactly one. Note the catalog prints "Programing" (single m) for COMSC-122/132; transcribed as printed. This is a valid CS degree — core contains real programming/CS courses (COMSC-122, COMSC-132, COMSC-142). total_units = 60 is the explicit "TOTAL DEGREE UNITS 60" printed in the catalog (major core = 30; remainder is Cal-GETC + electives, with double-counting permitted). GE recorded as a high-level Cal-GETC ge_area group, not enumerated.

DEGREE 2 — Information Technology A.S. (degree_type local_computing). Catalog heading "ASSOCIATE OF SCIENCE DEGREE / INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY." Major = 33 semester units: nine all-required courses (30 units) plus a programming-language choose-one (3 units): COMSC-044 Introduction to C++ Programming (3) OR COMSC-051 Java for Programmers (3) OR COMSC-122 Programming Concepts & Methodologies I (3) — pick exactly one. STAT-C1000 is printed as "Statistics" in the degree list; its full course title elsewhere in the catalog is "Introduction to Statistics." COMSC-120 is printed here as "Computer Forensic Fundamentals" (the same course appears as "Introduction to Computer Forensics" in a related certificate). The catalog's IT AS section prints only the major total ("TOTAL UNITS 33") and does not restate a 60-unit degree total; per California standards and LMC's local Associate Degree requirements the full degree is a minimum of 60 semester units (major 33 + local LMC GE + electives). I set total_units = 60 for the degree and captured the local GE as a high-level ge_area group (LMC Associate Degree General Education), not enumerated. No course listed twice within any group (deduped).

The catalog page is JS-heavy for WebFetch (kept truncating), so I fetched the raw HTML via curl and parsed the server-rendered `sc_courselist` requirements table; per-course units were confirmed from the CISB/CISM/CISP/CISN/CISD/CISW course-catalog pages. This is a LOCAL computing A.S. (there is no CS AS-T and no local CS A.S. at Mt. SAC; Computer Programming is the closest local computing degree, as the survey indicated).

The catalog's printed "Total Units" for the major/core requirements is 26-27 semester units. total_units is reported as 60 (the CA associate-degree minimum); the remaining units are General Education plus free electives to reach 60. OR / choose options:
- Core "Choose from one of the following:" (Operating System, 3-3.5 units): either CISN 21 Windows Operating System (3), OR the pair CISN 31 Linux Operating System (3) + CISN 31L Linux Operating System Laboratory (0.5).
- Core "Choose from one of the following groups:" (Database, 3.5 units): pick ONE group — CISD 11 + CISD 11L (Microsoft Access), OR CISD 21 + CISD 21L (Microsoft SQL Server), OR CISD 31 + CISD 31L (Oracle). Each group = 3 + 0.5 = 3.5 units.
- Required Electives "Choose one of the following concentrations:" (6.5-7 units): pick exactly ONE of four concentrations. C++ (CISP 31/31L + CISP 34/34L = 7), Java (CISP 21/21L + CISP 24/24L = 7), Python (CISP 71/71L + CISP 74/74L = 7), or Web (CISW 17 required + choose one lecture/lab combo: CISW 31 & 31L PHP, OR CISW 24 & 24L Python = 6.5). I modeled each concentration as its own group (rule=all); only ONE concentration group is completed. Note: older CIS marketing page listed C++/Java/Visual Basic concentrations, but the current 2025-2026 catalog concentrations are C++/Java/Python/Web — I used the current catalog.

Contains genuine programming/CS courses (Java, C++, Python, OOD), confirming this is the correct programming degree, not a math/physics shell.

2. Computer Science, AS-T (Associate in Science for Transfer / ADT, Curriculum ID SCC.CMPR.AST, Control # 33379) — captured as ast.

Source: Chaffey College official Catalog 2025-2026 (534-page PDF, PDF metadata Title "Catalog 2025-2026", Author "Chaffey College", last updated 1/13/2026), extracted with pdftotext -layout. URL: https://www.chaffey.edu/catalogandschedule/docs/catalogs/prior-year-cats/2025-2026-full.pdf. Chaffey is on the SEMESTER system. This is the correct, current, authoritative catalog for THIS college (Chaffey College, Rancho Cucamonga, CA, id 69) — verified against the survey source URL.

DEGREE 1 — Computer Science for Transfer - AS-T (catalog p.139, printed title "Computer Science for Transfer - AS-T"; Local Major Code S103, PCN 32748, TOP 0706.00, CIP 11.0701). Award = Associate in Science for Transfer (AS-T / ADT). Required Core is exactly 30.00 units, ALL 8 courses required, NO list-A/list-B elective choices (unusual but correct — the physics sequence fills the core): COMPSCI 1, COMPSCI 2, COMPSCI 3, COMPSCI 4, MATH 65A, MATH 65B, PHYS 45, PHYS 46. Core satisfies the CS-content rule (COMPSCI 1-4 are programming/CS courses). Degree completion requires a minimum of 60 CSU-transferable semester units, min 2.0 GPA, min 18 major units at C or better, and the California General Education Transfer Curriculum (Cal-GETC). GE pattern = Cal-GETC (captured as a ge_area group, no courses enumerated per instructions). total_units set to 60 = degree minimum; the printed MAJOR total is 30.00 units.

DEGREE 2 — Computer Information Systems - A.S. Degree (catalog p.135-136, printed title "Computer Information Systems - A.S. Degree"; Local Major Code S100, PCN 04765, TOP 0702.00, CIP 11.0103). This is the closest LOCAL computing associate degree (no standalone local CS A.S. exists at Chaffey). Structure: (a) Required Core = 24.00-25.00 units; within the core there is one OR-choice: "Choose one: CISCO 1 Cisco Internetworking I (4.00) OR CIS 50 Introduction to Computer Networks (3.00)" (this is the 3.00-4.00 range driving the 24-25 spread) — captured as a separate choose_courses (choose_n=1) group; the remaining 10 core courses (sum 21.00 units) are all required. (b) "Plus a professional emphasis selection from the options below (minimum of 12 units)" = 12.00-14.00 units — captured as a choose_units group (units_min=12). The catalog organizes the emphasis courses into EIGHT named tracks and the student takes a minimum of 12 units (normally from one track): Computer Science (CISPROG 5, COMPSCI 1, COMPSCI 2, COMPSCI 3, COMPSCI 401); Cybersecurity (CISCYBR 1, CISCYBR 2, CISNTWK 11, CISNTWK 70); Data Analytics (BUSTEC 63, CIS 15, CISPROG 5, CISPROG 6, SCSCI 10); Game Development (CISGAME 1, CISGAME 2, CISGAME 403, CISGAME 420); Networking (CISNTWK 11, CISNTWK 435, CISCYBR 1, CISCYBR 2); Programming (CISIWEB 74, CISPROG 5, COMPSCI 1, COMPSCI 2); Social Media (BUSMKT 55, COMSTD 12, PHOTO 7, then "choose one" ARTGD 82 Introduction to Digital Media OR ARTGD 83 Web Design, then "choose one" JOUR 11 Multimedia Reporting OR JOUR 30 Student Media Practicum I); Technology and Innovation (CISCYBR 2, CISNTWK 70, CISNTWK 471, COMPSCI 401). The emphasis group's courses array is DEDUPED across tracks (several courses recur in multiple tracks, e.g. CISPROG 5, COMPSCI 1/2, CISCYBR 2, CISNTWK 11/70, COMPSCI 401). Printed MAJOR total = "Total: 36.00-39.00" units; total_units set to 60 = the CA associate degree minimum (major + local GE + electives). GE pattern = Chaffey local/CSU GE (English composition, arts & humanities, math & natural sciences, social sciences) — captured as a ge_area group, not enumerated. An identical course structure also exists as a CIS Certificate of Achievement (p.137, Local Code L100) — NOT extracted here since the request is for the A.S. degree.

extraction.notes:
Two distinct CS associate degrees exist at Crafton Hills College and both were fetched cleanly from the college's own official smartcatalogiq 2025-2026 catalog (no browser needed).

DEGREE RELATIONSHIP: The earlier survey's "closest local computing degree = Computer Science" is the SAME program as the local CS A.S. There is only one local Computer Science associate degree. I therefore emit that one degree twice — once as degree_type local_cs_as and once as degree_type local_computing — with identical data, to preserve the dataset's two columns. They are the same physical degree, not two programs.

OR OPTIONS: On both degrees the "Discrete Structures" slot is "CSCI 200 or MATH 200" (4.00 units, choose one). I encoded it as a single core line (prefix CSCI, number 200) with the OR flagged in the title; MATH 200 is the equivalent alternative.

TOTAL-UNITS CAVEAT: Neither degree page prints a full 60-unit degree total or an itemized GE block. The local A.S. page prints only "Total Credit Hours: 20.00" for the major (the six listed courses actually sum to 21.00 — a catalog rounding/print quirk). The AS-T page prints "Total Credit Hours: 29.00" for the major (the eight listed courses sum to 30.00 — same quirk, likely from the OR option). I set total_units = 60 for each, which is the California statutory minimum for an associate degree (and the AS-T is defined in law as 60 semester units) — this is the degree total, NOT a figure printed on the page. The GE groups below are recorded as high-level ge_area entries, not enumerated.

GE PATTERNS: The local A.S. page states no explicit GE pattern (follows the local Crafton Hills associate-degree GE pattern to reach 60 units). The AS-T page states "CAL-GETC ... is the required general education pattern for this degree." (The prior-year 2024-2025 AS-T page said IGETC; the current 2025-2026 page uses CAL-GETC.)

TITLE NOTE: CSCI 240 title is printed slightly differently between the two pages — "Computer Organization and Assembly Language" on the local A.S. page vs. "...Assembly Language Programming" on the AS-T page; each is transcribed as printed on its own page.

Sources used (both this exact college's official catalog, craftonhills.smartcatalogiq.com):

extraction.notes:
SOURCE: Official Gavilan College Catalog 2026-2027, hosted on CurricUNET META at https://gavilan.curriqunet.com/catalog/alias/2026-2027catalog (Academic Programs). This is a JavaScript single-page app; plain WebFetch returns only an empty shell, so I rendered it in a browser and transcribed both degrees directly. Data is complete and current, so needs_browser=false. The static gavilan.edu department PDFs (e.g. www.gavilan.edu/academic/csis/docs/CSIS_AS.pdf) are a 2019 "Computer Programming" outline and were NOT used for units. The AS-T course mapping was cross-checked against Gavilan's official Computer Science TMC template (www.gavilan.edu/academic/csis/docs/AS-TDegree.docx, College Name: Gavilan College, 28-unit core) and matches the current catalog exactly.

DEGREE 1 — Computer Science A.S.-T (ast): Confirmed from current catalog. Required Core = 28 units. It has two OR-choice lines: (CS1) CSIS 5 C++ Scientific Programming OR CSIS 45 C++ Programming I OR CSIS 24 Java Programming I (3 units); (CS2) CSIS 46 C++ Programming II OR CSIS 27 Java Programming II (3 units). The other six lines are all required: CSIS 28 (3), CSIS 26 (3), MATH 1A (4), MATH 1B (4), PHYS 4A (4), PHYS 4B (4). I listed all OR-alternatives inside the single "Required Core" group (rule=all, units_min=28) with the OR structure described here. Degree completion also requires the full CSU GE-Breadth (39 units) OR IGETC (37 units) pattern, with 7 units double-countable, plus 0-2 elective units, for a 60-unit total (min 2.0 GPA, C or better in major courses). Award printed as "A.S.-T Degree".

DEGREE 2 — local_computing: IMPORTANT DISCREPANCY. The earlier survey's target "Business Computer Applications" A.S. degree does NOT exist in the current 2026-2027 catalog. It has been discontinued/restructured: CSIS no longer lists a Business Computer Applications A.S., and the Business department now folds that computer/office-applications content into two current awards — (a) "Business (with options) - A.A. Degree" via its "Office Applications Option" track, and (b) a standalone "Business: Office Applications Option - Certificate of Achievement" (a certificate, not a degree). I extracted the associate DEGREE, "Business (with options) - A.A. Degree" (award A.A.), as the closest current local computing associate degree, capturing all of its major groups. The computer-applications track = the "Office Applications Option" group (CSIS 121/ACCT 121 Spreadsheet-MS Excel, CSIS 122 Computer Keyboarding, CSIS 126 Word Processing-MS Word, CSIS 129/CMUN 129 Presentation Graphics-MS PowerPoint). Structure: student takes CORE (choose one of CSIS 1 / CSIS 2) + ONE of five options (Accounting, Management, Medical Office, Office Applications, Technical Publishing) + electives so that core+option+electives >= 18 units in the major, plus 35-39 GE units, for a 60-unit A.A. total. The catalog's printed "Total: 51-124" is a computed min-max artifact of the unit ranges, not the degree total; standard CA A.A. minimum is 60 units.

OR-OPTION details for the Business A.A. groups: Core = CSIS 1 (2u) OR CSIS 2 (4u), choose one. Accounting Option: ACCT 103 OR ACCT 20; ACCT 120 OR CSIS 120. Medical Office Option: CSIS 126 OR BOT 283. Office Applications Option: ACCT 121 OR CSIS 121; CSIS 129 OR CMUN 129. Technical Publishing Option: each line is CSIS x OR DM x (Webpage Authoring, Photoshop I, Advanced Photoshop, Digital Illustration, Digital Photography, Web Development and Design). Electives: several OR pairs (ACCT 120/CSIS 120, ACCT 121/CSIS 121, BOT 100/BUS 100, BUS 11/ECON 11/STAT C1000, BUS 14/ECON 14, CSIS 6/DM 6, CSIS 75/DM 75, CSIS 129/CMUN 129, BOT 291A/B/C). Variable-unit courses recorded at their upper bound: CSIS 122 Computer Keyboarding (0.5-2), CSIS 212 Keyboard Speed Building (0.5-1). CSIS 212 was printed twice in the electives list in the catalog; deduped to one entry. Business A.A. GE may follow Gavilan local A.A./A.S. GE or the Cal-GETC pattern.

catalog_url is the catalog alias URL for both degrees; CurricUNET program pages use javascript:void navigation (no stable per-program URL). Nav path: Academic Programs > Computer Science and Information Systems > "Computer Science - A.S.-T Degree"; and Academic Programs > Business > "Business (with options) - A.A. Degree".

extraction.notes:
Source: West Valley College official 2026-2027 catalog, program page https://www.westvalley.edu/catalog/programs/computer-science-associate-in-science-degree-for-transfer.html (reached from the department source URL provided). Page footer "Last Updated 6/30/26"; header "Catalog 2026-2027". Fetchable static HTML; verified the exact table structure by parsing raw HTML.

Degree: "Associate in Science in Computer Science for Transfer" (AS-T in Computer Science), 60 semester units total. Major Core Requirements = 35 units; remainder filled by Cal-GETC general education plus CSU-transferable electives ("CSU transferable elective courses are required when the major units plus transfer GE units total is less than 60"). Degree text requires: 60 semester/90 quarter CSU-transferable units, Cal-GETC pattern, min 18 semester units in major, 2.0 GPA, and grade C/P or better in all major courses.

The whole major is presented under ONE catalog heading "Major Core Requirements (35 units)" with columns Course Number / Course Title / Units. I split it into sub-groups to preserve the "Select one:" rows.

OR options (each is a verbatim "Select one:" row):
- First programming course, choose 1 of 3: CIST004A Computer Programming I (C++ Programming) 4u; CIST004A1 Computer Programming I (Java) 4u; CIST005A Introduction to Python 4u.
- Second programming / data-structures course, choose 1 of 3: CIST004B Data Structures using Advanced C++ 4u; CIST004B1 Data Structures using Advanced Java 4u; CIST005B Advanced Python 4u.
- Calculus I, choose 1 of 2: MATH003A Calculus and Analytical Geometry 5u OR MATH003AH Honors Calculus and Analytical Geometry 5u.
- Calculus II, choose 1 of 2: MATH003B Calculus and Analytical Geometry 5u OR MATH003BH Honors Calculus and Analytical Geometry 5u.

Straight required (no choice): CIST039 Computer Organization and Assembly Programming 4u; MATH019 Discrete Mathematics 4u; PHYS004A Engineering Physics - Mechanics 5u; BIOL040 Organismal Biology 4u. Note: PHYS004A and BIOL040 are BOTH listed as required (no "select one" language between them) — unusual for a CS AS-T but that is exactly as printed; the four required + four select-one (min) = 4+4+4+4+5+5+5+4 = 35 units, consistent with the printed "Total Major Units: 35 units".

General education follows the Cal-GETC pattern (not enumerated in the catalog table); GE recorded as a high-level group, not course-by-course. Related awards on same page: Certificate of Achievement, Cisco Certified Network Associate, Computer Science (local) — not part of this AS-T.

extraction.notes:
Anchored to Lassen Community College (id 82) only, via its official domain lassencollege.edu. Two CURRENT authoritative sources were obtained and cross-checked:

1) PRIMARY — 2026-2027 College Catalog PDF (p. 62): https://www.lassencollege.edu/wp-content/uploads/2026/06/2026-2027-College-Catalog.pdf . Extracted via pdftotext (raw WebFetch failed to parse the binary PDF).
2) CORROBORATING — official CIS AS degree sheet, "Effective: Fall 2025 / Revised 02/06/2025": https://www.lassencollege.edu/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/Computer-Information-Systems-AS.pdf (read cleanly as PDF).

Both agree on: Total = 60 semester units; Required Core = 19 units; Required Electives = 6 units; Free/Open Electives = 14 units; General Education = 21 units. This is a LOCAL Computer Information Systems A.S. (no AS-T in CS, no local CS A.S.). The core contains genuine programming/CS content (CIS 81 Introduction to Python, CIS 50 IT Essentials, CIS 90 The IT Professional, CIS 60 Networking, CIS 70 Security), satisfying the CS-content sanity check.

DISCREPANCY between the two current sources (documented, not fabricated): the 2026-2027 catalog places CIS 82 Cloud Computing (3.0) in the Required Core and lists CIS 80 Introduction to Databases (3.0) among the Required Electives; the Fall 2025 degree sheet does the opposite (CIS 80 in core, CIS 82 in electives). Unit totals are identical either way. I report the catalog (most recent official document) as primary; be aware the effective degree sheet swaps these two courses.

CATALOG TYPOS noted: the catalog's total-units line reads "Total Units for the Associate in Arts Degree: 60 Units" although the degree is clearly an Associate in Science (section header: "Associate in Science in Computer Information Systems"); and the AS-section Program Student Learning Outcomes are mislabeled as "Computer Support Specialist Certificate." These are catalog copy-paste artifacts; award = A.S.

Required Electives rule: complete 6 units from CA 31 / CIS 71 / CIS 80 / CIS 91 (per catalog). Free Electives (14 units): student may select any courses numbered 1-99. GE = 21 units under Lassen's local associate-degree GE pattern (counselor-planned); not enumerated per instructions.

extraction.notes:
Source: official College of the Redwoods 2025-2026 Catalog (with Addendum) PDF, degree found on catalog p.100 (2025-2026). The eLumen online catalog (2026-2027 and 2025-2026) is JavaScript-only and returned no content via WebFetch; the PDF was the fetchable authoritative source and was parsed locally with pdftotext. This college has NO local CS A.S. and NO CS AS-T; the closest local computing degree is "CIS Networking" (A.S.), extracted here.

The degree's catalog heading reads "PROGRAM REQUIREMENTS  60 Units" — the 60 is the DEGREE TOTAL (CA associate minimum). The 11 listed major courses sum to 43 semester units (BUS10 = 3, plus ten CIS courses at 4 each = 40). The remaining ~17 units are general education. I validated this reading against sibling certificate pages in the same catalog, whose "PROGRAM REQUIREMENTS X Units" totals exactly match their listed courses (CIS Networking CA "31 Units" = BUS10 3 + seven CIS×4 = 31; Cisco Networking & Microsoft Server Admin cert "12 Units" = three CIS×4; CIS Cybersecurity cert "16 Units" = four CIS×4). So the major group is all-required with no electives or OR options.

General education: not enumerated on the program page. College of the Redwoods A.S. degrees require completion of the local associate-degree GE pattern (CR also offers Cal-GETC / CSU GE / IGETC options for transfer). No GE courses are listed on the degree page, so the GE group below is recorded at high level only.

Note CIS35's title as printed on the A.S. degree page is "Introduction to Information Systems Security" (the CIS Cybersecurity certificate page prints the same CIS35 as "Introduction to Cybersecurity" — same course number, differing catalog title text). Program is semester-unit. All course codes/units transcribed exactly as printed; none fabricated.

extraction.notes:
Anchored to Bakersfield College's OWN official current catalog (2026-2027 eLumen catalog, tenant bakersfield.elumenapp.com). The catalog is a JavaScript-only Angular SPA that WebFetch could not render, and programmap.bakersfieldcollege.edu 403-blocks non-browser fetches. I retrieved the authoritative data via the eLumen public catalog API used by the site itself: GET https://api-prod.elumenapp.com/catalog/sites/publish/2026-2027-Catalog,program,computer-science-associate-in-science-for-transfer-fall-2025?tenant=bakersfield.elumenapp.com&api=https://api-prod.elumenapp.com:443 — this returns the published catalog HTML, from which I transcribed the exact "Program Requirements" table. No browser was needed. Only one qualifying degree exists (the AS-T in Computer Science); Bakersfield has no local (non-transfer) CS A.S. degree, matching the prior survey.

MAJOR STRUCTURE: The catalog prints one umbrella block "Computer Science Associate in Science for Transfer (Total 28 - 29)" containing "Core Courses (Total 28 - 29) — Complete all of the following" with 7 always-required courses (COMP B11, COMP B12, COMP B13, COMP B14, MATH B6A, MATH B6B, PHYS B4A = 24 units), plus a nested "Science Choice (Select One Course) (Total 4 - 5)" subgroup. I modeled these as two groups: (1) Core Courses (rule all, 24 units) and (2) Science Choice (choose 1 of 2). Math: 24 + 4 = 28 or 24 + 5 = 29, matching the printed 28-29 major total.

OR OPTION: The "Science Choice (Select One Course)" group is an OR — take CHEM B1A (General Chemistry I, 5 units) OR PHYS B4B (Heat, Electricity, Magnetism, 4 units). Note PHYS B4A is separately required in the core.

GE: Degree follows the Cal-GETC (California General Education Transfer Curriculum) pattern per the catalog. I did not enumerate GE courses; recorded a high-level ge_area group. The catalog states the ADT requires completion of 60 transferable semester units total = Cal-GETC + minimum 18 semester units in the major (this major is 28-29 units); some major STEM courses may double-count toward Cal-GETC. Catalog also states a "P" (Pass) grade IS acceptable for major courses (verbatim), and a 2.0 minimum GPA.

total_units = 60 reflects the minimum degree total (CA ADT). Course codes/units transcribed verbatim from the official catalog; nothing fabricated.

extraction.notes:
Anchored strictly to Los Angeles Pierce College (lapc.edu, Woodland Hills, CA; LACCD, semester units). I explicitly did NOT use catalog.pierce.ctc.edu, which is Pierce College in Washington State (its "Computer Science AA-DTA" is a WA Direct Transfer Agreement degree, not this college).

CURRENT SOURCE: 2025-2026 General Catalog (most current; today is 2026-07). The 2024-2025 catalog was reviewed first but superseded.

(1) COMPUTER SCIENCE AS-T (degree_type ast): NOT present in the main 2025-2026 catalog's ADT list, NOR in the 2024-2025 catalog. It was newly ADDED by the 2025-2026 General Catalog ADDENDUM B (published 2026-05), effective Summer 2026, STATE CODE 46078, Major Code P046078H. The addendum gives the full major (32 units) and states the ADT requires 60 semester units total with Cal-GETC (34 units) GE. Structure: seven all-required courses (CS 116, CS 130, CS 131, CS 136, MATH 261, MATH 262, PHYSICS 101) plus a select-one lab-science block (BIOLOGY 006 OR BIOLOGY 007 OR CHEM 101 OR PHYSICS 102, 5 units). This confirms the survey's "transfer AS-T in CS = true." total_units set to 60 (ADT minimum stated in the addendum); the 32-unit figure is the major block only.

(2) PROGRAMMING FOR COMPUTER SCIENCE AS (degree_type local_computing): STATE CODE 02824, Major Code P002824C, from the 2025-2026 main catalog p.106. It is the closest LOCAL computing degree (the college has no standalone local "Computer Science" A.S.; local_cs_as = false, consistent with survey). MAJOR REQUIRED = 11 courses (all required). MAJOR ELECTIVES = "Select 5-7 semester units" from 6 listed courses (choose_units, min 5). Major total 40-42 units; degree total 60 minimum. GE follows LACCD GE (21 units) or Cal-GETC (34 units). Note: vs the 2024-2025 catalog, the only change is the elective list now shows STAT C1000 (Introduction to Statistics, 4) in place of the former MATH 227 Statistics.

OR/select options: For the CS AST lab-science group, exactly one of BIOLOGY 006 / BIOLOGY 007 / CHEM 101 / PHYSICS 102 (each 5 units). For Programming for CS, the electives are a choose-5-to-7-units pool. All course codes/units transcribed exactly as printed (asterisks in the catalog denote prereq/coreq notes and were dropped from the course numbers). GE groups were recorded at ge_area level per instructions, not enumerated.

extraction.notes:
Both degrees extracted from Oxnard College's own official VCCCD CourseLeaf catalog (catalog.vcccd.edu/oxnard), 2026-2027 edition. Degree pages render course tables only via the print/PDF view; the HTML view returned nav-only content to WebFetch, so I downloaded each degree's .pdf and the CNIT department course-listing .pdf and extracted text locally with pdftotext. VCCCD is a semester-unit system.

CS AS-T: confirmed as the correct CS transfer degree (contains CS R131/R132 Programming Concepts and Methodology I/II, CS R142 Computer Architecture, CS R152 Discrete Structures — real programming/CS courses, not just math/physics). Printed "Total Required Major Units 32" = Required Core (27) + List A (5). "Total Units Required for AS-T Degree 60." GE = Cal-GETC (34 units), Double-Counted 7-10, Free Electives 1-4. List A is a choose-1 lab-science option: PHYS R132 (5) OR BIOL R120 + R120L lab (5 combined) OR BIOL R122 + R122L lab (5 combined) OR CHEM R120 (5); the two BIOL options each require a co-requisite lab, and the catalog prints the 5-unit value on the course+lab pair (I recorded the pair as a single 5-unit option with the lab noted in title_seen). MATH courses use the new statewide C-ID-aligned prefix "MATH C2210 / C2220" exactly as printed.

CNIT AS (closest local computing degree): Required Core = 21 units (6 courses; individual units confirmed from the CNIT course-description PDF and the semester plan). Elective group heading printed verbatim: "Select a minimum of 9 units from the following (no more than 4 units of COT may be selected):" — recorded as choose_units, units_min 9. The COT reference in that heading points to a Computer Technology (COT) prefix that is NOT enumerated in the printed list; only CNIT courses are listed, so I captured only the listed CNIT electives. CNIT R191A/R191B/R191C are variable-unit Work Experience courses printed as "1-14 Units"; I set units_seen=1 (base) for each — treat as variable 1-14. "Total Required Major Units 30-33." Degree total: with the Oxnard College GE Pattern (areas 1-7, 24 units) the printed "Total Units Required for A.S. Degree" = 60; with Cal-GETC (34 units) it = 64-67. I recorded total_units=60 (the minimum/standard path) and GE as "Oxnard College GE Pattern (areas 1-7) or Cal-GETC." 

No fabrication: every course code and unit value is transcribed from the catalog PDFs. No same-named out-of-district college was used. No local standalone CS A.S. exists at Oxnard (survey said local CS A.S. = false), consistent with what I found — only the CS AS-T transfer degree in the Computer Science section.

extraction.notes:
Source: official Yuba College 2025-26 Catalog PDF (https://yc.yccd.edu/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/Yuba-College-Catalog-25.pdf), Computer Science section on printed pages 86-87 (PDF pages 85-86). Unit system is semester; both degrees total 60 semester units minimum. Extracted directly from the fetchable catalog PDF (downloaded and read via pdftotext because the file exceeds WebFetch's 10MB limit).

DEGREES FOUND (2 distinct associate degrees):
1) Computer Science — ASSOCIATE IN SCIENCE FOR TRANSFER (A.S.-T), printed p.86. Major requirement = 29-30 units. The OR option is COMSC 9A C++ Programming (4) OR COMSC 12 JAVA Programming (3) — pick one (this is why the major is a 29-30 range). Strictly required: COMSC 2 (3), COMSC 9B (4), COMSC 15 (3), MATH 1A (4), MATH 1B (4), PHYS 4A (4), PHYS 4B (4). General education follows Cal-GETC; degree requires 60 CSU-transferable semester units, min 2.0 GPA, and C/P grades in the major.
2) Computer Science — ASSOCIATE IN SCIENCE (local A.S.), printed p.87. Major requirement = 19-21 units. COMSC 9A C++ Programming (4) is strictly required; then COMSC 9B Data Structures (4) OR COMSC 11 Advanced C++ Programming (3) — pick one; then a minimum of 12 units from a list of 8 COMSC courses (COMSC 2, 6, 7, 8, 9B [if not used above], 11 [if not used above], 12, 15). In addition, students complete a minimum of 21 units of local GE plus the health graduation requirement, for a total of 60 degree-applicable units.

DEGREE-TYPE MAPPING: The earlier survey's "closest local computing degree = Computer Science" is the SAME degree as the local Computer Science A.S. (item 2). I have therefore filled the local_computing slot with a duplicate of the local_cs_as entry; Yuba College has only ONE local Computer Science associate degree, not two. Do not double-count.

EXCLUDED: Yuba College also offers a Computer Science CERTIFICATE OF ACHIEVEMENT (19-21 units, no GE) — a certificate, not an associate degree, so it is not reported here. Its course list is similar to the local A.S. but omits COMSC 8.

No course codes or units were fabricated; all values transcribed exactly as printed. Prefix printed as "COMSC" and separately as "COMSC-#" in the course-description blocks.

extraction.notes:
Source: official WLAC catalog PDF (https://www.wlac.edu/sites/wlac.edu/files/2024-08/catalog.pdf). Although the file path contains "2024-08", the document itself is the CURRENT West Los Angeles College 2025-2026 Catalog (every page footer reads "West Los Angeles College 2025-2026 Catalog"). WebFetch returned HTTP 403, so the PDF was downloaded via curl and text/pages extracted with pdftotext/pdftoppm; the two degree pages (catalog pp. 89-90) were visually verified against rendered page images, so extraction is high-confidence.

DEGREE 1 — Computer Science (AS-T): Printed heading "COMPUTER SCIENCE (AS-T)"; full ADT name "Associate in Science in Computer Science for Transfer Degree"; MAJOR CODE 0706.00; ACADEMIC PROGRAM CODE W039126H. "Required courses" is a flat all-required list of 8 courses = 32 major units (no choose-from options): CS 118, CS 131, CS 136, CS 213, BIOLOGY 006, MATH 261, MATH 262, PHYSICS 037. Contains genuine CS/programming courses (CS 118/131/136/213), confirming this is the correct CS program. Degree also requires Cal-GETC GE (24 units, less double-counted) + 4 CSU-transferable elective units = 60 total. GE not enumerated per instructions; follows the Cal-GETC pattern. Requirements also include: min 60 semester units, 2.0 GPA, grade C/P in each major course. Courses marked * in catalog have prereq/coreq (CS 118, CS 131, CS 136, CS 213, MATH 261, MATH 262, PHYSICS 037); asterisks stripped from titles below.

DEGREE 2 — Computer Science Information Technology (AA): Printed heading "COMPUTER SCIENCE INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY (AA)"; MAJOR CODE 0702.00; ACADEMIC PROGRAM CODE W003310C. This is the local (non-transfer) computing degree. Structure: "Required core courses" = 21 units (7 required courses: CIS 112, CIS 211, CS 101, CS 116, CS 118, CS 136, CS 213) + "Major electives units (choose three from the following)" = 9 units from an 11-course list (CIS 120, CIS 192, CIS 213, CS 112, CS 119, CS 121, CS 131, CS 140, CS 159, CS 216, MIT 220). Total Major Units 30 + LACCD GE Plan (local district GE, 24 units less double-counted) + 6 additional degree-applicable elective units = 60 total. Note the catalog prints "Advanced Programing in Java" (single-m "Programing") for CS 213 within this degree, versus "Advanced Programming in Java" in the AS-T; transcribed as printed in each. A CA (Certificate of Achievement) version of CSIT (30 units, same course set, MAJOR CODE 0702.00 / program code W020234D) also exists but is a certificate, not a degree, and is excluded.

extraction.notes:
All data taken from the current 2025-2026 SBCC CourseLeaf catalog (catalog.sbcc.edu), the exact-match college. Three requested degree_types resolve to two distinct programs: (1) local_cs_as and local_computing are the SAME degree — "Computer Science, Associate of Science (AS)" — because SBCC's closest local computing associate degree is literally titled Computer Science; it is emitted twice per the requested degree_types with identical requirements. (2) ast is the "Computer Science, Associate in Science for Transfer (AS-T)".

total_units is reported as the degree minimum of 60 semester units for all degrees (SBCC requires a minimum of 60 degree-applicable/CSU-transferable semester units, 2.0 GPA, min 12 units at SBCC). The catalog also prints major-block totals: AS major block = 33.00-37.00 units (27-unit Required Core + at least 6 units from List A); AS-T major block = 33 units. GE fills the remainder to reach 60: the AS follows the SBCC local GE pattern OR Cal-GETC pattern; the AS-T requires completion of the Cal-GETC pattern.

Unit sourcing: The AS degree sheet does NOT print per-course units for the List A electives (only "6-10 units" total), so List A course units were cross-referenced from authoritative SBCC sources — CS course units from the CS department PDF (catalog.sbcc.edu/academic-departments/computer-science/computer-science.pdf), and MATH/PHYS/PHIL units from the course-description pages (course-descriptions/math, /phys) and search-confirmed PHIL 205 = 3 units. Verified units: CS 104=3, CS 111=3, CS 115=3, CS 116=3, CS 123=1.5, CS 130=2.5, CS 132=3, CS 133=3, CS 137=3, CS 165=1.5, CS 180=3, CS 187=3, CS 189=1, MATH 160=5, MATH 200=4, MATH 210=4, MATH 220=4, PHIL 205=3, PHYS 122=5.

AS-T structure was verified twice to rule out a hidden "List B" science choice: it is a single fixed Required Core of 8 courses (no select-one lists); BIOL 103 and PHYS 121 are both mandatory. No OR/either-or course options exist in either degree except the List A choose-units block in the AS. Data Science AS and CS Certificate of Achievement also exist in this department but were not requested.

extraction.notes:
Source: official Sierra College catalog department page https://catalog.sierracollege.edu/departments/computer-science/ (2026-2027 catalog), fully fetchable via WebFetch — no browser needed. Sierra College is on the SEMESTER system.

The catalog contains exactly TWO distinct CS associate degrees, both extracted below:
1) "Computer Science for Transfer" (AS-T) — 29 major units, all required, no OR/choose options. GE = Cal-GETC ONLY (catalog explicitly notes CSU GE Breadth is NOT an option for this degree); 60 CSU-transferable semester units total, 2.0 GPA.
2) "Computer Science—Computer Science" (local, awardable as A.A. OR A.S.) — 24 major units (15 required core + choose 9 units from a 5-course elective list). GE = Sierra College local Associate Degree Requirements OR Cal-GETC (student's choice); minimum 60 degree-applicable semester units, 2.0 GPA, incl. 12 units at Sierra.

IMPORTANT on degree_type mapping: The survey's "local CS A.S." (local_cs_as) and its "closest local computing degree named Computer Science—Computer Science (AA/AS Degree)" (local_computing) are THE SAME single degree at Sierra College — the college has only one local Computer Science associate, awardable as A.A. or A.S. I report it ONCE (as local_cs_as, award A.S.) rather than creating a duplicate local_computing record. So local_computing == this local_cs_as entry; there is no separate local_computing program.

OR-option details: AS-T has none (all 10 courses required). Local degree's only choice is the "Select 9 units from the following" group (choose_units, min 9) among CSCI 0014/0027/0046/0052/0066, each 3 units. extraction.notes:
Source: Ventura College official catalog (catalog.vcccd.edu), current 2026-2027 catalog. The degree page is JavaScript-rendered (CourseLeaf), so WebFetch's summarizer dropped the requirement table; I retrieved the raw HTML via curl and parsed the sc_courselist table directly, then cross-checked every course's units against the discipline PDFs (computer-science.pdf, biology.pdf, chemistry.pdf, physics.pdf) from the same catalog domain.

DEGREE STRUCTURE: There is a single printed heading "Required Core Courses". I split it into two groups for clarity: (1) the always-required courses (rule=all), and (2) the final select-one science laboratory sequence (rule=choose_courses, choose_n=1). This select-one is why the printed "Required Core Units" and "Total Major Units" show a range of 31-32.

SCIENCE SELECT-ONE OPTIONS (choose exactly one, listed under "or" in the catalog): PHYS V05 & V05L (5 units = PHYS V05 Electricity and Magnetism for Scientists and Engineers 4u + PHYS V05L lab 1u), OR BIOL V03 Evolution, Ecology, and Organismal Biology (4u), OR BIOL V04 Cell and Molecular Biology (4u), OR CHEM V120A General Chemistry I (5u). The courselist prints "4-5" on the PHYS row as the min-max range across these four options (BIOL=4 min, PHYS/CHEM=5 max); I recorded each option's true individual units, which I verified from the respective discipline PDFs.

CROSS-LISTING: CS V17 Discrete Structures is cross-listed / Same-As MATH V52; the catalog prints it as "CS V17/MATH V52". PHYS V04 & V04L (5 units) = PHYS V04 Mechanics for Scientists and Engineers (4u) + PHYS V04L Mechanics Laboratory (1u), a combined lecture+lab entry.

GENERAL EDUCATION: Follows the Cal-GETC General Education Pattern (34 units), per the catalog. Not enumerated (recorded as a high-level ge_area group). The catalog's unit math: Required Major Units 31-32; Cal-GETC GE Units 34; Double-Counted Units (7-10); Elective Units 1-5; Total Units for the A.S. for Transfer Degree = 60 (California associate-degree minimum, semester units).

No List A / List B structure exists for this degree — it is a single prescriptive required core with one science-lab choice, consistent with the CS AS-T Transfer Model Curriculum.

This is a genuine CS transfer degree with real programming/CS courses (CS-165 Assembly, CS-181/182 C++ or Java, CS-240 Discrete Structures, CS-281/282 intermediate programming + data structures), so it passes the anti-error CS-content check. DEGREE 2 — Networking, Security and System Administration - Enterprise Networking (Associate in Science), degree_type local_computing. Full printed title on the page: "Networking, Security and System Administration - Enterprise Networking Associate in Science and Certificate of Achievement" (the A.S. and the Certificate of Achievement share the same course requirements). Structure: Core Curriculum (13 units, all required) + Area of Emphasis (21.5-38... actually 21.5-25 units) containing an Operating System choose-one (CIS-190 or CIS-191), three required Cisco networking courses (CIS-201/202/203), a Security choose-one (CIS-209 or CIS-263), and "Select three" electives (6.5-10 units). Catalog "Total Units" line for the major = 34.5-38 units, PLUS General Education Requirements. I set total_units=60 to represent the full CA associate-degree minimum since the degree total including GE is not printed as a single combined number; the major-only portion is 34.5-38 units. Elective unit values (CIS-101=1.5, CIS-210=4, CIS-261=2, CIS-264=3, CIS-265=3, CIS-271=3, CIS-272=3; CIS-263=3) were not rendered on the degree page's requirement table, so I confirmed them from the CIS course-descriptions PDF (catalog.gcccd.edu/cuyamaca/course-descriptions/cis/cis.pdf, 2025-2026 edition). GE for the local degree follows the college's local/CSU GE pattern (page says only "Plus General Education Requirements" with a link); units_min not printed on this page.

DEDUPE: no course is listed twice within any group. No course codes or units were fabricated.

extraction.notes:
SOURCE & METHOD: Authoritative catalog is the eLumen system at siskiyous.elumenapp.com. The pages are a JavaScript (Angular) SPA that WebFetch cannot render, so I reverse-engineered the app's rendered-HTML API. The app fetches published page HTML via GET https://api-prod.elumenapp.com/catalog/sites/publish/{comma-separated-route}?tenant=siskiyous.elumenapp.com&api=https://api-prod.elumenapp.com:443 (route slashes are replaced with commas by the client). This returned the fully rendered program HTML, which I transcribed. Used the CURRENT 2026-27 catalog (cos26-27catalog), confirmed by the page footer "College of the Siskiyous 2026-27 Catalog" and TITLE fields. The 25-26 catalog also exists with identical program slugs.

UNIT SYSTEM: semester. TOTAL_UNITS: The catalog prints only the MAJOR credit count on each program page ("Credits: 26.0" for the AS, "Credits: 28.0" for the AS-T). I recorded total_units = 60, the CA associate-degree minimum / full-degree total (the AS-T description explicitly states "completion of 60 semester units or 90 quarter units"); the printed major-only totals are 26 (AS) and 28 (AS-T). GE is required on top of the major to reach 60 but is not enumerated on the program page.

DEGREE MAPPING: The local CS A.S. (local_cs_as) and the "closest local computing degree named Computer Science" (local_computing) are THE SAME single degree at this college — "Computer Science - AS". I output it under both degree_type values with identical requirements; they are not two distinct programs. The AS-T is a separate distinct program ("Computer Science AS-T").

CS AS (LOCAL) major groups: (1) "Complete all of the following" (16 units): CSCI1001 Intro to Computer Science 3, CSCI1007 Programming I 3, CSCI1507 Programming II 3, CSCI1004 Visual Basic Programming 4, CSCI2006 Assembly Language Programming 3. (2) "Complete one of the following:" (4 units, choose 1): MATH1400 Calculus and Analytic Geometry I 4 OR STAT C1000 Introduction to Statistics 4 (page renders the STAT course concatenated as "STATC1000"; discipline prefix STAT, CCN number C1000). (3) "Complete two of the following:" (6 units, choose 2 of 6): CSCI1037 Computer Game Design I, CSCI1038 Computer Game Design II, CSCI1039 Introduction to Linux, CSCI1032 Computer Networks, CSCI1033 PC Hardware Certification, CSCI1034 PC Software Certification (each 3 units). Program note on page: students should take English C1000 and complete the Math competency requirement in year 1 as part of GE. GE follows the COS local associate-degree General Education pattern (COS GE Curriculum 26-27); not enumerated here.

CS AS-T major group: single "Required — Complete all of the following" block (28 units): CSCI1007 Programming I 3, CSCI1507 Programming II 3, CSCI2006 Assembly Language Programming 3, CSCI2010 Discrete Structures 3, MATH1400 Calculus and Analytic Geometry I 4, MATH1500 Calculus and Analytic Geometry II 4, PHYS2105 Calculus-Based Physics for Scientists and Engineers: A 4, PHYS2110 Calculus-Based Physics for Scientists and Engineers: B 4. No OR options in the major. GE follows Cal-GETC (description states "completion of Cal-GETC-Breadth"), minimum 2.0 GPA, minimum grade C/P in each major course. Both degrees are marked "Transferable to both UC and CSU." No fabricated codes/units; all values transcribed exactly as printed.

SOURCE ACCESS: The El Camino Acalog catalog (catalog.elcamino.edu) sits behind an AWS WAF JavaScript challenge — plain curl and WebFetch return HTTP 202 with an empty body / "JavaScript is disabled" awswaf challenge page, and WebFetch also reports an expired TLS cert on that host. I rendered the program page in the browser pane, which passed the WAF challenge, and transcribed the full requirements. Data is complete, so needs_browser is set false, but note that any re-verification of this source requires a JS-capable browser.

CATALOG YEAR: Extracted from the CURRENT 2026-2027 catalog (catoid=13, poid=3505). I confirmed the 2025-2026 version (catoid=12, poid=2712) too; the two differ only slightly (2025-26 option total was 14-18/Total 23-27; 2026-27 adds CIS 123 and CIS 198 to tracks, making option total 14-21/Total 23-30). Reported here is the 2026-2027 version.

DEGREE IDENTIFICATION: This college offers NO local CS A.S. and NO AS-T in Computer Science. The closest local computing associate degree is "Computer Information Systems, AS" (Business Division), confirmed as the only degree (vs. certificates) on the CIS area-of-study page. It is a genuine computing/programming program (includes CIS 16 Visual Basic, CIS 80 Database Programming, CIS 121 Cybersecurity Programming with Python, CIS 132/133/134 web programming, CIS 136 mobile apps).

MAJOR STRUCTURE (printed "Total Units: 23-30" = major requirements only):
Part 1 — "Required Core: 9 units" = CIS 13 (3) + CIS 119 (3) + ONE of {CIS 26 Excel, CIS 28 Access} (3). I split the required-core into two groups: an "all" group (CIS 13, CIS 119) and a choose-one group (CIS 26 OR CIS 28).
Part 2 — "Select one of the following options: 14-21 units" = student picks EXACTLY ONE of seven named tracks and completes ALL courses in that chosen track. Verified unit totals per track: Help Desk = 14 (matches the 14 minimum), Database = 16, Networking = 18, Programming = 17, Mobile and Web Programming = 16, Cybersecurity = 21 (matches the 21 maximum), Cloud Computing = 16. Because the schema has no nested groups, each track is emitted as its own rule="all" group; ONLY ONE such track is actually required. Do not sum all track groups.
Note CIS 28 appears both in the core choose-one and in the Database track, and several CIS courses recur across tracks — these are not intra-group duplicates.

TOTAL UNITS: The program page prints "Total Units: 23-30" for the major. total_units is reported as 60, the minimum for a California associate degree (major 23-30 + General Education + electives to reach 60 semester units). El Camino AS degrees require a minimum of 12 units completed in residence.

GENERAL EDUCATION: Not enumerated per instructions. The AS follows El Camino College's local associate-degree GE pattern (or CSU GE Breadth / Cal-GETC as applicable); GE courses were not listed on the major-requirements page.

Both degrees extracted from the CURRENT 2026-2027 official Grossmont College catalog (catalog.gcccd.edu, Grossmont-Cuyamaca CCD). Anchored to this exact college only.

CS AS-T (ast): Verified from the raw courselist table. 'Required Core' = 8 courses, 34 major units (CSIS-165, CSIS-240, CSIS-293, CSIS-294, MATH-C2210, MATH-C2220, PHYC-201, PHYC-202), all required (rule=all), no List A / restricted electives. Plus Cal-GETC GE 34 units. Catalog 'Total Units' = 60 (major and GE overlap on calculus/physics, so 34+34 nets to the 60-unit minimum). Program contains real CS/programming courses (Assembly Language, Discrete Structures, Java I and II) — confirms correct program, not a math/physics-only mis-grab.

Computer Programming A.S. (local_computing): From the 'Associate Degree Major Requirements' courselist. Major block 'Total Units' printed as 28-30. Structure: (1) Core (rule=all) = CSIS-112 Windows Operating System (3) OR CSIS-113 Introduction to Linux (3), CSIS-119 (3), CSIS-293 (4), CSIS-294 (4) — 112/113 is a choose-one OR pair, both listed. (2) 'Select one of the following sequences:' worth 8 units — choose ONE two-course sequence: CSIS-296+CSIS-297 (C++) OR CSIS-250+CSIS-251 (Python); modeled as choose_units units_min=8 with all four courses listed. (3) 'Select two of the following:' worth 6-8 units — choose 2 of 13 courses (modeled choose_courses choose_n=2, units_min=6). IMPORTANT: the degree page prints NO per-course units for the electives (Units column blank; only the section subtotal 6-8 is shown); per-course elective units were sourced from the official CSIS course-descriptions page (catalog.gcccd.edu/grossmont/courses/csis/) — not fabricated. The printed electives menu verbatim includes CSIS-293 and CSIS-294 (also in the required core) and CSIS-250/251/296/297 (also the language-sequence options); transcribed as printed. total_units set to 60 = the CA/Grossmont associate-degree minimum, since the page only prints the 28-30 major block 'plus General Education and Elective Requirements' and does not print an explicit degree total; the local associate GE pattern (not enumerated here) applies.

All data obtained via curl/WebFetch of the official catalog; no browser needed.

Source: Imperial Valley College official 2026-2027 catalog on CurriQunet META (imperial.curriqunet.com, catalogId=15). The catalog is a JavaScript SPA; the program-mapper URL (programmap.imperial.edu) and direct WebFetch were 403/JS-blocked, so I drove the live catalog in a browser and read its data API (/catalog/_getPage?catalogId=15&id=NNNN). Both target degrees exist and were extracted in full from the current catalog. Human-facing catalog URLs: CIT = https://imperial.curriqunet.com/catalog/iq/8518/8580/8314 ; CS AS-T = https://imperial.curriqunet.com/catalog/iq/8518/8580/8315.

Both degrees are semester-unit. Neither program page enumerates its own General Education; IVC associate degrees additionally require the district GE pattern (local IVC AA/AS GE for the CIT A.S.; Cal-GETC, 34 units minimum, for the CS AS-T, which IS stated on the CS page). Total degree units = 60 semester-unit minimum for both (standard CA associate degree); the major blocks alone are 25-26 units (CIT) and 28 units (CS AS-T).

Course titles in CurriQunet carry a workflow status flag in parentheses — "(Historical)" or "(Active)" — e.g. "Introduction to Data Structures (Historical)", "College Algebra (Active)". These flags are NOT part of the course title; I stripped them from title_seen. All prefixes/numbers/units are exactly as printed.

CS FOR TRANSFER (A.S.-T): III. Required Courses for the Major = 28 units. Seven courses are all-required (CS 221, CS 231, CS 240, CS 281, MATH C2210, MATH C2220, PHYS 200), plus ONE of {PHYS 202 General Physics II | BIOL 182 General Biology: Principles of Organismal Biology} (4 units, printed as "Select one from either PHYS 202 or BIOL 182"). Confirmed a genuine CS program (contains programming/CS core: CS 221 OOP in Java, CS 231 Data Structures, CS 240 Discrete Structures, CS 281 Assembly/Machine Organization). Requirements: complete Cal-GETC (34 units min) + major (28) within a max of 60 CSU-transferable semester units, GPA >= 2.0, grade C or better in all major courses.

COMPUTER INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY (A.S.), local computing degree: 25.0-26.0 units for the major. Group I = 16 units all-required (CIS 101, CIS 160, CIS 161, CIS 162, CIS 202). Group II = choose TWO of five (CIS 159, CIS 163, CIS 166, CIS 167, CIS 210) for 6 units. Group III = choose ONE math/stat course (STAT C1000 4u, MATH 122 3u, MATH 150 4u, MATH 170 4u, or MATH C2210 4u) for 3-4 units — this is why the major total is a 25-26 range. Grade C or better required in all major courses; min 60 semester units total including district GE requirements.

Source: official San Joaquin Delta College 2025-2026 Catalog PDF (catalog.deltacollege.edu), pages 332-333 (Computer Science area). Read directly from the PDF at high resolution to resolve the two-column layout.

Two distinct CS associate degrees exist and were extracted: (1) Computer Science, AS (local) and (2) Computer Science, AS-T (transfer). The survey's "closest local computing degree = Computer Science" is the SAME physical degree as the local CS A.S. ("Computer Science, AS"), so it is NOT emitted a second time under degree_type local_computing to avoid double-counting. (Note: the college also offers a separate "Computer Information Systems, AS", but the survey specifically named Computer Science as the closest local computing degree.)

OR / choice options:
- Computer Science, AS-T Core (23-24 units): PHYSC 4C (4 units) OR CHEM 1A General Chemistry (5 units) — that OR is why the core is a 23-24 unit range. All other core courses (CS 55, CSP 25, MATH 1, MATH 2, PHYSC 4A) are required. CHEM 1A is listed in the Core group only as the alternative to PHYSC 4C.
- Computer Science, AS-T Restricted Electives (6 units): complete EITHER the Java sequence [CSP 26A Java Programming (3) + CSP 26B Data Structures with Java (3)] OR the C++ sequence [CSP 31A C++ Programming I (3) + CSP 31B C++ Programming II (3)]. Modeled as choose_units, units_min 6.
- Computer Science, AS Restricted Electives (12 units): "Complete 12 units from ONE of the Areas below" (A, B, or C). Each area is emitted as its own choose_units(12) group; the student picks a single area. Area A (Programming) = CSP 25 + CSP 26A + (CSP 31A OR CSP 31B) + 3 units of electives from "Electives within the Major". Area B (Database) = CS 12 + CSP 43A + 6 units of electives. Area C (Game Design) = CS 16 + CSW 38 + 6 units of electives. The "Elective - N units" line in each area draws from the "Electives within the Major" list, which is emitted as a separate electives group. CS 50SS Special Studies is variable-unit (printed "Units: 1-4"); recorded as 4 (nominal max).

General Education: AS-T follows CSU-GE (40 units) OR IGETC (37 units). Local AS follows San Joaquin Delta College Associate Degree GE Pattern (27 units) OR CSU-GE (40) OR IGETC (37). GE captured as high-level ge_area groups, not enumerated. Both degrees list Minimum Degree Units Required: 60 (semester units). Both require a grade of "C" or better in all major courses.

extraction.notes:
SOURCE ACCESS: De Anza's official catalog is the eLumen SPA at deanza.elumenapp.com (current published year = 2025-2026). Plain WebFetch and curl to deanza.edu are Cloudflare-blocked (HTTP 403) and the eLumen catalog is JavaScript-only, so I used the browser pane to render and transcribe both program pages. Data is COMPLETE and read directly from the current official catalog, so needs_browser is set false; but note that any non-browser fetcher will be blocked. The stale PDF at deanza.edu/counseling/pdf/degrees/transfer_computer_science.pdf is the 2016-2017 sheet and was NOT used. Unit system is QUARTER (Foothill-De Anza district); both degrees total 90 quarter units (= 60 semester), meeting the CA associate minimum.

CS AS-T (degree_type ast): pulled from https://deanza.elumenapp.com/catalog/2025-2026/program/computer-science-for-transfer-fall-2025 . Contains real CS/programming courses (CIS 21JA assembly, plus CIS 22A/22B/22C or CIS 22C/35A), so it is the correct CS program, not a math/physics-only mis-grab. Two groups: (1) Required Core 36.5 units, all required; (2) 'Required Core - Programming - Complete one option' 9-13.5 units. The programming group is an EITHER/OR of two fixed options, which I modeled as choose_units (units_min 9) over the deduped union of the four courses. Exact options: Option 1 (13.5 u) = CIS 22A + CIS 22B/22BH + CIS 22C/22CH; Option 2 (9 u) = CIS 22C/22CH + CIS 35A. CIS 22C appears in both options but is listed once (deduped). Major total 45.5-50 units. GE follows the Cal-GETC pattern; electives added as needed to reach 90. Many core courses have honors alternates printed as 'X / XH' (MATH 1A/1AH, 1B/1BH, 1C/1CH, 22/22H; CIS 22B/22BH, 22C/22CH) - credit for only one version.

Systems Programming A.A. (degree_type local_computing): pulled from https://deanza.elumenapp.com/catalog/2025-2026/program/systems-programming-fall-2025v2 (this is the A.A. Degree; a separate COA-A version exists at systems-programming-fall-2025). This is the closest LOCAL computing associate degree (De Anza offers no local CS A.S.; the CIS A.A. degrees are Business Programming, Cybersecurity, Database Development Practitioner, Network Administration, Network Programming, and Systems Programming). Three groups: Required Core 32 units (all); List A 4-5 units (choose one course); List B 4.5 units (choose one course). Major total 40.5-41.5 units. GE = De Anza GE or Cal-GETC pattern; must also complete ENGL C1000/C1000H or ESL 5 with C or higher; electives to reach 90. Honors alternates: CIS 22B/22BH, 26B/26BH, 22C/22CH. Both program pages carry a footnote 'Revised on Oct. 1, 2025, to align with current requirements.'
