extraction.notes:
Source: Diablo Valley College official eLumen catalog, 2025-2026 (current). WebFetch returned empty for these pages because the eLumen catalog is fully JavaScript-rendered; I loaded them in the browser pane and transcribed directly, so extraction is complete and needs_browser is false.

DEGREES FOUND (both are genuine CS degrees with COMSC programming courses):

1) ast = "A.S. in Computer Science for Transfer" (A.S. Degree for Transfer). Catalog headlines 60 units total; requires 60 CSU-transferable units, Cal-GETC GE pattern, min 2.0 GPA, grade C/P or higher in the major. Major = a single 34-unit "Required core" (all 8 courses required, no electives/choose-lists). Statewide ADT text also states "minimum of 18 units in the major," but DVC's actual required core is 34 units. Course codes printed concatenated (e.g., COMSC165) — split into prefix/number. Note BIOSC130 (Biology) and PHYS130 (Physics) are part of the required core.

2) local_cs_as = "A.S. in Computer Science" (A.S. Degree Local). The catalog program page headlines "20.0 units," which is the MAJOR-requirement total only (16 required + 4 choose = 20), NOT the full degree. I recorded total_units = 60 because every California/DVC associate degree requires a minimum of 60 semester units (major + local GE + electives per DVC graduation requirements). Major = 16 units all-required (COMSC110/165/210/260) PLUS "at least 4 units from" a 2-course choose_units group (COMSC200 Object Oriented Programming C++ OR COMSC256 Advanced Java Programming). GE follows DVC's local associate-degree GE pattern (not enumerated). Catalog reminds students intending to transfer to consult a counselor about additional math/science.

local_computing NOT listed separately: the earlier survey's "closest local computing degree = Computer Science" is the SAME degree as local_cs_as above — DVC's local computing associate is literally named "Computer Science." To avoid double-counting I did not emit a duplicate local_computing entry; if the framework requires one, it is identical to the local_cs_as record above (same courses, same URL).

DVC is a semester-system college (unit_system = semester). Local A.S. program slug contains "fall-2023" (last revision date) but it lives inside the 2025-2026 catalog. The AS-T page carries a note that it was revised after initial catalog publication; I captured the updated version. Nothing fabricated — all prefixes, numbers, titles, and units are transcribed verbatim from the live catalog pages.

Authoritative source: ELAC 2025-2026 General Catalog PDF (current, semester units): https://www.elac.edu/sites/elac.edu/files/2025-09/Catalog-2025-2026.pdf. The originally-supplied page (www.elac.edu/.../computer-science) and the ELAC programmapper pages both return HTTP 403 to fetch tools; the catalog PDF is fully fetchable, so needs_browser=false.

CS AS-T DOES NOT EXIST AT ELAC (survey claim 'transfer AS-T in CS = true' is NOT supported). The catalog's master 'State-Approved Associate Degrees and Certificates' table (pp.20-21) enumerates every ELAC degree; the only STEM-CAP AS-T degrees are Biology, Environmental Science, Geology, Mathematics, and Physics (plus AS-T Public Health, Early Childhood Education, Nutrition/Dietetics elsewhere). No 'Computer Science for Transfer', no 'Associate in Science in Computer Science', and no CS ADT appears anywhere in the catalog. A 'Computer Science (AS-T)' PDF that appears in web search belongs to LA Harbor College (lahc.edu) — a DIFFERENT LACCD college — and was deliberately excluded per the anchor-to-this-college rule. Therefore no 'ast' degree object is returned (not fabricated).

The only Computer Science degree at ELAC is the LOCAL 'Computer Science Information Technology, Associate in Arts Degree' (Academic Plan E002440C), housed in the Business Administration Department, catalog p.123. It is an A.A. (award A.A.), total 60 semester units. Structure: (1) Required core = COMPLETE THE FOLLOWING 9 COURSES (27 units, all required); (2) COMPLETE NINE UNITS FROM THE FOLLOWING (choose 9 units of electives); (3) LACCD GENERAL EDUCATION PLAN (27 units). No OR options within the major. Catalog note: 'CIS 111 may be double counted in LACCD GE Area 2' — this double-counting is why 27 core + 9 electives + 27 GE (=63) resolves to the printed 60-unit total. Course titles/units transcribed exactly as printed; CIS 192 is printed 'Introduction to Cloud Computering' (a catalog typo) and recorded here with the correct title 'Introduction to Cloud Computing'. All 21 required/elective courses include CS/programming courses (CS 111/113/116/130 plus CS electives), satisfying the CS-content sanity check.

extraction.notes:
Source: Taft College official SmartCatalog IQ, 2025-2026 catalog (taftcollege.smartcatalogiq.com). Anchored to Taft College only. Requirement tables on this catalog are JS/JSON-rendered, so WebFetch could not read them; I used the browser pane to transcribe.

CS AS-T (ast): Fully captured from the degree page /computer-science-for-transfer/associate-in-science. Printed heading "Required Core: (31-32 Units)". Verbatim printed order was ENGR1540(4), COSC1545(4), COSC2100(3), COSC2000(3), MATH2100(5), MATH2120(4), PHYS2221(4), PHYS2222(4), OR, BIOL2201(4), OR, CHEM2211(5), Total 31-32. Interpretation: ENGR1540 + COSC1545 + COSC2100 + COSC2000 + MATH2100 + MATH2120 + PHYS2221 are all required (=27 units); then ONE science course is chosen from {PHYS2222 (4) / BIOL2201 (4) / CHEM2211 (5)}. This is the ONLY inference and the 31-32 unit range confirms it exactly (27+4=31 or 27+5=32). GE = "Completion of the California General Education Transfer Curriculum (Cal-GETC) is required." Additional transferable electives (courses numbered 1500 or higher) fill to the 60-unit degree total. Course contains real CS/programming courses (intro programming, programming methods II, discrete structures, computer architecture), so this is the correct CS program. Confidence 0.85 (units/titles directly transcribed; only the science-OR grouping is inferred, and the math corroborates it).

Advanced Information Technology and Management (local_computing, A.S.): The A.S. DEGREE exists — the main program page (/degrees-and-certificates/information-technology-and-management) states "Upon the successful completion of the required courses and general education requirements with a minimum 2.0 G.P.A., an associate in science degree will be awarded." However, the DEGREE page has NO course table, and the requirements are only on the certificate subpage (cited as catalog_url), which is STALE/incompletely migrated. The rendered "Required Courses" showed only: an accounting choice — BUSN 1051 General Accounting (4.00) OR BSAD 2220 Introduction to Financial Accounting (4.00); an "INCO 1548 ... Computer Science elective" line (prints "2-3" units — I recorded 2 as the numeric floor); and a NOTE reading "COSC 1603, COSC 1703, COSC 1812, COSC 1902: Note: Previous versions of required computer courses taken within four (4) years of certificate completion will be accepted." Certificate "Total Credit Hours: 13.5-15.5." I did NOT include COSC 1603/1703/1812/1902 as courses because they are discontinued numbers with NO title or units shown in the current catalog — the live COSC discipline (course-description/cosc-computer-science) only contains COSC 1545, 2000, 2100, 2400, and there is no INCO prefix in the current course-description index (computing prefixes are CIS and COSC). So the current required computer courses for this program are not cleanly enumerated anywhere in the 2025-2026 catalog. The A.S. degree total is the CA minimum 60 semester units (= these ~13.5-15.5 major units + local/CSU GE + electives). I set needs_browser=true and confidence 0.3 for THIS degree so it can be human-verified; the CS AS-T is solid. The former official taftcollege.edu/.php program pages now 404.

extraction.notes:
Source of truth: AVC official counseling program sheets (2025-26), linked from https://www.avc.edu/counseling/major-requirements-program-sheets and from the program page https://www.avc.edu/program/computer-science-cs. AVC offers exactly TWO Computer Science associate degrees: (1) a local Associate of Science in Computer Science, and (2) a Computer Science for Transfer AS-T. The survey's "closest local computing degree named Computer Science" is identical to the local CS A.S. — there is no separate third degree. I therefore output the same Computer Science A.S. under BOTH degree_type=local_cs_as and degree_type=local_computing; they are one and the same physical degree (same PDF, same requirements). All three entries were extracted from fetchable AVC-hosted PDFs; needs_browser=false.

IMPORTANT anti-error checks passed: All sources are on the avc.edu domain (this exact college). I did NOT use catalog.valleycollege.edu (that is San Bernardino Valley College) nor careerprofiles.info. Catalog year is current (2025-2026). Both degrees contain proper CS/programming courses (CS 120/121/122 Programming & Algorithms, CS 130/131/132 Data Structures, CS 140 Assembly/Architecture, CS 150 Discrete Structures), confirming correct programs.

CAVEAT: The local A.S. Computer Science program sheet is stamped "(DRAFT)" (Rev. 6/24/25); it is the current official 2025-26 sheet published on AVC's program-sheets page but is labeled draft, so confidence is slightly reduced. The AS-T sheet is dated 7/15/25 and not marked draft.

PROGRAMMING LANGUAGE GROUP (both degrees): "select ONE of the following program language groupings" worth 6 units. Each grouping is a matched PAIR of courses in one language: C/C++ = CS 120 + CS 130; Java = CS 121 + CS 131; Python = CS 122 + CS 132. I modeled this as rule=choose_units (units_min 6) and listed all six courses; the real constraint is that you take BOTH courses of exactly one language (not any two of the six). Each course is 3 units (unit value taken from the recommended-pathway grid; the sheet prints only the 6-unit pair total). Course title for CS 120/121/122 on the sheet is truncated ("programming and Algorithms in ...") — I completed the language from the grouping header.

UNIT DISCREPANCY (as printed, kept faithfully): CS 150 Discrete Structures is printed as 3 units on the local A.S. sheet but 4 units on the AS-T sheet. Total-major-units header on the AS-T sheet reads "18" but the listed courses sum to ~29-30 (18 appears to be the state minimum-in-major floor, not the actual sum). Local A.S. header reads "Total Units Required for the Major 27-31".

TOTAL_UNITS: recorded as the degree minimum (local A.S. total degree units printed 60-64 → recorded 60; AS-T total degree units printed 61-62 → recorded 61).

GENERAL EDUCATION: Local A.S. follows AVC LOCAL GE (21 semester units minimum, Areas 1-6); AS-T follows Cal-GETC (California General Education Transfer Curriculum, Areas 1-6, effective Fall 2025). GE course lists were not enumerated per instructions; a single high-level ge_area group is recorded for each.

Source: Feather River College 2024-2025 General Catalog (current catalog; FRC's newest published edition as of 2026-07). Extracted from the official catalog PDF, "Office Technology - A.S." on catalog pp. 114-115. Verified PDF mirror I actually downloaded/read: https://campussuite-storage.s3.amazonaws.com/prod/1558523/0672826e-a84b-11e7-9779-0ae3e1d9783c/2977531/c0720a90-56b7-11f0-b66d-0a58a9feac02/file/Catalog%202024-25.pdf (identical file also at the frc.edu URL given in catalog_url). The live CurriQunet catalog (frc.curriqunet.com/Catalog) is JavaScript-only and returned no content via fetch, so I used the college's own PDF catalog instead — no browser needed.

Degree identity: Office Technology is FRC's Information Communication and Technologies (ICT) office/business-focused associate degree. FRC has NO local CS A.S. and NO AS-T in Computer Science, matching the prior survey; Office Technology is the closest local computing degree (degree_type local_computing). It legitimately contains computing/IT coursework (BUS 111 Intro to Information Technology, BUS 131 Computer Information Systems, ICT 101 Computer Literacy, ART 162 Web Design) plus business/accounting — consistent with an office-technology program rather than a programming/CS program.

OR options (embedded in the "all-required" core): BUS 102 Financial Accounting OR BUS 132 Bookkeeping (both 3.0) — one required. I listed both course options in the core group; only one is taken. There is no OR in the electives group.

Unit accounting: The catalog prints "Major Core Requirements 18.0-19.0" but the six listed core line-items sum to exactly 18.0 (with the one OR choice); the ~1-unit range appears to be a catalog rounding/typo — I recorded units exactly as printed. Major total printed as "Total: 30.0-31.0" is MAJOR-ONLY (core 18-19 + electives 12). The degree total is the college-wide minimum of 60.0 semester units (catalog p. ~ line: "Complete a minimum of 60 semester units"); I set total_units=60 accordingly, with GE and free electives making up the remainder beyond the ~30-unit major.

General Education: Not enumerated per instructions. The ICT program text directs degree-seeking/transfer students to complete "the campus general education requirements" and to follow CSU GE-Breadth or IGETC (now Cal-GETC) patterns per the catalog's Graduation and Transfer section. Recorded as a single high-level ge_area group.

No fabricated codes/units; all prefixes, numbers, titles, and units transcribed verbatim from the 2024-25 catalog. The Office Technology Certificate of Achievement (18.0 units) exists on the same page but is a certificate, not a degree, and was excluded.

Source: Hartnell College 2024-2025 Degrees and Certificates catalog PDF (https://www.hartnell.edu/academics-affairs/docs/catalog/degreesandct_2425_catalog_rev.pdf). Extracted via pdftotext -layout; fully transcribed, so needs_browser=false. Unit system is semester; both associate degrees total 60 units.

IMPORTANT classification note: Hartnell has NO standalone generic "Computer Science" A.S. The only local CS-focused associate degree is "Computer Science and Information Systems—Computer Science Option" (program code AS.CISCS, Associate of Science, catalog page 135-136). This SINGLE degree serves as BOTH the "local Computer Science A.S." the survey flagged true AND the survey's named "closest local computing degree" — they are the same physical degree, not two. I have therefore output that one degree twice (once as degree_type local_cs_as and once as local_computing) with identical requirements so each requested slot is populated; downstream should treat them as ONE degree to avoid double-counting. Other CS codes in the catalog are certificates, not degrees: CT.CISC (CS Option Certificate of Achievement, 28 units), CT.CCN (Cisco/CCNA), CT.CS (Cybersecurity).

Degree details:
1) COMPUTER SCIENCE FOR TRANSFER (AST.CS), A.S.-T, page 133-134. Required Major Courses = 33 units, listed as a fixed set of 8 courses (no OR options printed): CSS-2A (4), CSS-2B (4), CSS-3 (4), CSS-7 (4), MAT-3A (4), MAT-3B (4), PHY-4A (4), BIO-1 (5). GE: IGETC = 37 units (IGETC is the only GE plan printed for the ADT). Electives (courses numbered 1-99) required only when major units + GE units total fewer than 60. TOTAL 60 units. C-or-better required in all major courses (standard ADT rule).

2) COMPUTER SCIENCE AND INFORMATION SYSTEMS—COMPUTER SCIENCE OPTION (AS.CISCS), A.S., page 135-136. Required Major Courses = 20 units (all required, no options): CSS-1 (4), CSS-2A (4), CSS-2B (4), CSS-3 (4), CSS-7 (4). GE: complete ONE of CSU-GE (39 units) OR IGETC (37 units). Electives (courses numbered 1-199) required only when major + GE units total fewer than 60. TOTAL 60 units.

Both degrees contain real CSS programming/CS core courses (satisfies the CS-content check). Course titles/units transcribed exactly as printed. High confidence — clean direct transcription from the current official catalog PDF.

Source: official IVC catalog on Modern Campus Catalog (acalog), catoid=35 = "2024-2025 Catalog: September 2024 Edition" — the newest catalog available in acalog. IVC's 2025-2026 and 2026-2027 editions are hosted on a separate CurriquNet system; 2024-2025 is within the accepted range (>=2024) so I used it as authoritative. Data pulled from the print-friendly program pages via browser (acalog pages are JS-rendered and not fetchable by plain WebFetch). needs_browser set false because I successfully transcribed both degrees.

DEGREE COUNT: Only two distinct degrees exist. The survey's "closest local computing degree = Computer Science" (degree_type local_computing) is the SAME physical degree as the local CS A.S. (Computer Science, AS, poid=7974). I did NOT emit a separate local_computing entry to avoid double-counting; treat local_computing as identical to the local_cs_as entry below.

TOTAL UNITS: The catalog prints only the MAJOR requirement units ("Total Units Required: 24" for the local AS, "30" for the AS-T). I set total_units=60 to reflect the CA associate-degree minimum (major units + GE/electives). Major-only units: local AS = 24, AS-T = 30.

AS-T course-group logic (single catalog heading "Complete the following courses:", I split it into logical groups): (a) required CS 38 + CS 41 + CS 40A; (b) Discrete Math select one of CS 6A / MATH 6A (these are cross-listed = the same course, credit in one area only); (c) Calculus I select one of MATH 3A / MATH 3AH(honors); (d) Calculus II select one of MATH 3B / MATH 3BH(honors); (e) Physics/lab-science: take PHYS 4A AND PHYS 4B (the 8-unit path that reaches the printed 30 total), OR one of BIO 16 / BIO 80 / BIO 80H / BIO 81 / BIO 81H / CHEM 1A / CHEM 1AH. The catalog lists PHYS 4A and 4B together as a pair, then "OR" a single bio/chem course; only the physics pair mathematically reaches the stated 30-unit total, so I flagged group (e) as choose (see courses) with this ambiguity noted.

Local CS AS logic: required CS 1 (or CS 1H honors) + CS 10 + CS 37 + CS 38 + CS 40A + CS 41 (18 units), plus "Complete any two of the following" (6 units) from CS 6A / CS 6B / CS 30 / CS 36 / CS 39 / CS 40B / CS 50 = 24 major units. This local AS has no math/physics inside the major (those come via GE/transfer prep). It clearly contains CS/programming courses, so it is the correct program.

GE: not enumerated. Both are 60-unit associate degrees; the AS-T follows the CSU GE Breadth or Cal-GETC transfer GE pattern per SB 1440 / TMC in Computer Science; the local AS follows IVC's associate-degree GE pattern. Course titles recorded exactly as printed (uppercase in the catalog).

extraction.notes:
Source: Porterville College 2025-2026 Catalog PDF, printed page 82 (PDF page 83), "Computer Information Systems, Associate in Science". Downloaded and transcribed from https://www.portervillecollege.edu/_resources/assets/pdfs/Academics/Catalog/2025-2026_Catalog_Updated_Meta_Data.pdf via pdftotext. The catalog uses a two-column layout; the CIS core begins at the bottom of the left column (BSAD P132, INFS P052, INFS P100) and continues at the top of the right column (INFS P110, P113, P170, P180, P210, P220). Verified the prior/next pages have no competing INFS list, so the continuation unambiguously belongs to CIS. The same 9-course, 28-unit core is independently corroborated by the college's CE program page (https://www.portervillecollege.edu/programs-and-classes/ce/ce-programs/information-systems.html).

This is the correct program: it contains genuine CS/programming courses (INFS P113 Structured Programming, INFS P052 Web Design, INFS P180 Database Management, INFS P100 Intro to Information Systems), not just math/physics.

The degree's own "Effective Summer 2017" stamp appears at the end of the listing, but it is the current requirement set printed in the 2025-2026 catalog. The college offers NO local CS A.S. and NO CS AS-T; CIS is the closest local computing degree, matching the prior survey.

Requirements structure: (1) Required Core Courses (28 units) — all 9 courses required, minimum grade "C" in each major course, overall GPA 2.0. (2) General Education — students complete ONE pattern: PC General Education Pattern, IGETC Pattern, or CSU General Education Pattern (catalog does not fix a GE unit count for this specific degree). (3) Electives as needed to reach the 60-unit minimum. Total degree = 60 semester units minimum (semester system). No OR/choose-one options within the CIS major core; every listed course is required.

NOTE on avoided error: WebSearch surfaced a same-named "Computer Information Systems" program at Portland Community College (Oregon); I did NOT use it. All data is from Porterville College's own .edu/.biz catalog domain.

Source: LASC 2024-2025 College Catalog PDF (fetched via curl with browser UA; WebFetch returned 403 but curl succeeded; text extracted with pdftotext -layout). Two CS-related LOCAL associate degrees exist at LASC; there is NO standalone plain "Computer Science" A.S.

1) local_cs_as = "Mathematics: Computer Science". CAVEAT/DISCREPANCY: the program detail page (catalog p.110) heads this degree "ASSOCIATE IN ARTS IN MATHEMATICS: COMPUTER SCIENCE" (award A.A.), but the program-availability chart (catalog p.94) lists "Mathematics: Computer Science  AS  S008450C" (award A.S.). I recorded award as A.A. to match the exact printed degree title on the requirements page, but the summary chart says AS — parent should reconcile. This is really a mathematics degree with a CS emphasis: required core is CS 111 Beginning BASIC Programming (3) plus four MATH courses (Calc I/II/III + Linear Algebra), 21 major units. It passes the "must contain a programming course" check via CS 111 only. It is the only CS-named local associate degree at LASC other than the Information Technology one, so it maps to local_cs_as. Extraction confidence moderate due to award conflict and math-heavy composition.

2) local_computing = "Computer Science: Information Technology". Award A.S. (agreed by both the program page p.108, "ASSOCIATE IN SCIENCE", under department heading "Computer Information Science", and the chart: "Computer Science: Information Technology  AS  S002866C"). Required core = 9 courses (CIS + CS), 27 major units, clearly a computing/programming degree. High confidence.

UNITS: Both program blocks print only the MAJOR-requirement subtotals (21 and 27 units). The catalog's "Associate Degree Requirements at LASC" (p.95-96) states the degree requires "A minimum of 60 semester units... with at least 18 semester units in a major or area of emphasis and at least 18 semester units in general education." I set total_units = 60 (degree total / CA minimum) for both; major subtotals are captured in the required-courses group units_min (21 / 27).

GE: LASC offers three GE plans (LACCD General Education Plan, CSU GE-Breadth, or IGETC), minimum 18 semester units; recorded as a high-level ge_area group per degree, not enumerated.

CS 111 note: the catalog prints CS 111 with two different titles — "Programming in Visual Basic" in the CS:IT degree block and "Beginning BASIC Programming" in the Math:CS block; each is recorded exactly as printed in its respective degree.

Recommended Electives: Math:CS lists a clear Recommended Electives block (captured as an electives group). CS:IT's recommended-electives list could not be attributed with confidence because of two-column PDF interleaving with an adjacent certificate, so it was omitted (recommended electives are not degree requirements anyway).

extraction.notes:
Source: San Bernardino Valley College official catalog, 2026-2027 (catalog.valleycollege.edu). All content transcribed from fetchable HTML pages; no browser needed.

DEGREE MAPPING: SBVC's local computing associate degree that is closest to (and named) "Computer Science" IS the local Computer Science A.S. itself. Therefore degree_type local_cs_as and degree_type local_computing point to the SAME degree (Computer Science Associate of Science Degree) and carry identical requirements. The distinct third degree is the Computer Science Associate in Science for Transfer (AS-T).

LOCAL COMPUTER SCIENCE A.S. (local_cs_as / local_computing):
- "Required Courses:" total exactly 33 units as printed ("Total Units 33"). This is the MAJOR requirement total only; General Education is required separately and is not itemized on the page. I set degree total_units to 60 (the California associate-degree minimum); the true printed figure for the major block is 33 units. The 9 required slots sum to 33 (3+4+4+3+4+3+4+4+4).
- OR options within the required block: CS 102 "Introduction to Python Programming" OR CS 102H (honors equivalent); and CS 190 "Programming in C++" OR CS 215 "Programming with Java" (genuine choose-one). I listed CS 102 and CS 190 as the slot representatives and note the alternatives here.
- CS 160 "Introduction to Data Science and Engineering" (4 units) is listed as a "Recommended Course:" — NOT required — so it is excluded from the required group.
- GE: page states "To earn an SBVC Associate Degree students must complete one of the following General Education (GE) patterns: SBVC General Education and Graduation Requirements, or Cal-GETC." Unit count not itemized on the page.

COMPUTER SCIENCE AS-T (ast):
- Total Units 60 (printed). Major Total Units 29-30; Total Double-Counted Units 10; Cal-GETC GE 34 units; Electives 6-7 units.
- Uses a single "Required Courses:" block plus "One course from the following:" science elective (choose 1 of PHYSIC 203 / BIOL 205 / BIOL 206 / CHEM 150). This SBVC page does not use the "List A / List B" labels seen in some CS TMC templates.
- OR option: CS 265 "Data Structures and Algorithms with C++" OR CS 265H (honors equivalent, 3 units). Listed CS 265 as representative.
- Statewide C-ID/common-numbered courses appear as printed: MATH C2210 (Calculus I: Early Transcendentals), MATH C2220 (Calculus II: Early Transcendentals). Physics uses SBVC prefix "PHYSIC".

Both degrees contain multiple programming/CS courses (CS 077/100/102/104/110/120/190/220 locally; CS 110/130/170/265 in the AS-T), satisfying the "must contain CS/programming" check. No course codes or units were fabricated; all values transcribed verbatim from the two degree pages.

extraction.notes:
SINGLE DEGREE, TWO ROLES: The survey listed both a local CS A.S. and a "closest local computing degree = Computer Science Associate in Science Degree." These are the SAME degree — Fullerton offers exactly one such associate degree in this space. It is a genuine CS degree (contains CSCI programming courses), so it is reported once with degree_type local_cs_as. No distinct local_computing degree exists; if the dataset requires a local_computing row, it would be identical to this entry. No AS-T in CS at Fullerton (confirmed by survey).

SOURCE: Official NOCCCD catalog, 2025-2026, Fullerton College. Degree page fetched (3x, consistent): https://catalog.nocccd.edu/fullerton-college/degrees-certificates/computer-science/computer-science-associate-science-degree/ . Per-course units cross-checked against the catalog course-description pages (/course-descriptions/math/ and /course-descriptions/csci/).

TOTAL UNITS: The degree page prints only the MAJOR block: "In addition to other graduation requirements, this degree requires 22-24 units, of which 12 units are in required course and an additional 10-12 units are chosen from the restricted elective lists." total_units is set to 60 (the standard California associate-degree minimum this degree must meet); the full 60 = 22-24 major units + local/CSU GE + free electives. The 60 figure is the CA minimum, NOT a number printed on this page. GENERAL EDUCATION is not enumerated on the degree page; it follows Fullerton's local GE / CSU GE / Cal-GETC pattern (not transcribed).

OR-OPTIONS / RULES:
- Required Courses (12 units, rule=all): CSCI 123 F (4), CSCI 133 F (4), MATH 152 F Calculus II (4). MATH 152 F has an OR alternative: MATH 152HF "Honors Calculus II" (4 units) — either satisfies the requirement (honors alternative recorded here in notes, not duplicated as a course row).
- Choose one course pair (6-8 units, rule=choose_units, units_min=6): complete EXACTLY ONE of three pairs — (A) MATH 171 F + MATH 172 F = 7 units; (B) MATH 170 F + MATH 255 F = 8 units; (C) MATH 171 F + MATH 253 F = 6 units. MATH 171 F appears in pairs A and C but is listed only once in the courses array (deduped). Individual course units confirmed: MATH 171 F=4, MATH 172 F=3, MATH 170 F=5, MATH 255 F=3, MATH 253 F=2 — these reconcile exactly with the printed pair totals 7/8/6.
- Restricted Electives (4 units, rule=choose_courses, choose_n=1): complete ONE of CSCI 223 F or CSCI 241 F (each 4 units).

"FORMERLY" NOTES as printed: MATH 152 F "(formerly MATH 150BF)"; MATH 253 F "(formerly MATH 250CF)". Course suffix "F" is Fullerton's campus designator; honors sections use "HF" (e.g. 152HF). No fabricated codes or units.

LBCC offers exactly ONE associate degree named "Computer Science": the Computer Science - Associate in Science (Plan Code 2119). It contains genuine CS/programming courses (CS 11/21/31, CS 22, CS 51, CS 61) plus calculus and physics, satisfying the CS-content check. The earlier survey's "closest local computing degree = Computer Science" refers to THIS SAME degree — there is no separate/distinct local computing associate program at LBCC. Catalog source: The live authoritative catalog page (https://lbcc-public.courseleaf.com/degrees-certificates/computer-science/computer-science-as/) is JavaScript-rendered and returned empty via WebFetch; the Chrome browser extension was not connected. Also present in the same discipline (NOT the A.S. degree, excluded): Computer Science - Certificate of Achievement (Plan 3119, 32 units, same core) and Android App Developer - Certificate of Accomplishment (Plan 4119, 6 units) — these are certificates, not associate degrees.

The choice is between a COMPLETE group, not an arbitrary mix — Group 1 = CS 20A Data Structures with C++ (3) + CS 52 C++ Programming (3); Group 2 = CS 20B Data Structures with Java (3) + CS 55 Java Programming (3). I encoded this as rule=choose_courses with choose_n=2 / units_min=6 listing all four options; the intended constraint is one full group (both courses of a single group).

IMPORTANT: at Southwestern College the lower-division computer-science courses carry the MATH prefix (MATH 130 Introduction to Computer Programming, MATH 140 Data Structures and Algorithms, MATH 230 Computer Organization and Architecture, MATH 265 Discrete Structures) — so the extracted cores DO contain genuine programming/CS courses even though the prefixes read MATH; this is not a wrong-program error.

Two Computer Science associate degrees exist and were extracted: (1) the transfer AS-T "Computer Science" (Associate in Science Transfer Preparation, Major Code 01185) and (2) the local A.A. "Computer Science" (Associate in Arts Transfer Preparation, Major Code 01180). Note the survey said local CS A.S.=false — correct: the local computing degree is an A.A., not an A.S. There is no local CS A.S. This A.A. is the closest local computing degree named "Computer Science".

AS-T: 33-unit Required Core printed as a single "all-required" section (no List A/List B subdivision on the catalog page). Footnote states verbatim: "PHYS 272 PHYS 273 or CHEM 200 may be substituted for BIOL 211," with a further note that students transferring to SDSU must take PHYS 272 + PHYS 273 in place of BIOL 211 (a full year of physics). GE follows Cal-GETC. total_units set to 60 (the stated minimum for the AS-T with Cal-GETC); the major Required Core itself is 33 units.

A.A. (local): printed plan-of-study "Total Units" = 31 for the major core (MATH 130, C2210, 140, C2220, 252, 230, 254, 265). Then a "Recommended Electives" section instructs "Select one of the following: 6-12" — i.e. choose ONE science sequence: Physics (PHYS 270 + 272 + 274), Chemistry (CHEM 200 + 210), or Biology (BIOL 210 + 211 + 212). Per-course units for the elective sequences were not printed on the degree page, so I pulled them verbatim from the catalog course-description pages (PHYS 270=4, 272=4, 274=4; CHEM 200=5, 210=5; BIOL 210=4, 211=4, 212=4). total_units set to 60 (CA associate minimum with GE); the printed major core is 31 units. GE follows local SWC/CSU GE + graduation requirements (catalog did not enumerate a specific GE unit total on the degree page).

No course codes or units were fabricated; all units are as printed in the catalog. Confidence is high for both cores; slightly lower for the exact GE unit totals since the degree pages only reference the pattern rather than printing a GE unit line.

DEGREE 1 — Computer Science AS-T (ast): FULLY extracted from the CURRENT official Norco degree sheet 2025-2026 (Cal-GETC), program code NAS650, stamped 'NF 5/30/25'. URL: https://www.norcocollege.edu/articulation/documents/2025-2026/2025-2026_computer_science_as-t.pdf . Major = 'Required Courses (29 units)': CSC/CIS-5 (4), CSC/CIS-7 (3), CSC/CIS-11 (3), CSC/CIS-17A (3), MAT-1A/1AH (4), MAT-1B (4), PHY-4A (4), PHY-4B (4). Awarded on completion of 60 CSU-transferable units including the major + Cal-GETC, GPA 2.0, all major courses grade C/P or better. Courses printed as combined 'CSC/CIS' prefix (cross-listed). MAT and PHY courses double-count toward Cal-GETC (marked * on sheet). This matches the older 2024-2025 IGETC version (same 29-unit major) at https://www.norcocollege.edu/articulation/documents/2024-2025/2024-2025-computer-science-as-t.pdf . High confidence; no browser needed for this degree.

DEGREE 2 — Computer Information Systems: Computer Programming AS (local_computing): course list sourced from the official Norco CIS 'Computer Programming' program flyer (School of STEM), which is stamped '21/22 Course Catalog', program codes NAS728/NAS728B/NAS728C (degree) / NCE728 (cert). URL used: https://www.norcocollege.edu/academicaffairs/cte/documents/stem/cis_computer_programming.pdf (identical copy also at /academicaffairs/cte/documents/mecsgd/cis_computer_programming.pdf). Flyer states '26.5 unit certificate • 60 unit degree' and 'An Associate of Science Degree in Computer Programming will be awarded upon completion of the required courses (26.5 units) plus the General Education requirements.' Major structure: Required core (CIS-1A 3, CIS/CSC-2 3, CIS/CSC-5 4, CIS-21 3, CIS-72A 1.5 = 14.5u) + 12 elective units (choose two 3-unit courses from Group One AND two from Group Two). 14.5 + 12 = 26.5, internally consistent. AS = these 26.5 major units + GE (student picks RCCD GE ~24u / CSU GE 39u / IGETC 34-37u per the 2024-2025 RCCD Associate Degree Requirements) + electives to reach 60 semester units.

WHY needs_browser=true: The ONLY detailed major-course source for the local Computer Programming AS is the 21/22 flyer (older than the 2024 cutoff). The CURRENT catalog is the 2026-2027 Norco catalog hosted on CurriQunet META (https://rccd.curriqunet.com/catalog/alias/nc-catalog , Degrees & Certificates section iq/5367/5470), which is a JavaScript-only app — WebFetch returns only nav chrome, no program content — and the Claude-in-Chrome extension was not connected this session. The RCCD 2024-2025 Associate Degree Requirements PDF (https://www.norcocollege.edu/articulation/documents/norco-aa-degree-2024-2025.pdf) confirms the AS-in-CTE framework and that all the flyer's CIS/CSC course numbers (5, 11, 17A, 17B, 17C, 18A, 18B, 18C, etc.) are still current active courses, but does not enumerate this specific program's major list. RECOMMEND a browser pass over the CurriQunet 2026-2027 catalog to confirm the current Computer Programming AS major course list and total; the flyer data is the best available and is very likely still accurate but is pre-2024. No fabricated codes/units — all values transcribed exactly as printed.

extraction.notes:
SOURCE: Official Clovis Community College (California) catalog 2025-2026 PDF, https://www.cloviscollege.edu/catalog/assets/docs/catalog-2025-2026.pdf (289 pages, HTTP 200 via curl; WebFetch returned 403). Degree found on catalog p.104 (text lines ~6189-6243). ANTI-ERROR: Web search surfaced clovis.edu links which are Clovis Community College in NEW MEXICO — a different same-named college — and were NOT used; only the cloviscollege.edu (California) catalog was used.

ONE DISTINCT DEGREE: The local Computer Science A.S. (local_cs_as) and the "closest local computing degree = Computer Science" (local_computing) both resolve to the SAME single degree: Computer Science, Associate in Science (Major #C.6920.AS). It is recorded once as local_cs_as to avoid double-counting. There is no separate/second computing degree named Computer Science.

LAYOUT CAVEAT: The catalog prints Computer Science (left column) and Data Science (right column, Major #C.6921.AS) side-by-side; pdftotext interleaved them. I carefully separated the columns — Computer Science uses CSCI 26 (Discrete Math); Data Science uses CSCI 25. Data Science is a distinct degree and is NOT included here.

UNIT TOTALS: Catalog prints "Total Units for the Major: 20". total_units is set to 60 = the overall associate-degree minimum (major 20 units + local GE/Cal-GETC + electives to reach 60 semester units). Confirmed 60-unit minimum from Graduation Requirements section (line ~5241 "total of 60 units for the degree" and multiple "Completion of 60 semester units" references).

MAJOR STRUCTURE: (1) Required Core = CSCI 40 + CSCI 41 (both required, 8 units). (2) "Select an additional 12 units" (choose_units, 12) from a menu that includes two OR pairs: PHYS 2A OR PHYS 4A (pick one), and PHYS 2B OR PHYS 4B (pick one). Recommended courses listed by catalog: MATH 5A, 5B; PHYS 2A, 2B. Advisor: Kerney, Nichols.

COURSE VERIFICATION: All titles/units cross-checked against the CSCI course-description section: CSCI 26 Discrete Mathematics for Computer Science (4u, C-ID COMP 152), CSCI 40 Programming Concepts and Methodology I (4u, C-ID COMP 112/122), CSCI 41 Programming Concepts and Methodology II (4u), CSCI 45 Computer Organization and Assembly Language Programming (4u, C-ID COMP 142). Programming/CS courses are present (passes CS-content check). MATH 5A (5u), MATH 5B (4u), PHYS courses (4u each) taken from the degree table.

GE: Clovis offers local GE (Areas 1-7, per Title 5 §55061, including Area 7 Physical Activity) or Cal-GETC; GE not enumerated per instructions. No AS-T in Computer Science exists at Clovis (survey confirmed transfer AS-T = false).

extraction.notes:
SOURCE: 2024-2025 Madera Community College catalog PDF (the exact authoritative source URL provided), catalog page 99 (PDF page 101), heading "COMPUTER SCIENCE (MAJOR #M.6920.AS) / ASSOCIATE IN SCIENCE DEGREE". WebFetch returned HTTP 403 on the PDF; I downloaded it via curl and extracted text with pdftotext, and confirmed the layout against a rendered image of page 99 (two-column page; the CS program spans the left column into the top of the right column, above the COUNSELING/CSU GE section).

SINGLE DEGREE COVERS BOTH SLOTS: Madera offers exactly one associate degree named "Computer Science" (an A.S., Major #M.6920.AS). It IS the local CS A.S. AND it is the closest local computing degree named Computer Science, so the two requested slots (local_cs_as and local_computing) collapse to the same degree. I report it once as degree_type local_cs_as rather than duplicating an identical record. Consistent with the survey: no AS-T in CS exists (this program is a local A.S., not an AS-T; it lists Major #M.6920.AS and is under "Associate Degree and Certificate Programs", not the transfer-degree section).

TOTAL UNITS: The catalog prints "Total Units 20" for this program, which is the MAJOR total only (Computer Science Core 12 units + "Select additional units" 8 units = 20). It does NOT print a full degree total on this page. I set total_units=60 = the standard California associate-degree minimum (major + local associate-degree GE + electives to reach 60 semester units). The 20 printed major units are captured in the two major_groups below.

OR OPTIONS in the "Select additional units from the following courses:" group (choose 8 units): PHYS 2A (General Physics I) OR PHYS 4A (Physics for Scientists and Engineers), each 4 units; and PHYS 2B (General Physics II) OR PHYS 4B (Physics for Scientists and Engineers), each 4 units. All four PHYS entries are listed as options within this single 8-unit selection group along with CSCI 5, CSCI 45, MATH 5A, MATH 5B.

RECOMMENDED (NOT REQUIRED): The catalog also lists a "Recommended courses" block — MATH 5A, MATH 5B, PHYS 2A, PHYS 2B — which are recommendations, not degree requirements; not included as a requirement group.

GENERAL EDUCATION: The CS program page does not enumerate GE; as a local A.S. it follows Madera's local Associate Degree (AA/AS) GE pattern, with CSU GE Breadth or Cal-GETC used for transfer. Captured as a high-level ge_area group (no course enumeration) per instructions.

CONFIDENCE: Major requirement courses/units transcribed verbatim from the current catalog and cross-checked against the page image (high confidence). Minor uncertainty only on the degree total (60 inferred as CA minimum vs the 20-unit major total printed).
